Inseam is 29 NOT 30.5 I gave 4 stars instead of 5 because the inseam is NOT 30.5, it is 29 even. Originally, I measured myself according to the chart the company provided and ordered a large, but had to return then for a medium because these pants have incredible stretch to them! I'm 5'6" fluctuating from 133 -138lbs. I carry most of my weight in my mid section. As you can see from the pics, they are a bit short in length on me. I have long legs and an inseam of 30 inches would fit me perfectly.

Comfy and functional work pants! These pants are super comfortable, have 5 full pockets, belt loops, and come in fun colors! I typically wear a 12 size pant and ordered these in a L. They are a perfect skinny fit but and have a little give in the lower leg-ankle area so as to not look like a legging. Love these and will definitely order more. I’m 5’5” and 165 lbs for reference.

Very stretchy I bought a large. I am 5’2” and 134 pounds. My measurements are: Waist 28 1/2 inches Belly 33 inches Hips 36 1/2 inches So far they fit surprisingly well. They are very comfortable. I did notice some thread was loose in on the inseam of one of the legs, but other than that I haven’t noticed any problems. I plan on wearing these a little longer before deciding to purchase more.

Great pants! These are really cute, not see through (as most white pants are) and stretchy comfy pants. I am 5'2 and a 170. I ordered a size XL -- I could have ordered a L as the fabric is pretty stretchy. The bunching you see at the knees in the picture is because I was walking around and they rose up some.I will be ordering the other colors shortly. Thanks for a great product! Oh also wearing these in HOT Florida and they are still comfortable.

Stretchy, Colorful, Versatile Pants I’m 5’7” tall, 135 lbs with a long torso. The SMALL fit me well. I wore these pants many times this winter and early spring. They looked cute with high top sneakers, ballet flats, booties, you name it. I wore them for daytime birthday parties, running errands, and evenings out with kids in tow. The color and shape withstood multiple cold, gentle wash cycles. I highly recommend these pants if you’re looking to add color to your daytime casual gear.

Super comfortable. Size up. Bought these to wear tucked under boots. Really comfortable and fits well. I am 5'7" and 150 pounds and usually wear medium in most clothes, but ordered both a medium and large. The medium was way too tight, but the large fit great!

Fits Great But See Through I weight 190lbs and I’m about 5’7. I ordered an XL. These have great stretch I love the way they fit. The only reason I didnt give a full 5 stars was becuase I could never wear these out. They are very see through. I know they are white but Ive had a pair of solid white pants before that did not see through.

LONG! Love how these fit on me, but they’re super long. I literally stapled the hem inside, at least 4”. I’m 5’2”, but a high waist, short torso. I’ll need to get these hemmed. I was in a pinch and had to wear them same-day.

LONG! Love how these fit on me, but they’re super long. I literally stapled the hem inside, at least 4”. I’m 5’2”, but a high waist, short torso. I’ll need to get these hemmed. I was in a pinch and had to wear them same-day.

I love these! The fit on these pants is much better than I expected... they don't cling right to my hips and butt like a lot of pants like this do; they're just a tiny, tiny bit looser. That being said, you still need to order the same size you normally would. I'm 5'8" and 155, and wear a size 12. Pardon the belly in the pic...🤷‍♀️ The fabric of these pants is very nice... not too thick or thin so they can be worn year-round. Excellent quality. They came out of the dryer beautifully. The only tiny little complaint I have for these is that while the waistband appears to be about 3 inches wide, the elastic band inside is only about 1/2 inch wide, and I can already tell I'll have an issue with it rolling and twisting as time goes on. It would be nice if it was about 2 inches wide. I think it would feel more secure around the waist also, although it's fine now.

5' 8" and 150 lbs, got the medium. I have been looking for nice work pants that didnt break the banks. I found these and they had good reviews and weren't expensive so I decided to give them a try. I ordered the tan and white (with pockets). The white pants were cut more like straight leg pants than skinny (which isn't a huge deal) and were bit smaller in the waist. I actually like the fit of the white pants better BUT they are see through. Definitely need to wear a long tunic or sweater with the white pants. The tan weren't see through at all but they were a bit shorter than the white pants. I couldnt get a good picture with the tan pants on but I attached a picture of them side by side and you can see the difference. They fit more like ankle pants. Both fit well around the waist but were a little hard to get over my thighs and butt. Overall I just didnt love them, but they didnt fit bad enough for me to return them. I'm 5' 8" and 150 lbs and I got the medium in both pairs.

Great pants I originally bought the size 16 regular and they were so long. I’m 5’3 so I returned for the 16 short and they are a perfect fit. Super comfortable and perfect for the office. Has pockets and very flattering on my plus size body. Great value for the money

Nice quality dress pants These are very well made from a heavier fabric with a lot of stretch. Heavy elastic in the waist band to help keep the shape without looking like a stretchy band. I love that they have a short length choice. I am very pleased with my new favorite pants. I am 5’1” and 135 lbs. ordered Size 8 short for a loose comfortable fit.

Good Fit in Waist, Made for a Tall Person The gray color of the pants is very nice. The weight is surprising - the material is heavier than I expected. The waist fit is accurate to my size but the length is made for a tall woman (5'8" and above). At 5'7", I can only wear these slacks with very high heels.These pants are not designed to flatter one's figure. They lay straight down without any contour or styling. The sewing craftmanship is good. Overall I would give the quality an 8 out of 10 and the fashion design a 6 out of 10.

High quaity material and craftsmanship but VERY long! These were just a bit wrinkled when they came from the packaging, but right away, I felt they were really high-quality fabric. They are thicker pants with sturdy stitching, and they ironed out very easily with a quick steam/poly setting on my iron. After that, they did not wrinkle even when I grabbed them and balled them up, so that's great news. They will travel well, and when I arrive at my meetings, I won't be a wrinkly mess. The main issue is they are VERY long. I am 5'7", and these have at least two extra inches that will need to be hemmed up. I would say though, the quality of these pants is so good that I am willing to hem them rather than return them. As far as the other sizing - I wear a 16 and they were perfect. They don't ride up in the back and they are nice and flat in the front. Once hemmed, these will be my go-to pants for meetings and travel.

Extremely Comfortable & Pretty Pants I was really pleasantly surprised when I received these pants and put them on. They are very flattering and they are extremely well made. The sewing is excellent and the way they’ve put the pants together is quality. I am 5’2” and I ordered size 10 short and they are the perfect length for me. I usually wear a size 8 in stretchy pants, but I wanted to be sure that I’m going to be comfortable and that was a good decision. They fit perfectly and the cut is very good. The black color is very nice and attractive. I’m planning to purchase additional colors of these same pants. Zipper quality is smooth and well sewn too. I even received extra buttons with the pants in case one is lost. They are machine washable and they recommend hang dry which I usually do with my pants. It’s also excellent that the manufacturer offers 20 different options of sizes and length of these pants; that is impressive. I also love that these pants have pockets. A great purchase and definitely a recommend from me for anyone looking for quality good looking pants at a reasonable price.

I already ordered more. Feels like sweats, looks like work! Size reference: I am 5’6” and 124-128 lbs. I normally wear a size 4 in jeans and dress pants. After reading other reviews, I ordered the 4 SHORT in gray from iChosy. The fit is perfect with the legs being exactly right for flats. I LOVE these pants. They are soft and super stretchy, like sweat pants disguised as dress pants. The fit is flattering to my waistline and has enough give that I am comfortable when seated at my desk. The fabric does not wrinkle during normal well making these pants a contender for meeting days and travel. The fly is button and zipper. The cotton blend material is thicker than other slacks fabric so I’ve also been warmer at work. I am writing this review as I wear these pants in my office for the first time. I have received compliments from everyone I’ve seen and I bragged to my other half how beyond comfortable they are. In fact, I already ordered the black pair.

So nice! These pants are awesome. Great quality and the fabric doesnt wrinkle. The waist is very stretchy however the waist band is smooth and not scrunchy in the back like a lot of these pants are these days. The back has no pockets amd these are perfect for work or dressing up. For reference I am 5'5 116lbs and ordered a small. They fit long on me so I have to wear with at least 2 inch heels which is fine. Very comfortable and flattering fit.

High waisted perfection These black slacks exceeded every expectation I had. I never have any luck with these types of pants on here because of the material always being too tight. These pants are absolute perfection. I am wearing a size 4 and I am 5’3, 135 lbs and my lower measurements are 27-36. The material has a nice elasticity to it so it stretched nicely around my waist/hips. I love that it is high waisted and that they don’t hug my butt and thighs. I love the loose hang of them. They are very flattering to wear and look great with a blazer. I would recommend these pants.

Great work pant I’m really happy with these pants. A lot of the work pants out there these days seem to be wide-leg and I’m glad to find nice ones that are straight. I’m 5’9” and around 150 lbs and usually wear a size 6, but I will go up to an 8 in some pants and that’s what I ordered in these. I’m glad I did because the waistband would have been too tight for me comfortably. I’m really happy that these have great length and are long enough. The fabric feels nice, maybe a little stiff but nothing that bothers me at all. Overall these are really great pants and I recommend.

Comfortable -Slim- Fit I really like the look and style of these pants! Great for business casual but can be dressed up too. I am more of a casual gal, so I appreciate the stretchy material and comfort - even though the fit was pretty slim on me. I typically buy petite trousers (Loft or Ann Taylor 2-4P). These pants were perfect in the rise for me - very unusual for off the rack pants! - and could maybe use a bit of hemming, but I'm lazy so I'm perfectly happy with them as is. For reference, I am 5'3" -125# - 36B with an athletic/boyish build.

Nice quality pants, but inseam shorter than described I really like these pants and wanted to keep them, but the inseam is only 30.25 inches when the description and the tag say they're supposed to be 32 inches long. I purchased the size 6 in black. They fit true to size, and are a nice mid weight- substantial enough to be smoothing and not look cheap and flimsy. I'm tall with long legs, and really need the 32 inch inseam length. I thought maybe I just got a bad batch, so returned them and reordered. Unfortunately, they were the same 30.25 inch length. I contacted the seller and they said to return them and try again in a month or so to reorder. I will do that, and certainly hope that they corrected their quality control issues, and produce the pants in the stated length. It would end my endless search for a comfortable pair of nice looking black slacks.

In love! Perfect perfect perfect Every gal should have a pair of these, they’re equivalent to the little black dress. I’m 5’2, 100lb and I ordered a size 2. These are snug in the thigh/hip with a little left over in the waist band. The 29” length is perfect for me and I’m wearing an 1 1/2” boot heel in the pic. These have faux back pockets, front pockets large enough for a phone, and belt loops. Material is 53% cotton, 39 nylon, 8 spandex. These are beautiful pants that can be dressed up or down! Professional women look no further bc these are well made, nice weight, classy and elegant with the straight leg opening that accommodated boots perfectly. Finding dress pants that fit me is a nightmare and now I no longer have to fret when an occasion pops up; funeral, interview, nice restaurant, etc. Washing instructions: at or below 60C, steam iron, drip dry

WOW! Super quality for the price. I'm blown away. Very stretchy (necessary for my menopause bod). I'm 5'6", 150# and the 10 regular is perfect. Fit nicely around the thighs and knees, just the right amount of flare at the bottom. I bought the white, which is white-white, not off-white. The quality is much better in person than in Amazon's photo.

Great material but waist band runs small and doesn’t have much stretch I bought these in a size 6. Size 6 women’s at stores like JCPenny normally are my ideal size. The waist on these is absolutely Too Snug and tight , it is very uncomfortable. Normally I’m either a size S or M depending on the brand. These pants are 14 inches across the band when I measure them with tape (per see the picture attached), which seems like it could fit especially with these being advertised as stretch, I don’t think the waist is stretchy at all for this type of pants, I would probably have to size up to an 8 but I’m not sure I will be as these I am going to return due to the band being non stretchy imo. For reference right now I’m weighing around 130 lbs , my height is 5’1.5 inches my waist measures approximately 28-29 inches and my hips are 40.5 inches, athletic build.The material is smooth and pretty thick and feel comfortable on the skin as the fabric is 53% cotton 39% nylon 8% spandex , so material feels professional and soft. Front pockets are real and back pockets are fake which I think looks good because it doesn’t look bulky on the backside but smooth and sleek on the bum. Overall these pants could be great , and the inseam options are very good for different heights, but with 8% spandex I would expect way more stretch and these don’t have that. I hope this helps someone as I have a hard time buying clothing. On my experience stretch pants usually can accommodate 8-10 lbs loss or gain weight and these just aren’t forgiving and won’t stretch to your size with weight fluctuations.

11/10 I need every color and more colors added! I’m 5”7 160lbs and I bought these in a size 6 and I’m obsessssedddd with the fit. I have bigger legs and a smaller waist and it fits me like a glove. I’m a PT, and I need to squat, bend and move a lot so the stretch is so important to me and these are perfect. They’re also thick material and so so comfy. 11/10 highly recommend.
One size down fits perfect 5’4.5 137 lbs normally a size 8. Ordered the 6 and they fit perfect. Hopefully there will be more colors in the future. Very comfortable!
Super comfortable! Love these pants for the office. They’re a little pricey but they’re perfect. Comfortable, thick, stretchy. I ordered the 12 short. I’m 5’4”. Might order them in black.

Seriously good pants! I am ALWAYS afraid to order pants that I can't try on. I went by the size chart and ordered a 12 (For reference I'm 5'8 and my measurements are 41-31-42). Typically I wear an 8 or 10, but I wanted to be safe. I definitely could have ordered a size down. BUT I still seriously LOVE these pants. The material is thick, but sooooo stretchy! And they are truly high waisted, which I love. (No having to pull them up when I sit down so my kangaroo pouch isn't squeezed in half) There is a little bit of "puddling" at the ankle, but I'll usually be wearing boots with these so I don't care. And there's pockets! Good ones that I can actually put my hands all the way in! (The back pockets are not real pockets, but who cares when you have real front ones!) And although I haven't tested them to see if they are 100000% opaque, I'm pretty sure they're going to pass a lot better than some others I've tried. 1000/10 recommend.

Material that moves with you I ordered these black stretchy work pants in 14short since I'm 5'4" so I tend to need pants on the shorter side. Even though I ordered the short version, I still find I have to roll the cuff of the pants under once to give me a clean look, otherwise the pants look baggy on me- I included a photo with one pant leg cuffed and one pant leg as is. My hips measure 45" with a 34" waist, so according to the size chart I was taking a chance on my normal size 14, but aside from being a bit too long, these pants fit me like a glove. Love that the pants have an actual zipper and strong hold button closure, plus real belt loops. The butt has two fake pockets and the front has two real pockets but they're small. I appreciate how these pants are actually high waisted. The material is stretchy, comfortable, and moves with my body.

My hips measure 45" with a 34" waist, so according to the size chart I was taking a chance on my normal size 14, but aside from being a bit too long, these pants fit me like a glove. Love that the pants have an actual zipper and strong hold button closure, plus real belt loops. The butt has two fake pockets and the front has two real pockets but they're small. I appreciate how these pants are actually high waisted. The material is stretchy, comfortable, and moves with my body.

iChosy Women's Straight Leg Dress Work Ankle Pants Business Office Casual iChosey Women's Straight Leg Dress/Work Pant I chose was Navy Blue in size 6. The material content is 53% Cotton, 39% Nylon and 8% Spandex. It is supposed to be an ankle pant but being I am 5' 4" and 122 lbs the pants come longer on me. I would say that this is a pair of pants just right for work and for women that need a pair of pants that "hold" back some extra weight and/or bulges. Functional pockets are on each side of the hip. I personally like a pant whether for work or dress to have a natural flow to it and there is no flow that graces ones shape with this pair of slacks. Thank you.

Overall great, just a few minor complaints These came out great, they feel heavy duty and the definitely do the job, they are pretty hot to be in during hot weather but that's to be expected from pants like these, I took these out for a 2 day hike and they held up great, I felt like I could abuse these as much as I liked and they would still hold up. There are a few specific aspects of the pants that I will go over: 1. The knee pads: Trying to insert the knee pads will cause you to re-evaluate your entire life but once you understand the technique it's not a big deal, other than that they function great and really protect your knee when kneeling, only complaint is that they are very hard and inflexible so they can get uncomfortable when sitting. 2. The size: I measured my waist before ordering and ordered a 34" waist size, but when I tried the pants on, they were too loose on my waist even after tightening the waist adjustment mechanism, so I was forced to use a belt (which I was planning on using anyway so it wasn't that bad). 3. Overall comfort when walking: While walking in these pants after a while I noticed that the extra reinforcement material on the crotch area in the back of the pants would fold into itself and would bother me so I had to readjust it every once in a while, also the material of the pants is not very stretchy but I didn't find it bothering.

Amazing fit and nice quality I was skeptical buying pants for work online without trying them on and read tons of reviews first before buying. While I was looking for a lower rise pant and not one that goes to my belly button, these are amazing in every other way. They fit so nicely, keep the crease well in the leg, and look professional. They are great to wear more casually with a blouse tucked in but also would pair nicely with a suit jacket for more formal days when I don’t want to wear a skirt. I am definitely going to check out the other colors. I love the chunky waistband, it gives some personality without being over the top as I don’t ever wear belts. For reference I am 5’ 3” and they do drag the ground if I’m not in heels so I imagine with wearing flats most days the bottoms will get tattered if I don’t hem them up a bit. I am not sure how they will hold up after multiple washings but I am hopeful! They did not stretch throughout the day and start falling off like most other pants seem to do. Overall very happy and impressed for the price.

Great pants! Details to share I'm sharing because I find detailed reviews extremely if they contain details beyond "I love this." I hope this is to someone else shopping. _ I'm 5'7", weigh 150 lbs (sometimes 158 lbs.). My shape is hourglass, meaning I have hips (40") and a smaller waist (33") I am over 50, so I have done some shopping in my life. My US size is 10-12 at Macy's and 12 at Target. _I like clothes that do not bind, loose fit, but do not look like I am wearing an old canvas bag. _I like good fabric, and I am a longtime sewer, so craftsmanship of clothes is important to me. I look at the construction of a garment. So, these pants: _Linen fabric is quality and a mid-weight, no shine and has a great but subtle linen texture - the fabric is soft to the skin and does not scratch or itch. Good for three seasons in the north of the USA. The drape is excellent right out of the bag without ironing or putting them in the dryer for a touch-up. The elastic at the waist is about 1-1/4 inch wide and not stiff - the elastic waist could easily accommodate a waist 8 inches bigger than mine or more - it depends on the fit you like - I actually might make the elastic a little tighter, an easy 10 minute fix if you sew or know someone who does sew. The pants have two good sized pockets. The construction is excellent. The fabric is cut correctly so it drapes correctly. _Depending on your lifestyle and personal style these pants can be worn almost anywhere besides a formal event. How you style with a top, shoes and accessories is the key. Definitely outside pants, not sleeping pants. _When shopping online make sure you get a tape measure and know your measurements. I ordered US L and the fit was as expected for me - I could have sized down one size to a US Medium, but I like a loose fit. Also, make sure you look at the manufacturer's size chart, not the generic Amazon size chart to make your size selection. _My photos are of the pants right out of the bag, and in front of a dusty (sorry) mirror, but hopefully they are to you. _I have been wearing them for a few hours now and these pants are so comfortable, so cute. If this is your style, your silhouette, I can completely recommend these pants. IXIMO Women's Casual Cotton Linen Baggy Pants with Elastic Waist

I need to take in the waist line by just a tiiiiny bit for them to be perfect, but I also have a pretty defined hourglass figure (tiny waist, larger hips/butt), which is why I love wearing pants like these. I've spent years shopping worldwide for pants JUST like these, and these ones are among the most comfortable, durable, thick, and flattering that I've purchased. I have two pairs of the khaki and two of the black, and I'd like to buy more since I wear them daily, but... That's where my 4 stars instead of 5 come in. I've ordered from this company a few times, and the quality and color of the material is hit or miss and tends to change. My first pairs of khaki and black were thick, good-quality material, good and consistent color, but when I reordered another pair of khaki - this time in both medium and small to see if the small fit any differently - the fabric of the small was noticeably different. It was thinner and more yellow-toned than the medium pair I loved so much. I returned them. The mediums were fine; they were made of the same soft, thick linen my original pair had been made of, same soft khaki color, etc. A month later, I tried ordering another two. a medium pair of black and a medium pair of khaki, assuming the quality problem was perhaps linked to the small size only, but the mediums that just arrived are the color and quality I had returned before! In my photo, the pair on the left are the new pair that I'm returning. It's difficult to tell in a photo since you have to physically touch them to truly feel the difference, but it's absolutely noticeable in-person, so much so that I can't stand the feeling of them against my legs. The black was also the poorer-quality fabric. I decided to eat the cost and try washing them immediately to see if that helped, but even washed and air-dried, they're noticeably different quality from my first black pair I ordered two months ago. I'm disheartened - I want to buy so many of these so I never have to worry about ruining a pair, but I don't want to keep ordering and returning in hopes that the pair that arrives will be the quality and color I love so much. There's definitely a consistency problem, so be warned of that. If you end up receiving the better quality pants, you'll love them. I know I do.

my favorite pants absolutely adore these pants. 5’6 200lbs pear shaped wearing the XL. i have them in grey, green, and black. be mindful of what style you are getting to ensure it is the fit you want, all of mine are the original style. the three i have have are pretty damn consistent in fit and length but the waistband is just slightly looser on the grey than it is on the other two, after a few wears they all begin to feel the same. the material is not see through, it is a very sturdy and borderline heavy fabric but still light enough for me to feel comfortable wearing them throughout the summer in 100+ degree weather. they are so comfortable and flattering, but i think your height does matter for them to fit you correctly. they ride right on my belly button for my ideal length, i think people who are taller or have longer legs than me would need to wear them in a low rise fashion to prevent a high waters look. shorter people can definitely just opt for a higher waist or hem them to make it work. i wash them on delicate and hang them up to dry, i do not want to risk destroying them in the drier. i’m super happy with these pants and wear them most days. i’ve only had them during summer months but you could definitely wear them with leggings underneath for added warmth in the winter.

Pay attention to the style when you pick the color These pants are terrific. The color is not bright white (which is what I was hoping for) but the white is "whiter" than cream. The waistband is wide and comfortable. I am 5'6" with a short torso and I ordered a medium. I'm usually a 4/6. The length is perfect for me and I really like the elegant balloony bottoms. The fabric is medium-weight and totally fine modesty-wise. There is no fabric content tag, but I suspect the pants are more cotton than linen. There is a linen-like texture to the fabric, but the feel of the fabric leans towards cotton. As other reviewers have noted, the pockets are low, but I like their low placement. When you order, pay attention to the style names - some pants are available in different styles so be sure to look at the larger photos to make sure you're ordering the correct silhouette. I see these pants getting a lot of summertime wear in my future - I wish they came in olive green!

The MOST Comfy Pants! I live in these pants. The only time I'm not wearing them is when they are being washed, summertime, and when loungewear is inappropriate. They are so fun too. My BF calls them my "balloon pants." I've worn them around the house and when traveling. And I get SO many compliments on them when I wear them out. They can actually be dressed up a bit if you can believe it. AND POCKETS!!! Get yourself a pair of birks and tie a v-neck t-shirt in a knot + messy bun = DUNZO. The pants are thick and seem like great quality for the price. The biggest downside is waiting for them to hang dry since it's not recommended you put them in the dryer. Though, I imagine if you did put them in the dryer they would shrink up a ton (cotton/linen does that), so don't do that. I got the gray color. It's has a tinge of blue to it, overall I'd call it a dusty blue color. I'm 5'2" 140 lbs and they drag a bit on me but not much. I could see these being pretty short on someone that is taller (over 5'5'). Get them, you'll love em!

Comfy and versatile, perfect for apple body type, great alternative to jeans for travel These pants have become an unexpected staple in my wardrobe. I’m 5’7” with a 31-32” waist (apple shape) and wear and 8-10 in pants. I have several pair of these pants in both small and medium. I’d recommend going with the smaller if you’re in between sizes. The attached pics are my size M; the S are less baggy on my bum. I travel a lot and these are my default pants for long flights. They are a bit bulky to pack when not being worn, but are so versatile that I find they are worth the space. Great petite length wide leg pant I am 5'2" and around 150lb, typically between a M/L depending on fit; these I got a Large and an very happy. These pants have a button fly, which can sometimes be tricky to find the proper waist fit, but the back of these pants have an elastic waist that is perfect for a 'custom' and comfortable fit! The length of the pants is about 28" inseam, which hits me right at pant length but will be more of a culotte fit for someone taller. The waist has belt loops, as well as a removable 2-strand tie belt accessory. The fabric itself is a little rough, which caused me to remove one star; it's light and wrinkles just like linen, but is not quite as smooth in texture as I'd have hoped. Overall, these are decent quality for the price point, and very wearable summery/warm weather pants. I'm pleased with them and would recommend these pants, especially to petites looking for an inseam that won't drag on the ground.

Love love love, soft and flattering, very good quality These pants are exactly what I was hoping they would be, but better. The photo you see is after a wash and tumble in the warm dryer (minimal shrinkage). And no wrinkles. I ordered these size S pants - a linen and viscose (rayon) mix, for a 5'3", 110 lb petite young woman with a 24" waist and curvy hips. Although the actual waist, even washed and dried, is a bit larger at appx 26", the pants can be tightened for a smaller waist with the attached belt or another belt. Also, I noticed that there is room to move the button over about 3/4" to make the waistline a bit smaller. For those who have larger waistlines, there is stretch of about 3", maybe a tad more. These are a higher waist pant, meaning, belly button area, and there is adequate fabric in the behind so that it isn't tight or prone to give a wedgie. In fact, the way the pants are cut in the back, and the fabric, makes for a nice drape that resembles the model in the photo. The pants are well-cut and have good stitching, with no loose threads or shoddy workmanship. They are similar to what you might find at one of the large anchor department stores in the mall. The fabric is neither too thin (not see-through) nor too thick (denim). It's perfect for spring and fall, and summer days that aren't over the mid-80s. I would say they are the thickness of a soft chambray shirt, thicker than a man's dress shirt, but not stiff at all. I am so impressed with this fabric composition that I am going to continue to look for garments with this mix: 55% Linen / 45% Viscose. The price point is an excellent value and I would have no hesitation whatsoever about buying again.

Nice Weight! Comfortable to Wear! Wish They Were 2 Inches Longer! Current price $26.99. Size Medium - Green. True to size - Waist measures 28 inches, hips 40 inches, and inseam is 27 inches. The color is an army green, just like it shows in the pictures. I'm 5'6" and 130 lbs. The medium fits perfectly. The back is elastic, and the front has a zipper and button. The pants have two side pockets. The belt is really just two thin strips that you can close. The belt is removable, so if this is not your style, just don't use it! I LOVE the pants. The only thing, and this is consistent with most pants I order - the inseams are just too short. I would love a couple of extra inches in length. 4 Stars. Hope you find this review and pictures . I love the front pleats. These are great pants for work or causal! The fabric is a nice linen viscose blend that looks and feels like linen!

Full Length Pants Unless You're Really Tall I'm 5'2", 140 pounds. I ordered my usual size, Medium. They fit fine around the waist because of the stretchy elastic, but they are not capris on me. The legs come all the way down to the top of my foot. The color is definitely not maroon. It's terra cotta. They come out of the package with a faded, pre-washed look. Some people will like that, but it's not my cup of tea. To me they just look old and over-washed. They're also way too wide in the hips and legs. I understand that they're meant to be "wide-legged," and the width might be ok if you're over 5'8" and they're really capris on you. But on me there's way too much fabric. They end up looking baggy and ill-fitting, make me look dumpy. I'm going to have to take about 2" off the outside of the legs and maybe 6-8" off the hem. I washed and line-dried them before trying on, and they came out very wrinkled, which is a bummer because I'm not about to haul out the ironing board. Next time I'll try the dryer on low and see if that works better. On the plus side, they do appear to be well made and are a nice, light weight for summer wear. Final note: I am extremely sensitive to tags in clothes. I always have to remove them. This pair of pants, like so many others, has the tags sewn directly into the seam, so when I removed them it left a hole behind and a sewing chore for me to do. Annoying to have to mend a brand new garment!

Front band strange These pants are pretty cute for summer and fit fairly well except the front band area is a bit strange. The back and sides of the waistband and regular and straight, but in the front the band kind of curves down more and thickens. I’m not really sure what the point of that is but I feel it’s unflattering and almost just looks like a sewing mistake. I’ll still probably wear them a fair amount this spring/summer. I’m 5’5 and they do hit above the ankle as advertised but that is that awkward length where these pants would probably only look good with flip flops and look strange/cut off with tennis shoes etc.

Quality pants These women’s wide leg capri pants were delivered swiftly in simple packaging. They have been made of a good quality cotton blend material with no loose threads, neat stitching and hemmed beautifully. The material feels heavier than expected and is not at all see through in the denim blue color I ordered. Using the size chart provided, I ordered a size medium and they fit true to size. I’m usually a small-med, 5’6 130lbs for reference. The elastic waist is very stretchy and forgiving, thus allowing you to size up or down depending how you prefer your pants to fit. They sit right above my feet, hitting my ankles so too short for winter and boots but will be nice in warmer weather. If you’re petite they will probably fit like regular length pants. They have pockets! Not big ones but pockets nonetheless! The color is more true in the first and last photo. I’m happy with these pants and look forward to wearing them when it gets warmer. I highly recommend this seller and these pants. I will probably order more pairs in the other colors available.
Good cargos! Love these cargos! They fit amazingly, although a bit long for my 5 foot frame. They are light enough to wear late summer, fall, or spring.

TO ALL MY TALLER GIRLS Me: 5’10ish 140 lb woman. I’m typically a small-4/6 34 inseam 27 pre pregnancy 33 postpartum I bought a small- before babies Bought medium- postpartum To all my tall girls that feel out of place… MY TALL GIRLS, THESE PANTS COULD BE FOR YOU! These pants can definitely be dressed up or dressed down for a more chic look. They are thin and perfect for spring/summer weather. The material isn’t high end material but it isn’t trash either. The stitching is nice through out and clean. Normal trousers sit lower low but these are true high waist pants. The bottom is cuffed up and its sewed to keep in place(see picture) If you carry your weight in your midsection I can see these highlighting that area for you. Consider wearing shape wear to smooth everything out. I noticed it was less forgiving on my postpartum lower belly but pre-pregnancy I did not have that problem. The inseam seems to be approx 32 but because it’s cuffed and only sewed on 2 opposing sides- it slightly drapes adding a some additional length. If you’re short, these will be way too long for you. Pros: THEY FIT TALLER WOMEN!!!!!! I put these on with a low block heel and also platform sneakers. Classy/chic look can be achieve getting the most usage out of styling these for different occasions. Iron/steam easily Cons: They aren’t wrinkle free and hold a bit of wrinkles if you stay in a position for way too long. It doesn’t wrinkle nearly as bad at linen. 👍

Hardly any stretch, best for standing Photo size reference: I am 5’6” and 130 lbs. I normally wear a size 4/27 but decided to order the 28 in the trouser jeans. For my desk job, I was glad for the extra inch as there is very little stretch in the fabric. However, the denim did lose some shape after sitting a few hours and I did not love the “fupa” effect of the loosened lower abdomen area. Super high-waisted, with hip and back pockets. My silhouette was fly when I first put the jeans on but by the end of the day I had some lumps and bumps from all angles. I absolutely counted down the minutes until I could change though because the jeans are not comfortable for cross-leg sitters. The stiff fabric dug into all my crevices and made a few more. My opinion is that Joes Jeans is a sought after brand of high-quality denim…but this particular pair is best worn standing for this wide hipped female.

Wardrobe Game Changer I wasn’t sure about the high rise at first, but these Joe's Chloe Utility Wide Leg Pants quickly became a favorite. The deep chocolate brown color is rich, and the comfort stretch twill fabric is both soft and stretchy while holding its shape beautifully. The utility pocket detailing adds a cool, urban edge, making them versatile enough for both work and casual outings. At 5'8", the length is perfect, allowing me to wear both slight heels and flats comfortably. True to size and high quality, these pants are a fantastic addition to any wardrobe.

Comfortable, soft, and cool, quality is alright This is my favorite style of rayon pants: elasticated one-size-fits-most waist, and wide in the legs. When I first opened the package my initial thought was: the threads are too coarse and the weave is too wide. However, after washing them and running them through the dryer on a low heat they became the more familiar soft, flowing rayon that I'm more familiar with. The fit works well for me. I have a 35" waist and 46" hips. They are wide in the legs, which I love. These are a great length for me; I like pants grazing above the ankle. They have pockets - handy. About the quality: I would say it's just okay. As you can see in the photos, the ends of the drawstring are unfinished and ragged. Also, there is an obvious gap where the white elastic shows through the opening in the waistband. I've already removed the drawstring and whip stitched the opening closed so no white is seen. [I don't see the reason to have a drawstring unless you have an 18" waist and you have to tighten the pants up to stop them from falling off your body]. There are loose threads here and there, and the workmanship is just average. Bottom line: would I buy them again? Possibly. I'm not sure these pants are a good value, however, based on the quality. I don't imagine they are going to last for more than a season. Knowing that, and not having high expectations, but considering they are a style and fabric that works for me during hot weather, and they are conveniently available on Amazon, I'm giving them 4 *.

The pockets are deep and hold my Pixel 8 Pro without any issues, and the drawstring keeps the pats at my waist. The pattern is bright and beautiful, and other than some wrinkles that will come out after washing (which I don't actually care about) these are ready to go. Size up! For reference I’m a size 6 or 28/29 in jeans, 5’3 and 145 lbs. I used the size chart and got these in a size 10 color sky blue and they fit perfectly to me, however they are very stretchy so I think if I got a size 8 it probably would have just fit tighter. I don’t like super tight clothes so the 10s are perfect. I also like that they aren’t too long on me like a lot of pants are. They go all the way down to the floor but with shoes they’re fine. Also they are high waisted The material is very stretchy and soft. They definitely don’t feel like those high quality rigid denim jeans, and you can kind of tell by looking at them, but they are comfier. They’re a bit thin and would probs be cold in the winter time but good for fall or spring. I like them, I think they’re worth the price and they fit well. Not high quality though but the price isn’t either

Glad I followed their sizing chart I normally wear a size 4 or 6 in jeans but ordered these in size 10 due to their chart and I'm glad I did because these fit. These jeans are very soft and extremely stretchy--not a lot of structure if that makes sense. The tag says 1% spandex but I believe there's a lot more spandex than what their tag says. In addition to a regular set of belt loops these jeans have a narrower 2nd set of loops right beneath for the included decorative denim strap/belt. The denim strap/belt is easily removable (just looped through), which is good because you'll have to undo it each time you need to use the restroom. I don't have a very full behind and I find these jeans more flattering on me than most. At 5'4" these are a great length on me. I hope this information helps with your purchasing decision.

Flattering and stretchy material. I am typically a size 8/10 and I ordered the L. 160lb, 5’7”. These pants fall about ankle length and I really like the fringe on the bottom as well as the length. The mirror doesn’t do them complete justice. They also fit me very well in my hips and bottom being tighter there and loose down my thighs and legs. I really like the way they fit and they are extremely stretchy and comfortable to wear. I am able to actually pull out some of the material around my waist as you can see. They do stretch out a little as you wear them but nothing abnormal, and they don’t fall down as you wear them. I put these in my washer and dryer and they didn’t shrink or have any type of seams that fell apart after washing. They are a very good quality jean and I would 100% recommend to any middle size woman who has a little bit of a belly and loves leggings and doesn’t want to wear jeans. These jeans are still very comfortable.
Camp pants Cute camo pants. Zipper and button closure with tie. Loose fit. 130 lbs med fit perfectly. Love the paint splatter detail. Get it!

Great fitting camo wide leg jeans I'm 5'2" and 124 lbs, and got these in a small since a medium in these types of 'barrel leg' jeans in the past has been too big. The waist is a bit snug, but overall a good fit everywhere else. Hits right at the ankle so no hemming required. The fabric is high quality medium weight denim in a camo design. Two side deep pockets and 2 standard back pockets. The waist has a cotton drawstring waist band that you can choose to use or not. Fun and trendy for fall.

Cute Style But…. The style of the - Kedera Women Striped Pants Wide Leg Trousers Casual High Waisted Pants Summer Flare Jeans with Patched Pockets – is super cute but I there are a couple issues with these jeans that leave me feeling slightly disappointed. They fit my fuller figure however, at the side of the legs near the hips, they weirdly bulge out a little. This is not evident on the photos of the models in the listing. Perhaps, they are slightly too big? A size chart at the time of my purchase would have been . Also, the stripes at the back center seam do not line up correctly and this too looks weird in my opinion. The fabric is stiff as well. I’m hoping that with multiple washings, it will soften up and look better on. For sizing reference, my waist measures 38”, and my hips measure 48”. I am 5'4" tall. I purchased a size XX-Large.

Absolutely obsessed I LOVE these pants. I am 5’3 ish and about 145lbs and they fit perfectly. They have nice stretch but not too much, it holds its shape well throughout the day. I washed them inside out on a delicate cycle. All the pearls have stayed in tact through a few wears and washes!

SO BRIDAL I wore these pearl jeans for brunch on my bachelorette and they are so perfect! Definitely going to style different and wear again! I’m 5’8” and wish they were a little longer so I could wear with heels! I am around 160 and a large fit well. It was a little loose in the groin again but otherwise it was perfect!
LOVE These pants are an absolute FAVE! They were super comfortable. Every person I came in contact with complimented them. Size Medium 5’3 145lb.

The fabric is woven and the seams are finished nicely. They are a little long for 5'3" me buy that problem can be solved with taller shoes or my hemming them. Easy and not a big deal. I really like these pants and may get them in another color.

Accurate to product photos. More for looks than comfort on me. I am 5’6” inches tall, 123 pounds on an empty stomach, and a consistent US size 4 in pants and jeans. I ordered size 4 in olive from Khaki and Blue and they do fit true to size. The waist is low-rise. The material is stiff and even makes some noise when I walk. No stretch whatsoever so size up if you are between sizes or don’t like things digging into you while seated. After sitting for long stretches, the lack of stretch really starts to dig in around the seated section of my lower torso and upper thighs. There were some steam marks upon arrival on the back of the pants but they came out in the wash. All 4 pockets are deep but the pants are a little too tight for me to even be able to use them for much. I’m afraid the size 6 would be too loose in the waist but probably would’ve been less intrusive for my desk job. However, I do like the style and look of these pants with the flaring leg and hip hugging. In conclusion, the stiff/thick material with no stretch will keep this olive pair out my regular line-up but the design and style will draw me, when I’m feeling peppy and can stand up a good portion of the day. Definitely a high quality pair of pants!

No stretch, size up?… These are definitely work pants, not fashion pants. They are a thicker than average material so they should last a long time. Straight leg. They are fairly soft. They don’t have any stretch to them, so beware of sizing. I would recommend sizing up a full size. Mine didn’t fit and that’s before I even washed them. I am 5’4”. They are a little long on me, but not too bad - with shoes on, they won’t drag on the floor. They are well-made.

Drape Beautifully I liked these trousers even more than I thought I would. With white/cream, it is always a concern that they’ll be thin or see-through, which is not the case at all here. The fabric is silky and light, but not chintzy or cheap feeling. The draping is perfect for a lightly pleated front, Creating a clean line that doesn’t hug or accentuate my belly, which is also a concern for me, as I am not as flat in the stomach area. I ordered an XL, with the intention of having a looser fit, I normally wear a 12/14, for reference and am 5’3”. They were comfortable and loose, but not clown baggy. My shoes have a little lift, which is perfect for giving enough extra height so they didn’t drag on the floor, for my smaller height. They have a tailored look, without the tailored price. Love the pockets and extra elastic on back, which allows for more comfortable fit at the waist. I wore them to work and got a ton of compliments. I may need to buy in all colors now.

Office Pants I’m 5/6”, around 165 lb, and have short legs, so I got an XL. Those pants are pretty baggy. They fit okay, but as you can see in my photos, they are a bit long for me. They would be fine if I wore heels, but I no longer have any heels, so I need to send the pants for an alteration. Although the fabric is supposed to be breathable, it doesn’t feel like it’s breathable. Does it feel durable? Yes, but not so breathable. That said, because the pants are pretty baggy, they won’t stick to my legs, so it’s okay. The fabric is thinner, but I got them in black, so it doesn't see-through. The side pockets are good. I can put my big iPhone Pro Max in them, and I like that. I also like the elastic back. It definitely helps keep my pants in place. Those pants are not my favorite, especially since they don’t look very flattering on me. However, those are good as office clothes, so I wear them when I have to go into the office.

Flowy, comfortable, and trendy Size reference: I am 5'6" and 130 lbs. I ordered the medium and am very pleased with the fit. The size chart recommended a small but my measurements are 27/37 with wide hips and I did not want to risk the waist being too tight so I went up to the next size. Description: The wide waistband is elastic with a functional cream drawstring. The material looks like linen with little cream threads mixed in and is not see through. The fabric is better than linen, in my opinion, with a slight silky feel. I washed on regular and dried on delicate and there was not a wrinkle to be seen! Recommendation: For!!!! I love these pants. They are comfortable for travel, nice enough for work, and can be either dressed up with a tucked in shirt or dressed down with a crop top, tank, or tee. They do wrinkle a bit during wear, especially when seated, but still not as bad as true linen. I have no critiques and would absolutely order again.

Interesting Fabric These pants looked super cute on the model. I like the fabric, it is very interesting. Don’t know how to describe it, but it looks nice and feels nice on the skin. The fabric tag says it is Viscose-46%, Polyamide-51%, and Elastane- 3% . The elastic waist makes them easy to slip on and off. They have roomy side pockets. They are well sewn, with straight and true seams. I bought size large, because large seems to fit me best most of the time. But these pants were 3-4 inches too long. I’m 5’2”. If you have a matronly figure these pants are not the best choice. Doesn’t matter if you tuck your shirt/blouse in or wear it out over the waist, all the gathering and bow around the waist makes you look thicker than you already are. For someone young, slim and tall these pants would look very stylish. They may require a little ironing.

Comfortable, but Short [Height: 5'5| 155lbs | Bust 37 | Waist 27 | Hips 37] [Ordered: Medium] I can never tell how long a pair of pants will be on me based on the stock pictures, and often some reviews of those in a similar height [weight can be carried differently]. In this case, these pants are short. The inseam is 27" on these, and I prefer an inseam of 32" or longer (depending on the shoes). However, there is a hem that can be removed to increase the length of the pants. Overall, they are cute and comfortable to wear!

Great Fit and an Interesting, but Classic Color This tie waist, paper bag style design is a flattering high waist design. The brown color is quite an interesting version of the conservative classic. The tie at the waist is attached, so you won't need to worry about losing it. The length hits perfectly (I'm 5'2"), and the high waist hits at the natural waist. the fit is just loose enough to give the desired style, but not so loose that they look sloppy. The pleats give just the right amount of tailoring to the finished style, and the pockets are deep and usable. These are a great choice for a versatile work to running around town look.

A closet staple! Really loved this fabric because it was just a tad dressier than linen, but with the same weight. The cut of the pants with the ribbon waist is super classy, and POCKETS! The real test was washing and drying - washed on cold and dried on low, and they came out the same as they went in. I'm 5-7, 210 pounds, and these are not for you if you're long legged. They're borderline a little short when worn with real shoes...with flip flops in the summer they might be OK.

LOVE THESE PANTS!!! These pants are AWESOME! They are so comfortable and have pockets! I’m only 5’4 so they’re not cropped on me but I prefer full length. The tie is just for looks and doesn’t tighten the waist. I’m a size 16 and extra large. These pants fit me great! I have a big butt. These pants aren’t too tight and don’t show all my cellulite and dimples. They are really wrinkly out of the package. I will definitely wash on cold and hang to dry to make sure they don’t shrink

Great color and fabric I am 5’2” and these fit in length with tall heels. I can’t wear flats with these. Nice fabric and color. Good for work

Very dressy and classy, while still wide and comfortable. Very nice looking pants, very dressy and professional. I ordered the beige. The word says beige, but the picture looks more like a very light sand, almost white. They are as pictured. I was actually hoping for something a little more beige because I’ve never had much luck with virtually white pants. They all seem to stain so easy. The first time I wear them they come home with an ink spot, coffee stain, or something. These did not! I was very impressed. I wore them to the office on a regular workday. I took the train into the city. Wore the pants all day. When I came home, they looked great. A few wrinkles around the hip flexors, which is normal for an office job. I was eating a breakfast bar at my desk and dropped a few chocolate chips on the pants so I went into the bathroom and just wiped them off with a wet paper towel and the chocolate came right off. It’s great to be able to have such a light colored pair of pants that don’t stain easy. And they’re so easy to care for. They were a little creased out of the package, as you can see by some of the photos. I tried them on right away. I was wearing a black and purple knee brace and I just happened to have on black underwear. Could not see through them at all, which can be a problem with such light colored pants quite often, and they fit perfect. I touched them up with a steam iron and it came out fine. Care instructions. The tag inside the pants list just the care instructions symbols, and I didn’t know what they all meant, but there is a card attached to the pants that translates them. You might want to save the little card, that’s what I did. Care instructions for these pants translate to: Do not bleach, dry clean, Machine wash up to 86°F general cycle. iron up to 230°F tumble dry low. So you can dry clean them and they’re very nice pants. They’re worth it if you want to. But it also says machine wash up to 68°F gentle cycle, tumble dry low, iron up to 230°F. So I threw them in the washer by themselves on delicate, and then threw them in the dryer on low. And I am very impressed. They’re clean and they came out of the dryer virtually wrinkle free. So I’m not gonna bother to test iron them, they don’t need it. Very easy to care for. Sized as stated. I measured myself and bought the one according to the measurement chart, and it fit perfectly. May be too short for average to tall people. The one thing the measurement chart does not say is the inseam. This concerned me because I’m 5’4”” with a 28 inch inseam, and very long waisted. These pants fit me perfectly, I can wear them with flats. They come up to my waist. I measured the extra large inseam is 29 inches so it’s perfect for me. However, as my sister says, for anyone who actually has legs, lol, they may end up, looking like high water pants. But from my short legs, they’re perfect. I’m thrilled with the inseam.

Perfect fit, so comfortable! I'm so glad I found these pants! I'm also glad I packed them for my California trip. They were the perfect blend of business casual, comfortable, and summer vibes! The elastic waistband is a life-saver. The material is soft and light, and super easy to iron, no hassle. I am about 5'7, weigh 165lbs, and I ordered a size large. Perfect fit!

Nice slacks, a little large Nice slacks in a thin polyester fabric that resists wrinkles. The khaki is a nice professional color that goes well with just about any top. I ordered a large mostly based on the waist measurements on the size chart, but I think a medium would be better, maybe less pouffy on the front crotch area with all of the pleats. Short and curvy approved! Here are the details: The questions we all have were fully checked off. I’m 5’2, 194 pounds, and built like a box. Seriously, you could pack me in the same rectangle box these pants arrived in. I ordered the lighter beige (think French vanilla) and the darker khaki (not a light tan but a true khaki color). I was going for the quiet luxury or “old money” aesthetic that is timeless and put together. So, how do these pants fair against the luxurious box that is me? Pros: Tummy: I have one. Do these fit mom bellies? Yes. What about the pooch effect? A little. When I sit down there is a little pooching from the fabric but if I add a blazer or sweater then it’s hardly noticeable. There is zero, and I do mean zero, stretch in the waistband so if you’re between sizes I would go up a size. I am a new size 16, from an 18, and it took me a full minute to be okay with a fitted waist since most of my pants have elastic waists. Buttons and belts: The buttons (there are 2) were sewn on well. The zipper didn’t catch and after two flights of stairs to test out whether these pants could handle the demands of being part of my closet the zipper stayed put. Belt loops are wider so while a thin belt would still look cute I would go with a standard thickness in a belt to get that old money look. Length: I could walk barefoot in them as they’re wide legs and while maybe an inch too long they’ll do nicely with heels added to them. I walked outside with a pair of booties on and they did not drag the ground. The hem on the bottom of the pant legs is nicely constructed and gives a tailored look. Thighs: Did not make me look like a can of biscuits about to make an escape. They draped nicely over my luxury edition thighs and didn’t seem to hurt my feelings. Cons: There really aren’t any that would stop me from ordering more of these. They probably require hand washing, gentle detergent, or Woolite. (Photo attached for instructions). Can you see through them? I couldn’t. I could see the line but with shape wear that would solve the issue. Bumps and lumpy bits showing? A little but this is where shape wear comes in. All in all, these are high end quality with an everybody price tag. They’re pants with substance meaning they are true corduroy trousers with a little weight to them. They’ll likely do fine through autumn but I will add a second pair a size up so that I can wear footless tights under for an extra layer of warmth during winter. 10/10 do recommend!

Not the correct color Had high hopes for these since the reviews are good, but I am not impressed. Oh , they are super cute BUT the elastic was waaaay too tight (I ordered a large at 5’7” and 180 lbs.), and the color was off, to say the least. Instead of receiving a pretty medium grey, these were a weird off purple/puce/brownish grey underlayer that doesn’t match the top layer. Will be returning.

Scratchy Material For reference I am 5 ft tall and approximately 119 lb. I have a petite curvy build. The length of these pants were perfect for my height even if I wore a slight platform. However I will not be keeping this item because the material was scratchy and uncomfortable. The color I ordered was coffee but the color I received was orange. The fabric is not transparent and the shape was not very flattering for someone of my build. I will be returning this item.

Best linen pants ever! These pants are heavy enough to keep their shape but still cool in the heat. The color is great and they are comfortable. The pockets in front are the right size but the rear pockets are fake. I am 5’5” and 205 lbs., usually weal XL, but based on reviews got 2XL and they fit nicely.
100% linen I'm 5'6" 213lbs I normally wear 14/16 pants. these come above my ankles. Great look. Great feel I will definitely buy more

Awesome, fit my large body well Fantastic, I'm very happy with these. Got the large and fits well, For reference I am 40/36/39, 6’ tall and 195 pounds. I measured the unstretched inseam at 28", this is 4 inches longer than LinvMe. I easily put these on and pull up to my crotch. The are skin tight and see-thru. Not near as tight as other brands. The large is like XL in other brands. Thighs fit good on my 22 thigh. leg holes at hem were large enough to get over my size 11 men feet. Hem is 1/2-inch. I usually end up letting the hem out to gain that extra length and then add on another leg finish with either elastic band or stretch lace. easy to find 2 and 3 inch wide elastic bands. Stretch lace easy to find 6 inch wide. Waist is mid waist height, comes to just under my belly button. Rolls down to natural waistline. I roll over the waistband twice for the perfect front rise. Glad I took a chance on these, they are superb value.11-14-24 Bought again, still like, added photos. Even though the length seems short via measurement (I normally want at least a 30 inch inseam for my height) the length stretches during wear and is longer than the flat measurements. This pair I measured the inseam at 27" - fabric stretches so easily I think I stretched it a little to get the 28" measured before.

Love these! Definitely worth the buy! LOVE these pants! Not shiny or super fake looking at all. I would agree with other reviewers that say they run slightly small - I have a big butt & hips and small waist, so it’s always a toss up between a M and a L for me with pants, however, a lot of times the waist will be too big with a large and that was not the case here at all - they fit great and made my waist look snatched. They are a little on the shorter as some other reviewers noted, but the perfect length with heels even though I’m tall (5’8”). As for the smell - there was a bit of the typical faux leather smell, and based on other reviews that said they smelled horrible by the end of the night, I preemptively flipped them inside out, sprayed with Febreeze and ran them through the dryer with a dryer sheet on low heat for about 30 mins. No more smell, and still smelled good at the end of the night - and I live in Miami where it is HUMID to say the least. If you are on the fence, buy them! You won’t be disappointed, especially for the price!

Good quality, cheap button Good quality for the price. Run a little small. I’m 5’4 135 lbs and I ordered a Medium because small didn’t fit. The button is cheap and feels like it’s about to fall off but these pants do the job.

love! but too short I never write reviews but I figured it might help someone with sizing. I’m about 130 pounds and 5’5. I got a medium in these pants, they fit great and feel good! However they are just a little too short for me. They kinda stop around my ankles which I didn’t like. Other than that theyre a good buy! (I also got the bodysuit from Amazon and I’m obsessed with it!!)

Very flattering and comfy I am extremely impressed with these pants. Many faux leather pants make this annoying swishy noise when rubbed together (aka walking) but these just glide smoothly with no detectable noise. The waist band is stretchy which makes it so comfortable throughout the day - you can eat a large meal in these and not need to unbutton yourself. There is no gap at the waist either, which is difficult to find when you’re more well-endowed. They’re very trendy and I really couldn’t be any happier. For reference, I’m 5’7” about 180lbs and bought a size XL.

I bought a pair of black skinny jeans in the size 12 LONG month ago. They arrived in less than a week. I've been using them for 3 weeks and I'm happy to say I'm glad I bought these pants. I gotta be honest, I was not expecting much, 1/10 jeans I buy actually fit, given how tall and oddly shaped I am. I am almost 6 ft tall, have a relatively thin waist but thick thighs and big calves. Buying pants is a nightmare for me. Or should I say was, because I finally found pants just my size. These fit like a glove. I can comfortably walk in them and although I wish they were a few cm longer, I'm okay with the length and the fit. They seem to be made out of a good thick material too. I would gladly buy another pair.

So Comfortable!!! If you’re thinking about getting them, get them!!! I travel to different job sites and it is the middle of summer and way too hot for jeans! But these are perfect, they’re light weight, stretchy, comfortable, and most importantly have extra pockets!!! I’ve already bought three pairs in three colors, so I highly recommend! Great I am 5’6 and 170 lbs. these pants are really comfy, the flex band in the top is a game changer. The pockets are nice and real , and the cargo pockets on the side are good for putting your phone or keys in. These are thick and would be good for outside jobs or the winter. I normally wear 14 or 16 . I got these in 16 plus and they are loose on me and I do need a belt but I feel 14 might be too tight. As you can see in the pictures they are the correct length when standing however these ride up SO much when you sit down and they become extreme high waters when sitting. I put them in the dryer and there was no shrinkage. I would like another pair in long.

Great for curvy pear/hourglass shaped people! Let me start this by saying I wasn't expecting to fit in these as well as I do. I bought these knowing I could pass them down to my sister if I didn't fit in them as I wanted to. Jeans are hard for me to find that fit well, and I usually opt for trousers or leggings instead. Day 1 of their arrival I wore them to work (see picture) and I had no issues with them. They proved to withstand all the bending over, on my knees and fast movement through an 8 hour shift. They didn't make me uncomfortable, they didn't cause any chafing, or didn't unzip and expose my underwear like I've also experienced with jeans. I got a few compliments from coworkers and customers on them, as they usually dont see me in jeans. The flare on them is adorable, however I will say as im 5'3, I did have to roll them so they didn't drag while at work. So shorties, be aware of the flare drag! Color is beautiful I love the wash. I didn't notice any weird smell from them. And they're very thick, as I stated before I'm constantly on my knees at work and don't feel much stretch from them. I attribute that to their mostly cotton makeup, it's not cheap polyester that easily tears under pressure.

Awesome 1970’s Retro look Returned the bright blue day color, that cost 3 times as much but kept the elevated retro color that was a steal at $16.00 and took a chance on boiling them for 30 minutes on stove top and drying on sanitize cycle for maximum shrinkage. See photo laying on top of retuned pair, the length shrunk almost two inches. Still need to wear with heals, so not for everyday at almost 70 years old, but they make me feel young again. I am 5’3” tall with longer legs and shorter torso. I bought the size 12 short which did shrink all over so if you are big from the knees up these may not be for you. They were scary long looking out of the package but 99% cotton does shrink nicely. If I manage to shrink them a little more they could rate 6 stars!

The only thing is… make sure to check out the length in sizing. I usually wear a regular length but if you look at the length for regular in these jeans it says 33. Longs are 35 and shorts are 31. I should have looked at that more closely and ordered short. That’s my error. The regular were a bit long on me. Next time I will order the short. Lastly, I apologize for the smudgy mirror pics. My littles love to play with that mirror and it always gets smudgy.

Very long!! I am 5’7” 168lbs and carry most of my extra weight in my tummy. I ordered a size 12 regular. I’m not sure if I want to keep these or not. Good Flares I was drawn to these jeans because it said they were mid-rise (and on sale). I can’t do high waisted jeans and I have struggled to find mid-rise flares at a reasonable price. After reading the reviews I wasn’t sure if I would like them. Many sizes were sold out because they were on sale. I ordered an 8 regular in a blue wash and an 8 short in a grey wash. As you can see from the pictures, the regular comes up to my belly button. I’m only 5’ 3.5” and weigh about 135 but still. I was disappointed. They were definitely long, but I thought maybe with a heel I could get away with it. Then I tried on the short. They didn’t come up as high (although still a little higher than I wished). And the length was perfect. Some people said their size was a little loose. These are definitely not loose on me. I’m actually hoping they might loosen up a bit after I wear them. I’m returning the 8 regular and plan to keep the 8 short. I haven’t worn them other than to try on so I can’t yet speak for how they will hold their shape after all day. They do have some stretch to them; enough to make them conformable and not stiff.

Good material, odd fit for me. These jeans are quality denim and a great length for taller people. I'm 5'8", 175lbs, and I ordered the "Blue Veil Dx" color option in size 10L. The hem hits the floor and since I usually don't wear flat shoes, this isn't a problem. The price is unbeatable. I haven't owned a pair of Lee jeans in many years. Unfortunately, now I remember why: The fly area always seems to be crooked or slightly off center on Lee jeans. Every pair I've ever tried over the years has had this problem. After wearing for a while, walking around, etc, the fly pulls more to one side and looks crooked. This isn't the case on any other brand of jeans I've tried, so it must be some flaw with this brand. It's weird. But hey, they're nice jeans otherwise, and many people seem to like them. This brand just isn't for me.

Great jeans I was skeptical about buying such inexpensive pants. But I have worn Lee brand before and knew my size, so I took a chance. So glad I did! I ordered size 8 regular. I have a 34” inseam, being 5’8” and most of it legs! These jeans have a 33” inseam. Just long enough. The long size is a 35” inseam, which would be dragging on the floor for me. The fit is great for someone with slim hips and a small butt. Curvy gals might think the legs were too tight, but they work for me. Material is soft with just the right amount of stretch. Highly recommend!

SICK i am 5”8 and about 135 lbs and these jeans fit PERFECT for me. the inseam is a little long, but i only plan to wear these with boots. i got the 4 regular size. the denim is so nice as well!! these are perfect for going dancing or just wearing out!!! will prob be buying more colors

The wide legs are pretty wide, bordering on baggy; I think I'm going to get them tailored slightly so they're less palazzo-like and drape more similarly to Levi's Ribcage Wide Leg (which I love the width of the legs, but Levi's just generally do not fit me well in the waist, butt, and thigh areas). These have a bit of stretch as well. I love them. I got them in another color, and have another on the way -- they're that great!

Cute but size up! I bought a small. I’m 127 and 5’5. It’s tight in the crotch and no room for stretch in the thighs. I’m actually scared to sit down in these 😅 other than that, super cute and length is great for my long legs. Usually have to look for longer pant legs than what’s usually provided.

Lee?? I’m a new fan! I’m 48. Prior to this purchase, I wouldn’t have ever purchased Lee brand. I lean more towards AG, Paige, and citizens of humanity style and fit. BUT… I was struggling with finding modern fit work pants that weren’t “skinny” or didn’t make me look 5 sizes larger. High waist on me is no bueno. No one is more surprised than me that I am wearing a pair of $24 Lee pants that are stylish and exactly what I was looking for! They have stretch too. For reference I’m 5’8 125lbs and the 2 fit perfectly.

Super comfortable but inconsistent length I bought these in black, bungalow khaki and indigo rinse, all size 6 short. They are incredibly comfortable and flattering but the lengths are all different. I returned the indigo and khaki and kept the black. I love them and wish the lengths were consistent because I’d buy every color if they were.

Nice material and fit I really like the material and the fit. I’m not a fan of the wide leg bottoms (hence the 4 stars) but at least the top fits and they are long enough for my 5’10” height.
Perfect pants, finally I feel beautiful in these pants. 5’1”, 125 lbs, 31 years old and I bought the 2 Short.

What a disappointment! I decided to take a chance on these jeans since I could return them for free. They are being returned. They fit okay. They look pretty good in the side view pic, right?
