Excellent Pants, but be aware of possible defects! Let me start first by giving measurements for reference: 5'3" , 137lbs , 29" waist, 38" hips, 27" inseam That being said, I have tried all 6/8 short/petite relaxed/curvy pants on Amazon (Rafaella Gaberdine curvy, Signature Levi curvy Straight, Gloria Vanderbilt Amanda & GV Curvy Skinny, Dickies Curvy Straight, Lee Relaxed Side Elastic Straight, etc) and ALL of them either had a gap in the waist, were too tight across the rear, or were simply just too long.. however THESE pants fit great and had neither issue! I finally found a pants that not only fit, but are modest for work-- flattering but not figure hugging. I bought these in size 6 petite in colors (pictured from left to right) Indigo Wash (like a blue jean color/type fabric) Black Imperial Blue Mini Glenplaid (Black on light grey- NO BLUE in this, which i actually prefer) Cypress (Dark Olive Green) Jet Black/White Rockhill Plaid Falcon (pinkish brown) Upon close inspection, I noticed 1 of the 6 pairs I had purchased (Falcon) were defective. On the back leg, it appears as if holes were accidentally cut in the pants and then attempted to be "patched" by resewing it back together. (see pictures) Very disappointed that Amazon would allow this and have reordered/requested a return on this one pair. Because of the issue and trouble, I am deducting 1 star.

too long, (way) too wide at the hips What I love about these jeans is that they truly are high-rise, straight-legged, and fit me well around my waist. I'm 5'4" and weigh around 176, so I got a women's 20 petite. What I dislike is that these seem to be made for a height of someone who is around 5'6". In addition, though perhaps it's hard to see here, the hips are way too large, and I doubt going down a size would make much difference. While I understand that these are to be relaxed fit, these are more like no-fit. Nothing like the photo in any case. Having said that, I know Lee is a good brand, it's hard to find black, high-rise jeans so I'm having them altered by having them taken up a good inch or so and having them taken in at a cost of around $35.00 (which really isn't too bad).

A little small for size They are a little tight for the size and If you are apart of the big booty club they do not have enough room in the butt. they will ride down in the back making you look like the plumber when you bend over or sit down. I am 5" 9 320lbs size 24. I bought the 24 long

I need to order these in more colours! - Update - I did Update in September 2024: Well, I did buy them in two more colours. And I'm not particularly glad I did. The worst were the Ecru ones. A total disaster. Otherwise, I wouldn't have knocked off a star, but if it weren't for the blue ones, I might have knocked off two! I think my ecru pair is defective. All three pairs I ordered were 12 Long. In the first two new pictures, I aligned the crotch of my original blue pair with the ecru. The latter are lower in the waist (not a plus, for me) and so much shorter in the legs I would think they weren't "Long", although it is written on the labels. They barely touch the top of my foot, which definitely doesn't work with this cut! There is a lot more stretch in the ecru ones, which makes them sort of clingy. On the positive side, they are not see-through, in the sense that they are opaque. But you can see the outline of every bump and dimple on my knees. and the fabric clings so much that the ankles flare out, although when I laid them on the other jeans, I could see they are also cut that way. The blue ones have a slight taper, which makes them look straight on the body, since most people have larger calves than ankles. The ecru ones actually flare out a tiny bit at the very bottom, and it looks terrible on (you'll have to trust me, I have not taken a photo and have already gotten rid of the jeans!). Sadly for me, I live overseas and sending the jeans back would probably cost as mush as the price of the jeans, so I have given them to a much shorter woman in hopes she can cut them off above the weird little flare and get some wear out of them. I also think one leg is longer than the other. It looked like it on, and it looked ike it when I laid them on the bed. They were definitely new and had all the tags on, but there was a small brown spot on the back on one leg. I think it will wash out, but I didn't bother with it because the jeans were so hopeless. I think it might be fly p**. I also bought the Washed Black. I have kept them and I think I will wear them. Thankfully, since they cost $48 US, which is quite a bit for an ordinary pair of jeans. They are also smaller than my blue pair, but I can wear them. They are longer than the ecru pair, a smidge shorter than the blue. The colour is nice and they fit better than Ecru (I forgot to mention that the ecru pair comes up higher in the front than the back!!!). As they should be, they are higher in the back than the front, where they are about two inches below my belly button (of course, I am tall, but then again, these are supposed to be long). They are also stretchier, more synthetic than the blue ones, but less than the ecru ones (synthetic jeans are not a good thing in my book). But they are okay. I think Anchor two years ago rates a 4.5, Washed Black in 2024 rates a 3.5 or maybe 4, and the Ecru ones I received on the same day rate a solid 2!!!!!!!!!!! On the plus side: These are my jeans. They fit very well and are very comfortable. Most of the time, Lee jeans in size 12 Long are as good as it gets on me. I need a 34" inseam and these, hmm, they might be 33", but they are okay. (I'm 5'11") Although I prefer a high rise, and these are mid rise, they are okay and do not give me a muffin top. They are straight leg, but pretty narrow. Just that little bit of extra fabric at the ankles so you can tell they're not jeggings. Actually, they're nothing like jeggings, because the fabric is fairly thick, although nice and soft and doesn't stifle you like plastic (it's 30 degrees Celsius/86 fahrenheit where I am right now, so that's important). The colour is Anchor and pulling it out of the package, I thought it was darker than expected. Then I realized they have those fake wear marks down the front. The faded part goes to below the knee, so that it looks like I have really long thighs and short calves, whereas I am the exact opposite. But it's not a deal breaker. And there is a little whiskering on top, although not too much. I would prefer an even colour all over (hey, I'm on the far side of 55) but it isn't too bad. I really like these quite a bit, so I am going to go back and look at other colours. Hope this helps!

Perfectly simple, not see-through, white jeans Was looking for a plain straight leg white jean, no holes/no shredding, no flare, no wide leg, or skinny, not cropped or ankle length (I am 5'9.5" with 33" inseam), just straight leg jeans that was not see-through. These jeans are just what I wanted. Ordered 12L - my usual size in jeans. Perfect length. Note: these are not a bright white, more soft/winter white.

These are defective and should have been sold as rejects I am 5’8 145 lbs and ordered the Anchor color in size 8, long. Received order promptly. Pants looked like size ten, regular. They are baggy and inseam is only 31.5…. 31.5 is not a tall. Thinking I could tolerate these, I removed the tags. Now on wearing them, I noticed the stitching on the left leg is uneven, causing the pant leg to turn uncomfortably. This is uncomfortable and irritating. These do not meet my expectation of quality. Today I found a review showing how much shorter the Anchor color are than a darker color, another about misaligned leg seam, and many saying they suspect these as seconds. These should have been disclosed as defective rejects. I will not order jeans from Amazon again. ugh now I see rear pockets don’t match. These are new pants received today that I have not washed

A Little too much stretch so I would go a size smaller with these! They are very comfortable but not a lot of denim. So they are a good stretch jean. As you can see I order 14R too short for 5'6 and they fit good at first but a little baggy after a days wear. I just ordered a 12L in these. They should fit perfectly. Great buy and I just put these down in some boots! Good to Go!

Comfy I’m 5 foot 4 / 160 lbs. I got a 10 long and they’re perfect. I can’t stand all my dress pants being too short after one wash, so the long is perfect for me. I see what someone said about the color being more of a blue black.. but it doesn’t bother me.

Not sure but will try.... The pants received are slightly different than what was shown... I received higher waisted pant that has a zipper with a double button closure... since higher waisted, Waist sizing is fitted... if you have a defined waist no problem but if have no waist definition (or a short torso) go a size up or if you are in between sizes go with the larger of the two sizes... I wear size 8, am 5’2 with a short torso but have a slightly defined waist so I am ok wearing my normal size (but is snug) and the pants are perfect length to wear with heals (think business casual)... I have thick thighs so the pants a slightly fitted in the thigh but still flattering. the pant is very flattering on your bootie, in the advertisement you can see the model is slender and flatters, still is true for us full bottom beauties! material is kinda rough... Again I have thick thighs, so I am concerned the material will make noise as I walk... (oh dear, the sound of 70’s corduroy pants is popping in my head... best way to describe the sound these pants make) For the price, worth keeping to wash and wear... worse case I can give pants to one of my taller friends that has thinner thighs lol

NOT BLACK Trust me when I say I love these slacks. I do, I really do. BUT they aren’t true black. They’re more of a black blue. Unfortunately, Lee does not have a true black in this type of pant so I’ll sadly be returning. The length, fit, style is absolutely perfect but it needs to match this blazer. Perfect length and cut for heels. I’m 5’4, 175lbs, and a typical size large or size 10/12 in pants. I got a size 10 and they aren’t too tight, too loose, they’re just right. Extremely flattering and comfortable Levi’s gets it right with curvy clothes. I have the worst time finding pants that fit my thighs and butt but also my waist. I have very aggressive dimensions, 44x36x50, and I typically have to wear leggings everywhere. But when I need to be a grown up and look professional, but still feel like a woman, these pants did the job and fit like a dream. For context, I’m about 5’7”, 250 lbs, with ridiculously thunderous thighs and booty. These pants fit and flattered with no significant waist gap. I will be getting more styles.

Very comfortable and fit like a “glove” These pants are perfect for casual wear or work wear! Straight leg, mid rise and regular fit! The length (long) is perfect for me. True to size (6) for reference I am 143 lb and 5’7”
I am 5’8” but have a long torso as opposed to legs. The regular length fits perfect and the size 2 is great on my waist, which is about 25 inches.

Length shrinks A LOT I am not a tall person by any stretch of the imagination -- I'm 5'3" on a good day. I ALWAYS have to order short/petite pants or hem up regular pants by several inches. Having seen the reviews on these saying that they shrink, I went ahead and bought the regular length ones. Goodness, am I happy I erred on the side of caution. They started with a 29.5" inseam (see photo). After two or three wash and dries, they've shrunk 2" up to a 27.5" inseam. They're nearly too short for even me, but they'll work. That aside, they fabric is thick, soft, and high quality. It's perfect for work, lays flat, doesn't wrinkle, and it gives a nice silhouette for curvy hips/bum. They're also pull-ons for extra comfort, and they have front pockets. I really do like them and I'll be getting several more pairs for work, but I feel bad for anyone taller than me.

Nice, but not for my body type These pants are the “pull-on formal” type with no fly/ button, a thick elastic waist and detailing like overstitched pockets and belt hooks to make them work-appropriate. The fabric is of pretty decent thickness but if I kept them I’d probably need shapewear to cover the vpl and lumps and bumps. I ordered them in long, as the current fashion for flood pants doesn’t suit me, and they would be just about right with a modest heel (I’m 5’6” and somewhat short waisted). I have a small waist and big booty, but I was hoping they would work for me due to the stretch and elastic waist but they gaped really badly at the back. If I went down a size they would probably fit better at the waist but I wouldn’t want them to be any tighter on the hips for work pants. I returned them because of this but think the quality and style are good.

Very comfortable! Excellent quality Not sure why these pants got low reviews. But these Lee pants are legit comfortable. My measurements: waist 36 inch, hip 44 inch. Weight: 170, height: 5’6” I opted for the size 14 regular. I’m Somewhat curvier in the back. I did order 16, but it was too big, especially around the waist. The 14 is not too tight, or too loose. Inseam is perfect for my height. These are pull on with no zipper to deal with. Two side pockets that are deep. Back pockets are fake (cannot access). Material is a lil on the heavier/thicker side but soft and quality. Good stretch throughout. Try it out! I ended ordering more colors after this one.
Very comfortable I like the jeans but just didn't realize they were mid rise!! I think they run large but idk.. I got sz 10M I'm 5'7 185lbs (today) lol

Im 5’0 and weight 145. I bought a size 6 short. They fit nicely on me. The only problem i had was zipping it . I had to ask my son to zipped it for me since it was a little tuff to pull it up myself.

I am satisfied with my purchase. I am 4'11 & 143lbs and it's so hard to find clothes that look decent on me. I got these in size 6 petite size and they fit perfectly. Just know they do not stretch at all.
They don’t really stretch. They do fit I’m 5’7 and 380lbs. I am a size 24.
Nice material Good fit but a couple loose strings, not sure how long it will hold up but we’ll see. Fabric stretchy. I’m 5’4” 160lbs, size 10.

Pleasantly Surprised I had zero expectations for these, but I had them recommended by an Amazon influencer and decided to give them a try! First of all, I sized UP. I wanted a super baggy look, so after reading reviews I went with a Large instead of my usual Small. I’m glad I did! They’re super high rise, baggy in the legs but tighter in the waist. However, I can still pull them down lower and wear them slouchy. There is no stretch in the fabric, FYI. Length is long on me at 5’2” but I kinda like it. Print and fabric quality is actually really nice. But please note: they smell AWFUL right out of the bag. Like, truly so bad. I had to air them out overnight in my garage, then wash them inside out with detergent and laundry sanitizer. That seems to have helped tremendously. That’s really my only complaint. Still keeping them! :)

Great Jeans, Wrong Size. Beware “Amazon Size Recommendation!” Update: Got the right size (25), attached a new image of the fit above. Original review: I couldn’t decide between getting a size 27 or 28, and Amazon’s recommended size was 28. I got the 27 because it would arrive in time for my outing, and it still ended up being way too big (I prefer skinny/bootcut jeans to fit tightly), so the Amazon recc would’ve been way off. The Amazon size recc is based on just your height and weight, not all your measurements, and it messed up another order of Levi’s jeans I got, so I would trust the size guide instead! Other than that, the jeans are amazing. They’re comfortable levels of stretch while still being thick and giving quality, the deep blue color in combination with the contrasting thread in the “Casting Shadows” color is just 🧑‍🍳💋 chefs kiss perfect. Also, in the last image you can see it has the Levis stamp and tag so it’s definitely official, at least my variant! It also comes from the official Levis store and Amazon is strict against impersonators so I don’t think this is fake like that one reviewer claimed. Some Levi’s jeans have a tag that’s just red, without the logo, I own some of those. So yes! Buy it, but make sure to order the right size! My measurements: Height: 5’7 Weight: 120lbs Natural waist: 27 in Belly button: 29 in Hip: 33 in Butt: 37 in

Petite/short buyers beware: Inseam measurement I am 5’2 inches tall with a 27.5 inch inseam (crotch to floor). I ordered size M because sizing chart referenced inseam at 27in. When I tried on the pants, they dragged the floor (even with heels). Confused, I check listing (again) and measure myself (see pics) to discover the inseam measurement is 29.4 inches. Color is a very nice (classic) deep navy blue, the high rise waist measurement is accurate and very comfortable. Well positioned pleating allows for classic and traditional trouser fit (not too full in buttock/hip). 100% poly fabric is cheap, very lightweight and retains static electricity (clinging to legs and attracting lint and hair). It is disappointing that the mfg and vendor failed to communicate and ensure sizing chart reflected accurate information to the end user consumer. The trousers are low priced reflective of their low value and unworthy of the time and money it would take to hem. I will be returning.

Poorly made. Faux zipper off centered to the right. These pants are a cheap quality version of slacksweats. They fit pretty well, and are comfortable on, but as soon as I put them on, I immediately noticed that the waist is not centered. You can see in my pictures that it’s off to the right, which is even more noticeable when they’re on. It’s a definite no for me because that’s the only thing I can focus on now; just how much off center they look. So for them to look right with the faux zipper centered on the tummy, I’d have to twist them to one side, which throws off the whole fit of the pants due to the other seams and pockets. Looking back at the other reviews, I can see that many of the other colors are cut/sewn the same way, but may not be as noticeable in a darker color. Bottom line, just not worth it when there are so many other better options with more flowy material that’s not this weird rough waffle weave anyway. I prefer a more sophisticated look, but wanted a pull-on comfortable dress slack. This is definitely not it, even if the seams did line up. FYI, I am 5’6, 190lbs, wear a size medium pant, and these were true to size in a medium.

Lightweight, Comfortable, Breathable & Versatile Palazzo Pants I ordered these because I’ve got about 10 pairs of Libin’s cargo joggers, which I love, and these palazzos looked fun. (For reference, I’m 5’2”, 36 inches at the waist, 41” bum). After trying them on here’s my impression: PROS: 1. 100% polyester, so they won’t shrink. The outside is a textured waffle weave that is 100% breathable, but the inside is smooth and soft. 2. SIzing is Spot on. I went by both my usual Libin size (XL) and their sizing chart (36 in waist), and super happy I did. These don’t pinch at the waist, but the top part of the pants aren’t excessively baggy. 3. Versatile and cute. I was just going to try them on this morning, thinking that they’d be great for church, but then I ended up wearing them on today’s errands. Considerations: 1. The fit is comfy given my large hips, thick thighs, and will be comfy and flattering around my IBS flare ups. Fabric is actually flattering! I tested a bunch of linen pants at various shops recently and didn’t like the thin, see through linen (again, sensitive about my big hips.). These are a little thicker, and while the fabric is a little rougher (I suspect they’ll soften in the wash with some fabric softener), they’ll wear much better. And, the pricing here really can’t be beat for the quality. I ordered the striped navy blue. Navy is super dark (my lighting in pics is bad, sorry!), so the pinstripes pop nicely. I’m considering ordering another pair before my upcoming trip! These will travel so well, I can already tell. In a nutshell- yes, buy them.

Waist is tight, they run small and short, they shrink! I'm very disappointed in these pants, as they are a cute design and looked like they'd be comfortable. But I can't really wear them after washing them. For reference, I am 5'7" and 133#. I ordered size Small. My photos were taken BEFORE I washed them... 1) There are many loose threads hanging off these pants, due to low quality control. 2) The elastic waist is very tight. Too tight for a supposedly comfy pajama/lounge pant, especially one worn mid-rise. I think buyers should size-UP. 3) These pants are 100% Cotton, and definitely shrank after the first wash in cold water and tumble dry on Low heat. Now they feel even smaller and are too short for me. In general, I recommend that buyers SIZE-UP by 1-2 sizes, and either hang dry or dry on low heat for very short time. Too bad the quality is low, because the navy/white gingham design and "boxer" look is cute.

Nice, comfortable, sized down one and fit was perfect I was looking for a mid to high wasted pair of jeans that are on the thinner/looser side and can be worn throughout the year, even in the warmer season. And, a pair that aren't too distressed because there are times when that look just isn't appropriate. These meet all my criteria. There is nothing super special about the way they look, but they can be dressed up or down and are extremely comfortable. I am usually a small or size 26 in jeans (5'4" 118 lbs) but had read these run large so I got the extra small and they fit perfectly. They are very stretchy so I believe will accommodate any body shape, but they don't bag or bunch anywhere on me. I often have that problem with jeans because my hips are so narrow but not with these. Good quality, fits well... I ordered the Lookbook Denim Jeans for Women in Pecan Brown Size XXL. To start, these are not high waisted pants. They are hip huggers, meaning they sit low on your waist and will not restrict your tummy if you have a few extra pounds. Furthermore, they state they jeans are stretch. They have a slight stretch to them, but they are not what is normally considered a stretch pant. Finally, these are not baggy on the average girl. They are boot cut/flare leg, but not baggy.With all of that said, I am 5 foot, 5 inches tall, 275 pounds, wear an 18/20 USA size and a 2x/3x top. My waist measurement at belly button level is 46 inches. My upper thighs and calves are thicker than most. I was concerned these pants would not fit, however, the Lookbook Denim Jeans for Women in Pecan Brown Size XXL fit comfortably. I will say there is a bit of tightness in the knee area, but I tend to have this issue due to my thick legs. I am not use to a hip hugger pant, so was genuinely surprised that these pants fit tight, but comfortably, as they sit under my "jelly roll", so there is none of that constrictive feeling on your stomach. I also like my pants longer and I found these to fit perfectly, touching the ground, giving them room to shrink a bit.Overall, the Lookbook Denim Jeans for Women in Pecan Brown in Size XXL are well made, the denim is thicker than most and I absolutely love the color. The fit is surprisingly good and the hip hugger style makes them extremely comfortable. So if you are a curvy girl like me, check these out. Five stars.

Stylish & Comfy Wide-Leg Jeans. I got these jeans for my daughter & they look adorable on her. She especially loves how stretchy & super comfy they are. Sizing might run a bit small. She’s a size 6, which the description equates to a "small," but I decided to order up 1 size as a precaution & opted for the "medium." Thankfully, that was the right decision. They fit her perfectly. The high waist is flattering. They’re fitted on the hips & backside without being too snug. Although they’re wide-leg, they don’t look baggy, maintaining a stylish shape. These jeans have a sturdy zipper, metal button, & back pockets, giving them that classic denim jeans look. However, the material is thinner than your average denim & even thinner than other stretchy denim I’m used to. Despite the thinner fabric, you can’t see through them in the white color, so it’s not a major concern. My daughter is 5'7", & the bottom hem lands just beneath her heel, which makes them look long with flat sandals. However, they pair well with shoes that have a raised sole or heel, like boots & wedges. She did mention they’re a bit long below the zipper, causing a slight bunching when she sits. Fortunately, this improved after washing, as I expected since stretchy denim tends to draw up a bit. Although the care instructions advise against tumble drying, I did toss them in the dryer on a low, wrinkle-free setting & they held up just fine. Overall, these jeans are stylish & fit great. I’d recommend getting precise measurements & comparing them to the size chart, as you may need to size up. The denim is decent quality, though I’m a bit concerned about how long they’ll last given the thinner material. But mostly, she’s thrilled with how comfortable they are.

Super high waist but very little shape I’m 5’6”, 129 lbs., and wearing the medium (8-10) in my photo. I’m normally a consistent size 4 in US pants/jeans but I’ve learned to order up from Amazon. I’m happy with the fit of the medium. The fabric is lightweight and feels like a thick chiffon. The pants have a button/zipper fly and deep hip pockets but the back pockets are fake. There are belt loops on the waistband but the back is elastic so the belt is optional. The color is a deep navy. The legs are long enough that I have to wear at least a short heel to keep them from dragging the ground. What I like: 1. The super high waist and wide leg style. 2. Lightweight and flowy, good for summer work pants. 3. Real (and deep) pockets. What I don’t like: 1. The material has a nylon pantyhose feel, just scratchy enough to be noticeable. 2. Profile of the pants is kind of shapeless. The fabric just hangs and does very little to flatter the figure.

Nice fitting, middle-weight jeans. Perfect length for someone 5'4"! These are a well made pair of jeans that surprised me with a little more flare than I had anticipated. I loved bell-bottoms when I was growing up (yes, I am that old, lol) but my mom mostly did not allow me to have them, so I was thrilled the past 15 years or so when they have been in style a few times. They are of medium weight and softness. The stitching is solid and looks nice on them. I like the seam that runs down the front of them. I can remember a long while back when that was a style too. They have 2 front and 2 back pockets, all of them functional. I got the X-Large and they fit me very nicely with a bit of room to spare due to the 2% elastane in the material. Definitely can recommend these for nice looking and comfortable jeans.

Cute pants & I like them a LOT, but they have a slight fit issue you should be aware of! Ok, I ended up buying two different sizes, & since they didn't have the large in the same color, I got it in a lighter blue instead. For reference, I'm apx. 5'7" & 138lbs & have what they say is more of an 'hourglass' figure with an apx. 29-30" waist & apx. 40" hips and the darker blue pants are the Med. & the lighter are the large! The med. fits more like a small in the waist, that's why I decided to try the large. I was thinking it'd be perfect, but it fits more like an XL! Odd to say the least! I can't decide yet which I will be keeping, so thought I'd post this with BOTH so you could see for yourself & hopefully it will help you decide. The colors are oddly close, & pretty close to true in these pics.. This is how they look right out of the package, so yes, they are very wrinkled! I have no doubt though, being as I've dealt with this type of material before, that once washed & hung or dried, they will be fine! Worst case, nothing an iron won't fix! :-) I love the flowyness (if that's even a word LOL) of the pants, but prefer the large for this! Just a little more like a skirt with that size, which is what I like better! Love the flirty aspect of the slit in the front too! It's on both sides, but I just posted the pic of the one. I like the length better on the large too, as the Med. just seems a little TOO short for my taste! But, I actually prefer the darker blue?!!? So I'm torn!! I think I will end up keeping the lighter Large & just take in the elastic some so it'll fit me better & return the Med.! The waist is mostly elastic, except for the very front where it has the tie, so it's on the forgiving side for fit which is nice if you fluctuate in weight like I do! I tend to go up or down 5-10 lbs now & then! LOL Nicely made though, and is priced ok, so I would recommend. They have some nice detail that other pants like this don't which is a huge plus for me! The material is nice & light weight & perfect for spring/summer, but then they could just as easily be paired with nylons for those colder times! Love that they can be either causal or dressy too! Very versatile! I'm always on the lookout for those kinds of buys! Especially now when our dollar isn't going as far! The material has a sort of texture to it to which is kind of nice! Not something that is distracting though! All & all, they are cute, & I like them! LOVE that they come in so many colors too! I just might have to get another couple of these in other colors soon!! Only wished they had a royal blue and/or a hot pink! Maybe they will hear me and add some?! Well....hope this helps! Thanks much! ENJOY!

Comfortable, good length, but inside is not lined I like these pants overall, but WHY is the inside not lined with the same material?? The white inside sometimes shows and it really cheapens it in my opinion. If you’re going to have a giant slit the inside should be lined. Anyway, they are very comfortable, lightweight, and the inseam is just the right length for me! I’m between 5’7 and 5’8. But I should mention, I tried these pants in other colors (solid colors) and returned them. For some reason the solid colors look like long church-girl skirts to me. I only kept this pair. The pattern somehow makes it look more fashionable and less fundamentalist-churchy.

Great Yoga Pants These wide leg yoga pants are incredibly comfortable with nice stretchy material and a good fit. I love the fit especially for yoga as I find they have a nice, loose, wide leg style yet are still fitted in the right areas, and I love that they move with my body when I am doing different poses. The addition of pockets is a nice touch, even though I might not put anything too bulky in them since they are form-fitting to the body, but they are nice and deep and still great for slipping your hands into. I ordered my usual size Medium, and they fit great. For reference, I'm 5'6", 145 lbs, with a 28" waist and 38" hips. The only small issue I noticed is that panty lines can be visible when wearing these pants, so you might want to opt for seamless underwear depending on how they fit you. Overall, these have become my go-to lounge and yoga pants. They're so easy to slip on and off, and they look flattering while still being incredibly comfortable.

Comfy, Flattering & Stylish The Maxbee Wide Leg Pants rate high on function and style. They're super comfortable, flattering, and breathable. I got my wife a large for her size 12/5'5 form and they fit perfectly. They're slimming without feeling tight and they fall nicely over the legs. They feel really smooth and soothing on the skin and go with almost anything. They're not just for yoga, but can be used for any occasion. They pair well with tunics and tees and really hit the mark for style and timelessness. They're also easy maintenance and quick hang dry. The side pockets are a welcome inclusion as well. ~ Kort

Size Up and Bend Over I’m 5’3, 120 lbs, normal build, ordered size small. Okay, so... my first two pictures should explain why these get only 3 stars. My husband and I both busted out laughing when I pulled these out of the package. They could easily be mistaken for a child’s pants by their size, until you turn them around and they look like a deflated pin cushion. But then I put them on, and was quite impressed actually at the material - I personally love this material for leggings, it’s the only kind I’ll buy. So kudos are due there. I immediately realized I should have ordered a size up to be safe (although the description/reviews say true to size). Granted they’re a size to small, I like how they fit (assuming they’ll be more impressive when I order a medium). The butt shaper detail is “okay” at best, it definitely gives some definition but the way the butt seam is sewn in makes it shiny so it looks like you’ve got a string up your crack. I’m not sure if that part will go away when I size up, but it’s not so bad that I’m not willing to risk it. I just recommend doing a “bend over” test before leaving the house in sunlight. Overall they’re alright, crossing my fingers a medium makes them 5 star. Key takeaway: SIZE UP!

Cute design, thin material and snug fitting I am 5'4", 120lbs, and usually a size 4 in pants (though I can sometimes be a 6 since I am curvy). I bought these in a 28 and they are comfortable but snug. The brown color is nice, and I like the button, the hemming, and the high waist, which works well with crop tops. The length is fine, and about as short as I would go in pants. The fabric registers as a very 70's, thin polyester. VPL is a real issue, so exercise caution with your choice of undergarments. You may also want to size up for a looser fit, as these are very fitted and stretchy. I took two stars off because for this price (about $35 at the time of my writing) these are not cheaper than what I might find at a store specializing in casual basics, and those stores would have higher quality materials and craftsmanship. The tag on these says "do not put in the dryer" so they seem like they won't hold up.

Not flattering on wide hips For reference I am 5’8”, 155 lbs and typically a US Women’s size 6-8. I have wide hips, backside and thighs. I got a size Medium. This was not at all a flattering fit. Pros - color is true to photos and green was great for fall. Material was nice, thicker than expected and comfortable. It would be too thin for fall wear in colder climates though. Cons - Silhouette was bad. The high waist with elastic and zipper did not work. The added zipper was flimsy, jammed on first use and made the fit at the waist very unflattering. It wrinkled easy but that was expected. They ended up being a little short on me as well. The quality was also subpar. I was very concerned with the tightness in hips that I would tear the flimsy seam in any sitting movement. Overall I do not recommend unless you are very thin or have a more straight build.

I've lost a lot of weight and switch between anywhere from M,L,XL depending on the brand (not weight fluctuatation). I have larger breasts, wide hips and butt. I gave the pants a good review because it's not the pants fault I'm in between sizes and short. Fits awkward body type! I have diabetes, and am insulin resistant. Because of this, my stomach bulges like a pregnant lady. It’s hard to find pants that fit right because they are always too tight in the waist and loose on the legs. If I get them to fit my waist right, the legs will be very loose and look so bad and frumpy. I am 5’7 and 175lbs, much of it carried in my stomach area. I chose 12Long but may have been able to size down 1 size, but they would be very snug. If you are my size and body type, I’d say stick with 12L, but if you prefer tight pants go down 1. If you have a big booty, go up 1. These pants fit my stomach AND legs properly. I have longer legs and the length covered my ankles very well and did not rise throughout the day like high waters, even with me bending and stooping down a lot at work. They were very soft and comfortable while still holding me in and giving me a nice shape overall. Made me look like a woman instead of an Oompa Loompa. I have only worn them once, but I have a cat and chinchilla so my black clothes always hold hair, but the hair did not stick to these. 5 stars! Will buy again! FYI: The back pockets are not real pockets.

Highest Waist Ever - And Slim Fit too! I've wanted some dress slacks that were longer in the leg, since I'm taller, and a 40" outseam sounded perfect. Usually that's about a 10" rise, so this should be perfect. Right? Well, I would be wrong there. The outseam indeed is 40", but the rise is - 14". Yikes! So when laid down and measured, the inseam is 27". So while they aren't capri pants, they are still a bit short for me. There is enough material at the seam that I can let out the hem, so that can work. However - while the size chart describes a 33" plus wait, in reality, it measures only 15" across. Which makes the waist 30". Yikes again. And with the heavy elastic in front it's a light fit. I notice that the model has a 25" waist, and wears a medium size, which is a 27" waist on the size chart. So that'll work for her, but for the rest of us, but this means ordering up a size... OVERALL - Which I should have done. Oh well...my search for the perfect dress pants continues!

Linen-Like, Casual Summer Pants For reference, I am 5’2” and a size 4 (27”) waist. The length of these pants were great on me, but seeing as how they were a perfect fit length wise, I could see that being problematic for anyone more than a few inches taller than me. The material is lightweight (linen-like) and the waist is elastic. The fabric itself is not stretchy, so I would say the waist is only moderately stretchy (limited by the actual fabric itself). I love the look of paper bag waist pants, but just have not found them to be that flattering on my body type (straight, no hips). Not an issue with these pants, just an overall life gripe! I will enjoy these in the summer with a cute pair of casual sandals and maybe a white or black casual tee on top.

Size up if you are a between size I am 5’6.5”, about 143-145 lbs, with a slightly athletic/muscular build. I tend to wear a 26/27 in pants and the medium fit me nicely. I also tried a size small (also pictured) but they were too tight in the mid thigh. Quick review version: pant material is nicer and thicker than expected, love that the waist band is smooth and not elastic. Nice length and overall shape. I can wear these with a pair of sneakers and the ends won’t drag. Longer/in depth: as mentioned, I have a bit more muscle, especially in the thighs. I first bought the small based on reviews and size guide. The waist fit me well, but the actual leg part was a bit too tight around my mid thigh. My pictures show me in the medium and small. You can see that the small is more tight in the thigh which causes a bit of buckling/a pouch to form at the top. The medium stays flowy through the leg. The waist on the medium is not too much larger and still fits me fine. These pants are good for cold weather or cold offices, but you could also probably wear them with a t shirt and not be too hot.

These fall beautifully I always hesitated to get pants online, as you never know how they end up looking and fitting. Dresses and tops are much easier. I really wanted a pair of wide leg pants though, and saw these through the Vine program. Read the reviews and pretty much everyone who got them seemed happy with them, so I pulled the trigger. Happy to these really do look and fit very well and the white is not see through. Which was surprising, as the fabric is a fairly light (but not very thin) polyester. I am not a fan of synthetic fibers and would certainly prefer these in a linen blend, but I must say I do appreciate how they fall because of the material and the feel is not bad at all either. They're quite soft and silky. At this price, I think they're a great value and totally recommend them. Might get them in another color too. For reference, I am 5'5, 130 lbs and got an S.

I would order again! Size reference: I’m 5’6” and 128 lbs Witt a 27.5” waist and 37” hips. I wear size 4/27 in pants/jeans and have both small and medium in stretchy pants. The size chart showed the small from Noahella to have a 28.34” waist and 41.34 inch hips. I think the small is just a little snug on my waist so the button looks slightly pulled. The length is perfect for flats. Description: The material feels and looks like “real” slacks, opposed to the cheap polyester look that snags easily. The fabric is 100% polyester but looks like chiffon and is flowy. The pleated front gives a dressier style and the wide legs are comfortable but look sophisticated. The back waistband is elastic but the front is flat with a button/zipper fly. There are belt loops, deep functional hip pockets, and faux back pockets. Recommendation: I love these pants for work! I feel stylish and comfortable when I wear them. The high waist is flattering with a fitted top or tucked-in loose top. I would be okay with wearing these for business travel, meetings, church, and wedding/baby showers.

Extremely long; nice fit, but fabric is a little thin This is something of a mixed review. While the fit looks attractive, and the pants are comfortable, it’s extremely long. Perfection ,and read for stats I'm a high rise convert, these are perfection! I'm small but have the muscle tone of Wonder bread plus I'm a 62 Y O grandmother. But in these jeans? I don't look it. I'm 5 7 @ 127 lbs - 29" waist and 39" hips and I bought the size 4 with 32" inseam which is More than long enough. I sew so non issue. My problem area is heinous muffin top if jeans not "just right" and many times I wear a Spanx cami to compensate for this. Not comfy in the south east summers lol. No Spanx needed with these. I'd always thought if I avoid my waist all together I'd avoid muffin top hence low waist line pants, I was wrong! These fit excellent in crotch which can also be another issue with me. The dreaded CT lol. heavy enough fabric that these are not see through, wearing black under things in this picture. Not blessed in the back of the house and these make the most of what I've got by being nice and snug without flattening what I have, great back pocket placement. Nice and higher but not too high up. I'd just tried on $200 white jeans yesterday at the mall but they were too sheer not to mention too expensive for what they were offering me. I'd pay if I had to but... Came home and went on Amazon, ordered Joe jeans at big $ and ordered low at this price. Ecstatic to Find these on my door step this morning and put them on to find my happy place in my fitting world. I can't believe the price of these and am ordering another because coffee guzzling me and white anything means I always order two if I love it lol.

As happy as I can be Ordered size 14, 5'7" / 202lbs and I don't love the way anything fits me at this weight. That being said, these jeans are not bad. They fit well, not super tight and not baggy. There is lots of stretch in the denim, so they are very comfortable even when sitting down. The denim is not thin so that it does not show every lump or bump. The waist goes to just at my navel, which means there is no muffin top. I posted a picture of the inside of the jeans front panel. I don't know that I look "thinner" but they don't make me look worse and thats a good thing. The jeans are long enough for 3 inch heels (I only had 2.5 inch heels on in the picture.) The denim is thick enough and has enough stretch to cuff them at the bottom to make them ankle length to wear with flats and still look cute. If someone wants the jeans to be tight and form fitting (for the lifting and tucking) I would suggest sizing down one size from usually size.

Soft, comfortable and classy I've worn NYDJ jeans for several years now and always worn a size 10. I ordered the NYDJ's Ponte Trouser in a size 10 and found the fit to be as expected for a knit trouser. Snug in the seat and upper thigh, with a very slight flare at the cuff. The length of these trousers on my 5'6" frame was slightly longer than expected but perfect with a 2-3" heel. These trousers are super comfortable. They are a mid-weight knit and look great paired with a sweater or blouse. They provide a snug, classy look that is perfect for work or play. Good looking, well made and easy to fall in love with because they feel so good on, the Ponte Trouser by NYDJ's is the wardrobe basic you've been looking for. Definitely recommend them! NOTE: I ordered both black and charcoal colors. One of the included photos are the two colors side by side.

Decent Jeans For reference: I am 5'8", 190ish lbs, pear shape with a meno-belly, 31" inseam, 37" waist, 45" hips. I typically wear 14/16 depending on the style and brand. I did look at their size chart and although my measurements did not match up I decided to try size 14 Tall. After washing on cold and drying on low heat I can say they fit. The inseam on the pair I received is 30", which works fine for me. These are snug around the waist but loosen up as you wear them. A 16 would have been too big/baggy, so with this jean I believe I am between the two sizes. There is a good weight to these but not so heavy they cannot be worn. Will be good Fall and Winter jeans, but too heavy for Summer. These are high waisted and indeed wide legged as described. These may be tummy controlled for smaller tummies, but without additional help from shapewear undergarments these do not help the meno-belly. Not a complaint, just an fyi. Overall I find these to be decent quality jeans for a reasonable price.

Not a style for me but very nice quality The denim is really nice weight in these high waist jeans. No visible panty lines, they’re not so heavy that they are hot to wear and have just enough stretch for them to keep their shape. The seams and hems are uniformly stitched and sturdy also. The legs are very wide, as shown, but I don’t feel that they have a baggy fit elsewhere. Unfortunately, on my body shape and height I don’t find them to be at all flattering I don’t know if it is the back pocket placement, the leg length or the snug fit of the jeans from the waist to the hips. (I’m 5’4” tall and wearing a size 10.) Even with a 2”heel I felt I looked short and squat. Not a look I can successfully carry off. On a more slender, taller frame they would be very cute.

Definitely size up! Pay CLOSE attention to the size chart! I think the size chart might be pretty accurate. I am 5'4" and about 200 pounds. I usually wear around a size 14 but I got the 16 just in case because of the measurements. These are still too small and much tighter than I like. The only reason they even still fit is because of how stretchy they are. So don't expect the sizes to match what you are used to. Check the size chart. If these were the right size for me I think I would like them ok. The stretchy material should be very comfortable. The length is good for me. The zippers is not smooth and takes some effort. It gets caught up a lot. The pockets are not very deep and the fabric feels like it won't last very long. The size on the pants says 32 everywhere. Nowhere on the pants does it have the size 16. Only on the plastic wrapping does it say size 16. So it isn't surprising that this doesn't match closer to actual US sizes. Overall it is an ok pair of pants. Just ok though.

Yes! Love them. 5'1, 143lbs, 31in waist, 41in hips, apron belly, and short torso. Normal pant size typically 10 or Large. I originally purchased a size 30 and quickly returned them as they were too loose. I then purchased the size 28 and still could have gone down one more to give my apron belly more compression without causing waist/handles spillover. These pants have a generous amount of stretch. You can also observe that I have 2 3/4 in - 3in heels and the pants fit perfectly without scraping the floor. I am very happy with this purchase.

Great jeans- total 90s vibe I’m 5’6, 125lb, 34DD. I usually wear a 26 and sometimes 27 in designer jeans and ordered size 26. I love these jeans! It’s like I’m reliving 1993! They are super stretchy and fit great. I took photos from three angles so you can see. They’re a nice fit all the way around but almost too tight in the waist for me. The material stretches so even though it’s tight, it’s not uncomfortable. I will, however, be careful to wash them in cold and hang them to dry. These are the highest waisted jeans I’ve ever owned but the buttons on the front really make them work. This is actually perfect for the crop tops that I sometimes hesitate to wear because now my midsection will be adequately covered. The length is perfect- I can wear tennies or boots or even a low heel and these jeans will look good with any of them. I highly recommend these jeans. If you want a looser fit, I would size up one size.

Really flattering and comfortable I had a pair of jeans like this back in high school and I think I wore them all the time. These will be no different! They fit true to size thank goodness! They have plenty of stretch if you need it, but fit really well without the stretch. They button up easily and don't really require a belt to stay up. They look great on and even my almost 60 year old body managed to make my tush attractive. The pockets are all fully functional and the leg length is just right for my 5' 5" frame. They sit at my natural waist and are very comfortable everywhere. Not too loose, not too tight anywhere. They're made well too. While they feel like an old pair of jeans when you put them on, the seams are firm and the material is thick enough to wear in winter in New Hampshire. A very good quality jeans and plenty of room to be worn with boots.

Cute & Comfy These pair of denims are quite good and the color looks pretty as mentioned in the product description. Fits as per size chart. Super soft & stretchy while surprisingly comfortable despite being high-rise ones. These are wide flared ones. For reference, I am 5'2" & 100 lbs, and the fit is good though I need to wear these with either heels or sneakers. Can't really wear any flats with these. Highly recommended!!

Pleasantly surprised! I purchased these in the color B-BLACK in a size XL. For sizing reference, I'm usually a medium-large or a 8-12/29-31 in bottoms and jeans. If it helps, I wear a size 6 or 8 in Lulu leggings. I'm 5'3" tall with a short torso, and my measurements are about 44"-33"-44" (bust-waist-hip) right now, give or take. I'd say they run a tad small if you have bigger hips/butt. I sized up because I was concerned about them being too tight, and I'm glad I did! I could do a large but I really like the looseness of the XL, as it's comfy but still flattering, and with a bodysuit, the fit is perfect in the waist (this one is from Pumiey, btw; I have a review of it as well). They are loose in the tummy area without being too baggy. I'm glad the pleats are super subtle. This style does not have the elastic back, as I don't love that, but I do wish they had some little fake slit pockets in the back, like other slacks do. I'm a little on the shorter side and had some extra length at the bottom, even though my legs are longer in proportion to my body, so I could wear a pair of heels and still be fine, but I don't often wear anything but flats, so I'll probably end up getting them hemmed by an inch or two. The only small complaint I can make is that I wish the button was darker, as I feel the tan one cheapens the look a bit. The material is a bit hard to describe, but I'd say it's pretty standard Amazon loose slack material, if you've tried others. It's thin and a little slick, but not very stretchy, if that makes sense. They feel kind of cool on the inside and they move nicely. I couldn't tell you how well these will hold up in the long run, but I'll try to update after I've worn and washed a handful of times. Overall, I'd definitely recommend these, but maybe size up if you have bigger hips/butt. I've been looking for some cheap slacks to try out because I haven't tried to dress nicely for the office in so long, not since I gained weight years ago, and these are a nice intro back into it. I actually purchased this exact style in B-BEIGE in the same XL size, so I'll post pics of those. I may grab the C style next, since they have tonal buttons and slit pockets in the back, even if they have elastic in the waistband too.

Roomy, comfortable and still looks good in the office These feel very thin yet even though I got a light color, you still can't see through them. Perfect summer work pant. Inconsistent Size Chart I’m 5’7”, 129 lbs, 29” waist, 38” at the widest part of my booty. Size charts always seem to be so misleading. I can never rely on them. I read reviews trying to figure out what size to order. Several reviews said the size chart was pretty accurate, which meant I would have needed to order a large. I’m usually a small, but in considering the reviews I ordered a large and a medium and figured I’d return whichever didn’t fit. I ended up ordering a small after they arrive because the medium was too big. In the photos, I’m wearing a small, and you can see the pants could easily fit someone 2-3 inches wider than I am. They stretch big time. I’ll probably keep the small but not sure because the length is right at where I would want it to be and after washing/drying, it seems like this fabric would shrink quite a bit and then be too short. Either way, I would order a size down or at the very least your regular size when ordering these.

Pleasant Surprise for Midsize person I am 5’8”, approx 170lbs with a 30” waist and 40” hips. I struggle to find pants - jeans specifically - that fit me how I like, and usually don’t have a ton of luck online. I took a chance on these and I’m so glad. Are they perfect? No - I wish the zipper was about 2” longer because when I first went to put them on I thought there was no way they were fitting, but once they were up they fit my waist and have the perfect amount of room through my hips and thighs that I have been looking for. They check a lot of boxes for me, and are even ever-so-slightly long which I never find anymore, and is one of my favorite things to have in loose jeans. I ordered my usual size, 12, again being skeptical, but it’s just right. They fit to my waist without digging in - I had abdominal surgery a year ago and now have a belly pooch that makes most jeans uncomfortable - but I can wear these all day. Also I haven’t had to touch it, but I love that they have the adjustable strap at the waist in back to get just the right fit.

Vivid, comfortable & stretchy These pants have such a vivid color. The green is very solid green. I feel it’s almost not true to the photos in the description but pretty close. They are stretchier than I thought they’d be and fit well. I am 5’ 5” and about 186 lbs. I hover between a 12-14 depending on the brand and the day. I went with a 14, and they’re spot on. I definitely would not have gotten away with a 12. Sometimes 14s tend to be long on me, but these are perfect bootcut length for my proportions. The rise is like a high mid. I carry my weight in my legs and belly, and while they don’t have tummy control I still feel they are pretty flattering. They are also on the thinner side which avoids feeling bulky like corduroy tends to, but it’s not the choice if you’re looking for something super warm. I would buy these in more colors and likely will, soon.

VERY flattering and soft/comfortable It's true about fashion. Wait long enough and it comes back in style. Flared bottoms and corduroy fabric, I'm back in Jr. high. But these pants, omg, they are NOT the pants from back then. The fabric is solid corduroy, not striped, super soft, and made from cotton and spandex like jeans these days so they have give and stretch. As soon as I opened the package I could tell I was in love. They hug your hips, high waisted, cute jean type buttons for your fly (yes no zipper but adds to the charm) 4 pockets, slimmer thigh to the knee, then flare out to the bottom. I walked into the living room for my husband to see and his eyes got big and said NICE! and now you're in style! (head slap for that).A couple of things. Normally I wear 8, sometimes 10 if no stretch or give. According to their size chart, because I have a 40" hip I went with their size 12. I should have paid more attention to the fabric because upon first try, no problem but slightly bigger than my body, a little sag in the crotch and from the pic you can see touches the ground. If you're wearing high heels or wedge, that's perfect. I could have easily gone to a 10. I decided to wash them last night, alone because they were new and black, on cool water wash, then dried on high. They came out slightly shrunk, which I hoped they would, and then tried them on again. I didn't take pics, but they still were easy to put on, hugged my hips, no crotch sag, waist a little more fitting and the length does not touch the floor anymore, just about less than 1/2" off the floor. I feel like I made them more like a size 10. So give some consideration that these are stretchy and either order your normal size and watch how you wash, or maybe try a size up and see how they fit, possibly knowing you can make them a little smaller in the wash. I did not notice any lint or fading after I washed them.I would say these pants are quality made, cute, cute, cute, very soft and comfortable to wear and will flatter just about any body type with it's cut. You do have to button them up. I'm glad the style is coming back so all shapes can look good, which to me means flattering to your body type. I would say the price to me is on the high side. I've always been thrifty and look for bargains or discounts, especially on a Sr fixed income. If you're willing to pay this for 1 pair of pants, go for it! They will not disappoint.

They are extremely comfortable and very well-made. The corduroy is excellent. My only issue is that I am only 4 foot 11 and I have a rather large pouch of fat in my lower stomach area (fupa) And I noticed that these pants seem to sort of accentuate that quite a bit I’m not sure if I wear some extra tight shape wear. Kinda adorable I don’t think I’ve worn corduroy pants since middle school, but I’m loving these. They are so, so cute on and I love the tapered leg and the cuff at the bottom. I’m 5’9” and around 150 lbs, and wear size medium (6-8), and according to their sizing, a medium is 8-10. However, the waist looked like it might be a little tight and I’m adverse to anything being tight there, so I sized up to a large. Love these! Super cute and comfortable! Material is thin, lightweight and super soft! Runs true to size chart, I ordered a size small. Stylish trousers for work I'm very happy with these pants. The trouser style is great for the office. The elastic waistband is super comfortable, but the pants still have a tailored and professional look. They look sharp with a belt and a fitted shirt. The material is an interesting waffle knit. I wasn't sure I'd like it, but I do. It has a nice weight, and I'll be able to wear these even in the colder months. The pockets are nice and deep. I'm typically a size 14 pant; I ordered these in Large and they fit great. I'm 5'6" and these hit just below my ankle. I can still wear them with flats without them dragging on the ground. I've gotten lots of compliments on them, and a couple of women at work have asked me where I got them. Great business casual pants that I'll get plenty of wears out of!

Peekaboo... Everyone Can See You The fit and comfort were definitely on point; loose and flowy. However, they did not look frumpy. I usually size up for length, and I am not sure that was necessary. As a 5'9" girl, this was huge. Too bad I had to return them to the seller. Unfortunately the low rating is all about transparency. In the image attached to this review, the silhouette of my hand is on the right and the rectangle retail tag to the right of the drawstring. They were visible in indirect, natural light standing in my office. I kept the army green pair purchased in the same order, because the transparency was less evident.

Silky, comfortable, flattering. Shorter than expected length These silky high waisted palazzo pants are a timeless classic. The fabric is a silky feeling synthetic material that drapes well, and they are very flattering. When taken straight out of the box, they were a little wrinkled but that is to be expected. With a few minutes in the dryer they flattened right out. The waistband falls at the smallest part of my waist -these are very high waisted, and plenty long in the crotch. The pockets are not too shallow, and can fit my phone without slipping out. The waist band is both elastic and drawstring, so they are comfortable, functional and adjustable. I wish they were longer though- I am 5 foot 6 and with completely flat sandals they were about two inches from the floor (and I prefer a floor length pant). I think if you are shorter than me, the pants would hit the floor or perhaps allow a higher heel if you're close to five feet tall. If you're taller than me (for example 5 foot 8 or more) then they would be more of an awesome Culottes length. I ordered a size Medium, which based on the chart fits a size 8 to 10. I am a size 10 and these fit nicely in the waist (meaning the elastic was a perfect comfortable fit and did not require additional cinching with the drawstring). Overall these are a breezy daytime pant, the type that you see on European women with strappy sandals, a form fitting body suit and a great pair of sunglasses.

LOVE these pants! Comfy, flowy, cute, & POCKETS! Did I say POCKETS??!! Real ones that fit my hands or a phone or my glasses!! POCKETS. ~~ I can see these dressed up with a blouse & heels or more casual with a simple tee & flats. Definitely recommend & I am going to order another color or print! **Thank you for reading this review & I hope that it’s !** 😊

Great dress pants, style and quality I was hesitant to purchase these pants based on mixed opinions in posted reviews and overall score. Being a Latina with curves and big thighs, it’s sometimes challenging to find the right fit. For context I wear size 10 or 12 depending on the style, material, cut, etc… I bought these in a 12 to ensure they weren’t too fitted! The material is great quality, very soft and flowy, they are roomy in the thighs, fabric not overly thin so we’re not transparent as you can see in the pics (I zoomed in on all so you can really see the details), and the cut fit very pretty. The waist has some elasticity in the back and the zipper is in the side. I’m 5’5 and length was perfect. I can wear with flats, kitten or regular 3”heels without a problem. Finally, classy pants that fit right! I plan on getting them in more colors.

Not for curvy women If you have hips, these pants are not for you. The pleats on the front are absolutely horrible. You can't tell from looking at them, but they do actually fit me ok - they just bunch horribly from the pleats. Also, the zipper on the side is terrible and catches on the fabric, making them very difficult to zip or unzip. FYI, I'm only 5'2 and these didn't feel ridiculously long on me (which most pants that aren't petite/short do), so I'm guessing they'd be pretty short on someone of average height.

Comfortable, stretches as you wear I bought two different pairs- an xs and a s. The XS was more "fitting" but then the waist was more snug than I would have liked. The S was a looser trouser look. Also, the coloration from the images isn't exact especially for the brown, it's much more "desaturated." They are stretchy and soft. Almost like fancy sweatpants. I have yet to wash, but due to their composition, I assume the will have the tendency to shrink. The pockets are deep, the quality seems alright. It's an easy to pull fabric, so gentle cycles. No issues with the zipper and button. Opaque, decent length, lightweight. I am 5'6, 128lbs and they sit 2-3 inches above my ankle - I'm pretty used to that. I'll update my review after a few wears.

I’m 5’6, ~150lbs and pear shaped. I originally got the medium but returned it for a large because I wanted the legs to be a little longer. When I washed the large pants the legs did shrink a little bit, but so long as I wear boots or high tops they don’t look like high waters. The pants are extremely comfortable. My state is currently in the high 90’s for summer and I haven’t had any problems with sweating, so I’d said the fabric is pretty breathable. I’ve seen pictures of the pants dressed down, but I wear them to work so they’re easy to dress up with a nice shirt or sweater as well. The pockets are deep for women’s pants. You will need to wear seamless underwear or the creases will show through the back, but if you wear seamless they look great from behind. I actually liked them so much I got a second pair in brown 👍🏻

Pretty good First off, the waist is tight on these — I’m usually a size 6 and I ordered the small and right now they’re too tight to comfortably wear. Also, I am 5’1” and the jogger bottom ends up making these look a bit clowny on me with an inflated bottom. All that is a shame though, because otherwise I really like the pants. They’re cute and look dressy while still comfortable.

WOW, these are pretty good!! I am really surprised at how good these are! They fit me (almost) perfectly and they look super cute! I have them on straight out of the package in the pics so they are holding some wrinkles still, but overall, excellent pants and I will be ordering in more colors! Some thoughts below. - Fit is true to size with some flexibility. I am 5'7" and 145lbs and holding on to some extra holiday weight! I ordered my true size small and they are slightly tight but its only noticeable at the pleats since they are not laying flat!! Otherwise, these fit perfectly! The back of the waist has elastic which makes these flexible in terms of sizing. I would say order your true size. - The material is not the softest but its not uncomfortable either. I am in Michigan and they don't have a ton of warmth so not sure these will see much wear until it gets back into the 40's or 50s around here. - I LOVE the jogger look for a business outfit. It adds some fun to the outfit and they are flattering! They could easily be worn with sneakers for a more casual but chic look too! - Love that they have pockets and belt loops! Overall, these are shockingly so good. I will be buying in more colors. True to size.

Love The color and design of these pants are great. They are made well. The material is good not thin and has a softer texture to them. They fit true to size. I like that I can wear these with heels to dress up or tennis shoes to wear casually. I picked these up for our Europe trip. They do have some stretch in the waste for a little flex and just a slight bagginess for comfort. I got the large I'm 170 at 5'7"

Love these! Super comfy pants that are extremely versatile. They are loose/more relaxed fit, but aren't baggy looking on me which is exactly what I was looking for. For context, I bought the size small (I'm 5'7" and about 125lbs). I think these would probably be more flattering and comfortable on people who have a waist smaller than their hips. These pants could totally be worn with sneakers or dressy shoes...I wore them to work today with sneakers and a blazer and I felt super comfortable and put together. I would have given these five stars except that the type of material on the outside seems to be picking up every fluffy thing they touch...maybe a lighter colour would be better for that but on the black it shows, so if you buy the black, have your lint roller handy! Overall, I just love these pants and am seriously considering purchasing a second pair in a different colour!

Not office wear, but great anyway… I bought this size Medium in 2 colors - I am 5’6” and 150 pounds and the fit is perfect. I bought them in hopes to “dress them up” as I’ve seen other reviewers mention, but I definitely won’t be able to wear them in my office setting workplace. The side seams (see pic) look like leggings/athletic wear which unfortunately won’t work for me at the office, but I will definitely get use out of these! They are a high rise (almost too high for me) with a loose fit thru the legs. The flare is gradual and flattering, and they hit right at the top of my foot (almost too short with shoes). They don’t do anything for my fluffy middle section, but I usually wear a longer top to cover that. It washes well - I did not dry it but the tag says dry on low. I got the taupe and cameo colors, and they are both nice. They are a comfortable addition to my athleisure wardrobe which seems to be growing lately!

These pants are mid-high waisted, and they stay in place really well. I can move freely in these pants and they don't move around, I can do a full squat and the waistband doesn't roll down. They're fitted through the waist and top of my bum, and then they have a loose fit through the lower bum and legs - very flattering, VERY comfortable. They also have pockets! These pants rock - they're exactly as expected/described, they're comfortable, and I think they look nice on, whether dressed up or down.

QIANXIZHAN Women's Boho Pants I am 5'1" 105lbs for reference. First impressions, these pants are AWESOME. I have a ton of harem and boho type pants but non that are this kind of material. It is almost like a cross between jersey and bathing suit material, is pretty thick compared to similar type pants and has a ton of stretch! The pattern/colors are exactly as shown in the listing. I absolutely LOVE these pants but alas! I cannot give them 5 stars, They are very long and are also too big. The elastic on the waist band is not very tight. The bottom seem where the band meets the pants is the tightest part of the entire waist band and even that to big to stay up. The first photo is where I think and would like the pants to sit, however, the second photo is where they fall. I cannot keep them any higher. They fall down when I walk, not completely, but enough where I caught myself constantly pulling them up. The third picture is me on my tip toes. You can see how the length is pretty perfect at that height but you can see by the other photos how it sits just standing regular. Because of the length, I was also constantly stepping on the bottoms which also pulled them down as well. I am washing them now so I will see if they shrink at all. Hoping they do but I have a feeling they won't due to the material. As is, I really need an XS or XXS in these pants to wear comfortably. If the waist band was actually tight and held them up securely, I could def wear a size small out of the house. But, as is, I can't see myself being able to wear these pants outside due to the constant falling down. So if you are more petite like me, I would pass on these unless they add smaller sizes.

So comfortable and very cool print! These harem pants are 95% polyester 5% spandex and so comfortable!! I am 5ft2in, 191 lbs, 40in waist, 47in hip. I usually order an XL in pants and so that’s what I ordered. I am happy with the XL fit. The waist elastic is very comfortable. They are a little long even with 3in heels on, so I will take them up a couple of inches and they won’t drag the floor. They will be fine for taller gals. They have pockets on the hips that lay flat and don’t stick out making your hips look larger. I washed them in cool water with like colors and tossed them in the dryer on medium heat. No color run and they came out without wrinkles. I ordered the Orangeflower print and there are plenty of colors in that print to choose from to coordinate whether I wear them in the fall or in the spring. I love them! So groovy!

Fit, fabric and pattern all don’t match the advertisement Purchased based on the photos, the green tie dye print (it isn’t actual tie dye) was not matched up right. So there was a litter colored blob above the belly. Real issues are with the fabric. Stretch fabric on the 2XL was clingy not flowy like the photos. It was almost like it had static but it didn’t. The sizing was off. 2XL was almost smaller than the XL. Both fit way too close to the body. I am a size 14 and these fit like a size Large. The inseam is very small if you wear these pants as they should be- with the gathered elastic up high. If I pulled them lower to get the length right, there was sagging in the crotch and in the back. Not flattering. Seller should get better fabric, and make sure of consistency with sizes and fabrics. I am almost 5’9” but these don’t fit well for me when worn as intended. NOT recommended for tall women.

Decent fit but colors ran horrifically in the wash and even stained later loads These fit nicely, but first I want to make sure anyone reading this is aware of what an ordeal it was to wash and clean up after these pants, as the dye ran worse than anything I have ever seen in my life. Full disclosure: I am not 100% sure who is the culprit, as I washed both this pair of pants and a new pair of the Rafaella lightweight satin twill ankle dress pants (also in black) at the same time. I wouldn't have noticed how horrifically they color ran due to the them both being black, except that I had also tossed a brand new light pink bra in a white mesh delicates bag in at the same time - that one's on me, I was just thinking "wash the new stuff before wearing" and not about color bleeding. I used Woolite detergent, and when I pulled the previously white delicates bag out, it was the blue/purple color of ballpoint pen ink (fortunately the bra inside was just a slightly more mauve-y dusty pink, not completely dyed). I soaked the bag and bra, and have gotten some of the dye out, and I washed the pants through two more cycles. Out of an abundance of caution, I ran the washer again in case dye was left in the machine, and put in an old white cleaning rag as a test. It came out purple. I ran it multiple more times with a new white cleaning rag each time, and they eventually got lighter and lighter purple. I ran a load of Tide Laundry Machine cleaner through, and then bought a box of Shout Color Catcher sheets to run through, and after maybe a dozen wash cycles across two days, I'm finally confident I've gotten all the residual dye out and have run a load of regular laundry through with no problems. I'm nervous to wash either pair of pants again, as I'm not sure if I'll have to go through this whole process again or if I've gotten out all the dye that's going to bleed. I've included pictures of my purple rag progression (the mesh bag is also in the photo, but that's after soaking and running it through the wash again), as well as the tags from both pairs of pants where you can see that they are stained purple as well (although it has lightened significantly from the first wash). I'm actually rather glad I had the bra/mesh bag in that same load; even though they got stained, I would have otherwise just tossed a load of regular clothes in next and ruined nearly my entire wardrobe. I left this same warning and photos on a review for the other pants' page as well. But let's talk about the fit of the actual pants! These are very nice, but not perfect pants. I really struggle to find pants that fit my hips and thighs but don't have a horrible gap at the waist - I usually end up cinching it in paper bag style with a belt. For reference, I am 5 foot 3 and ordered a size 14 curvy (sized up from the suggested sizing chart, which would have put me in a 12: 32 waist, 43 hips). These pants fit and flattered my legs nicely, and the waist is a bit loose but manageable. I'll probably move the buttons/hooks over a bit to help. They are quite long on me and will need hemmed, even to wear with heels, but I expected that. I wish the material was a bit thicker - I would describe it as almost midway between dress pant material and tee shirt material. In black, this isn't as much of an issue, but in a lighter color I think it would be somewhat clingy and probably showing off bumps and dimples in ways that I wouldn't like. Overall, I'm satisfied with the pants, but I don't think they were worth the laundry debacle.

Flattering relaxed cut These pants are very nice for curvy girls. I have a 31" waist and 41" hips. The cut combined with the stretch is very flattering in the size 10P for me. I think that because these are cut for this specific body shape and with the stretch in the fabric, I could have sized down (normally I size up for my hips). There is a bit of excess fabric at the hips but with a bit of ironing they relax and look great with plenty of room to move. Even though they're a bit big, I love the way that they drape, looking wide and relaxed with a gently graduating boot cut. The finish is nicely tailored and professional. The length is great and measures 29" at the inseam: flats or heels appropriate for me at 5'2". I would still recommend to size down if you're in between. With them being sold as a gabardine trouser, I was expecting them to be a bit more of a classic suiting material. It still has a nice weave to the fabric so I'm pleased with the finish. In all, these Rafaella pants are good quality and excellent value and my bum feels great in them!

Perfect little pocket, unisex, good fit, durable, phone friendly pockets, looks sharp I ordered the bright green for the color. These are true unisex. I'm a 75 year old man. I could find essentially the same cargos marketed for men. Button and zipper on same side. The men's version don't come in good colors. I like these cause elastic in waist so don't need a belt. They come in my length 29". I don't need to alter. After I wore a few time: Very comfortable, my huge cell phone in wallet case will fit in the 4 front pockets, also good for glasses case. I like the buttons on the cargo pockets. I keep the inner one buttoned and my phone does not fall out. Much better than velcro. Don't use rear or calf pockets. No more fanny pack. After a few weeks I bought the camouflaged for working around the house. Don't want to stain the green. I do a lot of DIY. Sitting on the ground I appreciate the room in the seat and crotch. Sometimes my pants will get wet like washing cars with spray hose. That's when I discovered my key fob with house keys fit easily in the little zipper pocket. NOT JUST A ORNAMENTAL DECORATION Very functional. The pants do well in the wash. The green has not faded. Just shake them out when taken out of the dryer hot. Looks good for going to doctor or market. My wife irons the green if I going to wear them to church. Now I'm looking at the women's dress/business trousers. Their almost identical to the pleated suit pants I wore 30 years ago. Just looking for the right fabric. I would like tropical wool.
