Love these pants! I have big time love for these pants, I have three different pairs: green camo, black, and the cow print ones. I have a size 6 in the green camo and the cow ones, and a size 4 in the black ones. The fit isn't super consistent, which would be my only complaint about these pants, because the two pairs I have that are 6's fit me differently. The waistband has some elastic in it, so they're stretchy enough to be super comfy without that elastic being worn out and too loose by the end of the day. I can wear these for 14+ hours and still be just as comfortable as anything, I've worn them to travel in, and I work in them constantly. The pockets!! There are 9 of them, all completely functional and very spaciously sized. Like the front pockets are deep enough to get my whole hand down to my forearm in them. I've never used the little pockets towards the bottom of the pants, but I like the way they look anyway. The side pockets are big enough for a ten inch tablet to easily slide into them, and the bottoms of the pants have ties on them so you can wear them loose or tie them and wear them more like a pair of joggers. I think they're versatile enough that they can be cute, or they can just be your new favorite work pants. I. Love. These. Pants.

Great Pants, but some Caveats I've owned two different lengths of these pants, and have learned some things you'll want to know before you buy. First of all, the material on these is great. They feel like professional grade pants, unlike most pull on pants of this style that are made with a cottony material, that while comfy, is also a lot like sweatpants in a different cut, and just feel very casual to me for going to the office. This material still has a lot of stretch to it, and the strength of the material plus the high waist keeps me "tucked in" around the middle, and not in an uncomfortable way. These pants do not have real pockets, so if you want those, move along. These are slim fit, so pockets would create lumps and bumps, so I'm okay letting that go for these. The biggest unknown when it comes to these pants is the length. The Regular size is listed as 32" and the Tall size is listed as 35". I am 5' 8 1/2" tall, and have issues with pants being too short, but long ones being too long. The Regular size look fine if I'm in flats and standing, but the stretchy nature of these pants means that when you sit, they move quite a long ways up your leg. That's why I included a picture of me with my leg up in the air like a stork. For the Tall length pants, 35" in any other pant would be WAY too long for me, and they are definitely long when standing, I have to have some heels on (though you can see in the picture, the heels don't have to be crazy tall). But when I sit in the Tall pants, they are the right length. For me, sitting at my desk at work in the Regular size pants, the short length drove me batty. My socks poke out, and they are super high water, so I'd rather have the long size and wear some heels with them. This is my personal preference. Also, they are quite slim fit, I am wearing a size 8 and normally I wear a size 6 in pants. I am 135 lbs. I hope this review helps you with these pants. I have several pairs now that I've dialed down the sizing I like.

Stylish & practical I love the cut and style of these stretchy pants! I bought a size up due to the potential for shrinkage if someone else did the laundry. I also didn't want a "muffin top" created by a tight waist band. They fit great--and if they shrank--they'd still fit. They have a wide waist band with belt loops. Sit nicely at at the hip (I'm short waisted).All the pockets are fake, but nicely detailed. I am 5'4", weigh about 145-150, and I ordered a size 12. Yes, I could've ordered a 10 and feel confident they would fit even better, but I can't risk the potential for shrinkage and muffin top. They were long, but I just folded them under the exact width of the existing hem and hemmed them myself. I wash in cold water and hang dry. The ONLY drawback (but worth it in my opinion) is they do need to be ironed on LOW (synthetic). If it's not a low heat, you risk melting your pants. The back pockets look puckered after washing, but once you iron them and wear them, you don't see the puckers. Also, the pants stretch back into form, meaning they don't stretch out and look saggy. These pants are light weight, perfect for warm/hot climates. Very slimming. Who doesn't like that?

These are my new go-to dress slacks! This is my first shot at trying the yoga pant style work slacks and OH MY STARS THESE ARE INCREDIBLE. I am 5'8, 160 lbs and typically wear size 10 American eagle jeans. The sizing chart dictated to order a size 12. I ordered four different pair of slacks from Rekucci. Amazon accidentally sent one pair in size 8 instead of 12 but I tried them on anyways. Even they fit, but were just a smidge too snug to be professional. These pants go on so easy. They move with you. They don't fall off. They just fit really well. I was afraid of what the material would be but, again, I'm impressed. They look just like a pair of dress slacks, not like a pair of yoga pants or pajamas. Rekucci comfort fit pants will definitely be my new go to for dress slacks. You just can't beat this kind of quality, especially for, what, $33?

Nice dress slacks Update: So I purchsed a 14 regular and a 14 long hoping those would give me the shrinkage room i need to get a nice 12. Huh! Jokes on me. The 14L, although much longer in length, fit a smidge SMALLER than the 12 and are tight in the knee! The 14R fit about the SAME as the 12 with this kicker-they about 2 inches shorter!!! So no shrinkage room. Maybe the 16?? I'll try it but do i the regular or the tall? Hmm, Can't win. Too bad as they are nice work slacks.Edit: they shrink, in and up, a lot. I lost probably 2 inches in both directions. Don't let the polyester feel of these slacks fool you. And read the washing instructions. And they still wrinkle. A lot. Which is too bad because they are a great work pant!!Slacks fit great, very comfortable. I did size up 1 size (normally a 12, bought a 14). A little snug in thigh but not uncomfortablely so. I'm 5'7 and hem sits just above my boot sole. Difficult to describe the material....maybe a little too polyester-ish and they wrinkle a LOT after one wear. Overall, a nice pair of slacks and i would buy again.

Fell in love I didn’t like them much when I first tried them on. I felt they were way too long. I’m in the over 50 age range, I’m 5’1” I think my inseam is 27 (or 28”) and about 200 lbs. I bought a 16 Short. They seemed to have snugged up after the cold water wash. I did put them in the dryer with the other laundry. I lack the desire (read “patience”, lol) to hang up clothes to dry. I probably should have went up a size, but I’ve lost 30lbs and am working still working on it. I also dislike it when pants become baggy/saggy during the day; and these did not. I did feel like I had to pull them up here and there through out the day, so I may wear a belt next time. The tummy control could be better, but I could also stop eating cake, so there’s that. Overall, I’m ordering another pair as we speak because I really do like how comfortable they are. I may try an 18 and return them if they’re too big. Hahaha, you see that? I made a joke! I’m not gonna stinking return anything (said in my ‘Granny’ voice) lol. I’ll just have to eat more cake so they’ll fit better.

Silver looks like Green!! I’m 5’7 145lbs and I order a size 10. They fit as expected, they are soft and look good always. I have about 4 colors and they are consistent in size and length. I took a star off because in person the silver looks like a light olive kahki green. It doesn’t look silver. I posted a picture with my leg next to a silver bowl so you can get some sense but the picture doesn’t do it justice. These pants are more comfortable than jammies, I prefer wearing them over jeans. This same company puts out another non tummy control version of these that are exactly the same except they are actually boot cut as opposed to straight leg. After ordering about 4 pairs of each, I realized this was the only difference between them. But they don’t tell you in the description or give you an option to buy straight leg vs boot cut. The boot cut is a wider leg, but the straight leg makes me legs look more slender all the way down and fit over the top of my heels too. I just wish they had an actual very light grey in size 10. They need an option to wait list until those colors come in again. The pretty light blue is also not available in my size. But otherwise I lovse these pants!

Cute, comfortable, perfect fit! I just received these and they fit perfect! I usually wear DressBarn secret agent straight leg pants in 2 petite. Stretchy, soft and comfortable. These wide leg, high waisted jeans are very comfortable. They fit me well, except because of my shortness (5' 4") they are not really cropped on me. More like flood pants, but I am okay with that. I do not have to worry about the bottoms getting wet or my lower legs getting too cold, lol. They have three buttons that are quite stiff to unbutton when you get them but are fine after the first time. The zipper works well. There are four functional pockets, two in front and two in back. The pockets are a little smallish, but would hold a tissue or car keys. As you can see in one of my pictures, if you are apple shaped like I am, the white of the inside of the pocket (on the front) will show, despite the fit being not too tight. Not a problem for me as I most always like to wear shirts / blouses that are long enough to cover at least most of my bottom. Not sure how the pocket issue could have been prevented in the designing of them, but there probably is a way. These jeans seem to be mostly well made. The only thing I noticed really was that the buttons are sort of the cheap type that wiggle and rattle. Not sure what the durability would be of those. Aside from that, I can recommend these jeans and I am happy with them. The reason I only gave four stars was for the buttons and the white of the pocket showing. If anything comes up about those buttons on mine, I will update my review.

Ridiculously wide legs, inseam shorter than given on size chart I ordered these in the “Blue” color in size Medium. I typically wear a size 8 in pants, but the size chart could have had me in a Large instead. However, the listing says the model is “wearing size Medium, Hips 40 / Height 5’3” / Weight 136. I’m about an inch and half taller, weigh about the same, and hips are maybe an inch smaller. The Medium fits me very well and is comfortable. The inseam is at least an inch shorter than the stated 26.38” (I measured it at about 25”). The fabric has a nice weight and is soft and stretchy. The waistband is beautiful and also comfortable. It seems like it would retain its size and shape, however, I have not worn them long enough to test this theory. There are no belt loops to hold them up should they stretch out, or if they would be too loose on you. The buttons seem a little loose, but don’t show any indication of coming off.Now, I know these are advertised as wide leg, but they have W-I-D-E legs! Since they are denim which has structure, If I stand with my feet about hip width apart (a natural stance), the legs of these jeans cross over each other, which looks rather ridiculous. The seams go crooked and it looks like I'm not standing straight even though I am. I have to stand with my legs spread strangely apart for them to look as they do on the model. If only I could slim down the legs, these would be attractive, but as they are, I just can’t see myself ever wearing them, not even just around the house, as the legs crash together with every step. I’ve included photos of how they look on me (5’4.5”, 30 waist, 39 hip, about 29 inseam), and also some with a measuring tape to show the actual measurements of the size Medium. I hope this helps in your decision.

Love These (Very) Long and Stretchy Jeans! These jeans are super cute! They’re also super long. In my image I’m wearing these with almost 3” heeled platform clogs. But I love the look - I think it’s super slimming. I’m 5’5,” 165lbs, waist is 35,” hips are 44” and my thighs are 23” around. I’m also very short-waisted. Per the size guide I ordered the XL. Because these are SUPER stretchy, I’m wondering if I could’ve gone down a size. I can literally pull these on with unzipping them. However, I don’t mind that at all. It’s also difficult to find high waisted jeans that are flattering on me because I’m so short waisted. I really like the rise on these. I’ll see how these do after I wear them for a day, out and about and also after washing. If they don’t stretch out too much or shrink, I’ll stick with this size. If anything changes, I’ll update my review. For now, I’m very happy and I do recommend these jeans. I’m almost 60 yrs old, but I think these would appeal to a broad range of ages.

So stretchy and comfortable These jeans are beyond comfortable. They are very stretchy yet not thin or cheap feeling at all! The stitching is very well done. I love the three buttons and thick band at waistline. They fit well with no gaping at the waist. Bottom opening is very wide and length was perfect. I always expect jeans to be crazy long as I am only 5'3" but these are the perfect length to pair with my boots. I was pleasantly surprised with how deep tge front pockets are as most women's jeans have pretty useless front pockets. The ONLY complaint I had was with the ay the denim portion is cut on the front pockets. I noticed that the denim is cut very close to pocket opening so that you can actually see the stitching where denim meets pocket liner. Not a deal breaker, but I felt myself constantly readjusting that pocket (see last photo).
Love these jeans, they fit perfectly with enough stretch to stay comfortable yet not get baggy they are about a 30” inseam so the length is bang on and I LOVE the wide leg!!

Not flattering I feel like a frumpy old lady wearing these pants. They run large. Almost perfect These pants are a near dupe of the old navy mid rise pixie full length pant. Great length (I'm 5'7), and four REAL (albiet small) POCKETS! I say nearly perfect because there are some minor flaws. They are not quite as stretchy and comfortable as I had hoped. They are slightly too high in the front which makes them bunch in an unflattering way when seated. They are slightly too low in the back so when you sit there is definite whale tale potential if you're not careful (might not be an issue for those with smaller butts). That said, they are better than 90% of dress pants I have tried, and cheaper than nearly 100%. If they don't fade or wear out, I'll probably buy more colors.

Better than I expected I was pleasantly impressed with these pants. I am 5'6", 29" waist, 40" (39") hips, 136lbs. These feel a bit like parachute pants. They are 17% nylon after all. But they do have a good amount of stretch to them. I wish they were a little more supportive in the rear and that might have been achievable maybe one size down if I thought they were really stretchy enough for that sort of feature but that's just wishful thinking for a forty-something y/o whose assets aren't where they used to be. The waistband is very comfortable, the length is just where I want it, and I think they look really good on my body. As for my measurements, I am 5'6" but I have short-ish legs and a long torso - specifically my "rise". Like, high-rise pants fit me more like mid-rise because there is over eleven inches between my crotch and my bely button. I mention this because these pants are a little tight in the crotch for me and have a little camel toe effect but the waist is so forgiving, I can adjust and pull them down whenever I feel them creeping up in there;) Plus they are black and it's not noticeable. I don't think that would be the case with lighter colors though which is a bummer because I like these pants so much I'm tempted to buy them in all the colors. Anyway, lots of reviews complaining about how they don't hold up to washing but it clearly states that these pants are best dry cleaned. I never dry clean anything so I'll probably wash on cold in the gentle or hand wash cycle and hang to dry. I included front (heeled and flat footed), back, and side pics because I know that even someone with the same measurements as me may have a different shape. Hope this info helps.

Cute warmer weather slacks These pants fit fairly nicely. I wear a US 8 in jeans, and the waist is not snug (I received a M). I’m not a huge fan of the elastic band at the waist, but it isn’t too tight. The inseam is long enough for me. I’m 5’4”, but have longer legs. The crotch area leaves a little to be desired. I don’t have a butt, but if I did these pants would be riding up in places that I don’t want them riding up. The material is flowy, comfortable, and not see through, but I don’t feel that these would be as cool as lighter weight materials in the high heat of the middle of summer. They are a cotton/linen blend (70/30) so maybe they might be okay in the high heat? I just don’t know that answer for sure. I feel that since they are slightly thicker than what I was expecting, that they would be great from very early spring through mid fall - depending on the climate where you live. They are thick enough for me to wear in temperatures in the 60°F (15°C). They are well made. I love that they have pockets, and the color is perfect. It’s like a mix of denim and teal. Overall, I would recommend, but if you have wider hips, a butt, or thicker thighs, there might be an issue in the crotch area. Check other reviews and find people who are similar in body type to see how it works for them.

Serviceable but Don't Love It I feel that this runs a little large in comparison to the size chart. I just ordered my usual size instead, M, and overall the fit is ok and matches up to a typical M, I am just a bit too short & chubby for these to fit right. I am 5'3", 38-33-38 and the hem is about 3 inches too long, rise is too long for my taste (definitely a high waist on me when I would have preferred mid-rise). This is a very fine-boned corduroy fabric (see pic) and it does not stretch a lot apart from the elastic waist, so it's more like real pants than stretchy leggings. You may possibly want to order up and think of it more like baggy comfy pants? Or it may fit better on someone with a trim body. These won't fit well if you have large thighs (mine are 20" and I feel that they're about the max) or bootie, I think they will drag down on you. Overall these will probably work the best on someone with a 30-32" inseam & a slim-rectangle kind of body type. Looking at other reviewers that seems to match up with who was more happy with these pants. Really nice color selection (I was so tempted by both the greens but am surprisingly lacking in plain black pants) & they look good but they're a bit ill-fitting for me on comfort.

Tall plus Girl Okay so first off I didn’t pay for expedited and it still came the next day! When it says earliest delivery date, most times Amazon pushes through and it’s COVID-19 so I’m shocked. I am 6 foot I weigh 280 pounds and I’m thick thighs no butt. For the thick thighs women with no butt is best to get your exact size because we want to also flatter our butts just as much as are thighs. If your thighs are bigger than your butt or vice versa to an extreme amount probably get one size bigger but it may be a little loose. I like tighter fit jeans in my thigh area to complement my hips and thighs also because I have a small butt once again lol! And you also know the more you wear jeans they stretch. So I know that my thighs will start to make room for themselves if I need any more LOL.These are perfect. They stretch but you can tell There’s some denim in it because it doesn’t have an extreme stretch. These are not jeggings! They are the regular polyester plus size material for big girls I just have jeans are being right now with a little stretch but I still denim feel! I don’t mind that lip space under the abdomen because I know my thighs are the reason why! Also it goes all the way down ! No high water !!!!

Almost perfect I like how stretchy the material is. Thick enough. I love the flare as well. For your reference: I am 5'2", US Medium. 160 lbs. Somethings can be improved in the design. Adding some side pockets would be really nice because it would really make it look like real pants instead of jeggings with elastic waistband. I kind of like to tuck my shirt in my pockets. The waistband is a bit too tight. These are high-waist pants and it being so tight around the waist is restricting because I have GERD it messes up my stomach because it pushes my stomach too much. For that reason I can't wear it as much as I want to. If the waist band is at least 2 inches loose, these would be comfortable. I like that the hemline is raw so I was able to cut these to match my height and not worry about sewing. Because I am short, the hole is not hitting me at the knees, but it doesn't bother me. The only thing I really would say are not very good at not having pockets and the waistline being super tight. Other than that, I would have give these 5 stars because they are cute! If you are tall and skinny, you would love these!

Fits perfect 😍 Perfect - the stretch, length, material, EVERYTHING… except the knee holes I have to fix and make a tad wider. I’d relate them to leggings but a little more tighter. Short, not capris length At 5’7”tall these are more like clam digger length on me rather than capris length. I’m also not in love with the fit because while the waist fits me perfectly (for reference 127 lbs 27” waist ordered size 4) there’s some rather odd bunching going on in the hip, leg and butt area that’s not shown in the model photos and the fit is somewhat a little tighter in the calf area. Material wise, I got the dark blue and although the material is pretty nice, not too thin or too thick, I’m not really liking the color too much, makes them look kind of cheap and again, doesn’t look like the same color as shown on the model. The description also lists these as mid rise but they are actually high rise and hit me just slightly below my belly button, not necessarily a bad thing if you prefer high rise but if you’re looking for mid rise, keep looking. What I liked, good stretch (no need to size up - if anything size down one, especially if your measurements fall in between the sizes noted in the sizing guide) , nice deep front pockets and they are comfortable and seem pretty durable. Bottom line: 3 stars mainly because the listing description and seller photos are somewhat misleading. These jeans are just okay. I’ll still wear from time to time but for casual outings and outdoor activities only.

I bought these jeans for my kids. They like trendy clothes, they wear jeans with holes in them, and I thought these are a shoe in. The jeans arrived yesterday, and surprisingly my girls weren’t too interested. I think because it’s summer and they’re thinking shorts and mini skirts rather than jeans. Big win for me! I cannot believe how amazing I look in these jeans. Now, I’m not the type of person to compliment myself pretty much ever. I’ll be the first person to point out my wrinkles, jowls, saggy bat wing arms, and lumpy cellulite allll over. But these jeans make me look like a movie star. I don’t know what kind of magic was sewn into the fabric, but they’re comfy with just the right amount of stretch, they stay on all day… So they’re stretchy, but they snap back where they need to, they aren’t low rise, but they don’t go up to my chest. I’m telling you, these are perfect. I think the only people who might not love these jeans as much as I do are taller people. I say that only because I have a 30 inch inseam… a lot of my friends have a 32 or 33 inch inseam and these jeans would be a little bit too short for their liking. Some women prefer the ankle length look, and this would definitely work for that. These jeans are absolutely perfect on me and I’m going to be looking at some of the other colors as soon as I’m done writing this review.

5ft 4, Petite Friendly! I Moonwalked down the hallway! Oh my word it’s a size 4~~!! It’s really difficult to find a size 4 in stores and in general jeans that I’m not walking on because I’m a petite. The hems are right at the top of my feet. I’m 5’4”, Waist 26, Hip 36, and 107lbs. The elephant in the room needs to be addressed—yes the “light blue” is a little darker than in seller’s pictures. These are super soft and stretchy with a feeling that a distant relative of it could have been a Jegging! Lol I usually wear size 5. Size 5 is too big around the waist but it’s the looseness that I like in the legs. Size 4; complete opposite; perfect around the waist so they’re not falling down but tighter than my *personal* preference in the legs. Tighter around the legs is easier for me to deal with than having to hike them up constantly! They are not as heavy as my other jeans and they are not as cold when putting on. I’m very, very happy with these jeans, I skipped and moonwalked down the hallway!!

Cute Style, Flattering Cut, Comfortable Stretch, Sturdy Denim I ordered these jeans in color Blue, size 8. They fit me just the same as most name brand jeans of that size fit me. The waist is high yet well-proportioned in that it doesn't rise above my belly button like some other high-waisted pants I've purchased. I like where the hemline hits my legs. For reference, I'm 5'4". The fabric is substantial in weight and has the feel of a very comfortable, stretchy cotton denim. I've put these jeans through 5 laundry cycles using cold water and one brief drying cycle on medium heat and although they did shrink a bit, it didn't affect the fit much because the fabric is stretchy. I think the cut of these jeans is both stylish and flattering, and the wide pant legs allow lots of freedom of motion in the thigh area. There was one hiccup, however. The jeans arrived carrying a strong odor in the fabric, which to me smells like petroleum. This is the reason I took off one star. I've now put them through 5 wash cycles, one brief dryer cycle and hung them outside in the sunshine. I'd researched that baking soda, vinegar and/or lemon juice might help, so I used those in the last wash cycle and it did help to a degree.

Nice for summer. As someone who practically lives in jeans, I’m always on the lookout for something lightweight to wear when it’s warm. These cropped, gauzy pants looked like a great option, so I selected the neutral khaki pair. Here’s a list of what I liked and what I didn’t.What I liked:• The pants are a cotton/linen blend which makes a soft, lightweight fabric that keeps you cool.• They are washable on a gentle cycle in cold water. I hung them to dry, and they looked ready to wear afterwards.• The color didn’t fade.• The length was great and ended right above my ankles.What I didn’t like:• According to the manufacturer’s sizing chart and the Amazon recommendation, I selected a size large (my usual size). On me (5’6”, 185 pounds, size 12), these pants seemed to run a little small. The waist was comfortable, but they fit slightly small through the hips. As a result, I didn’t get the casual, flowy look that I was wanting.• As much as I love pockets, I don’t like the ones on these pants. They are positioned on the outermost sides of your hips. These pants probably would have looked and fit better without them.I tried these pants with both a cropped t-shirt and a long t-shirt. Because of the fit through the hips, I didn’t care for the look on me when I tried them with the cropped shirt. I preferred the look of the longer shirt over the pants. It created more of the casual, flowy look that I was hoping for. I wore these pants for a day of errands and was delighted with how comfortable they were. I was secretly hoping that the fabric would stretch out through the hip area, but it didn’t (maybe a tiny bit).Overall, these are a nice, lightweight summer pant. I might consider sizing up if you want them looser through the hip. Additionally, I’d probably remove the drawstring, so you don’t see the bulky bow underneath a longer shirt. I’m giving these pants four stars because of the minor sizing issue and the unattractive pockets.

Great color & comfort ~ Size small = fairly big + high rise Just got a compliment on these from someone in my house & she is glad to take these off my hands as they're pretty massive on me ~ Hip = 45" / Front rise 13.5" / Back Rise 18.5" I am 5'4 and pulling up to my belly button I still have 2' left hanging down in the crotch (and also must use the drawstring even to get them to stay as the elastic is also made for someone a bit larger). So it's not a fix to make them look tighter/more fitted just pulling them up unless you are taller with a wider hip. I range from xs to 4/6 all depends but these to me feel like a medium or like one of those very generous vanity sizing smalls. Also just realized the knees stretch so I get the kangaroo look like I'm bending my knees when I'm standing straight. The front panels of fabric down the legs are for some reason narrower than the back, so essentially you can see both inseams & outward seams from the front & not sure what that's doing to the silouette & wear. The wide hip takes away from the wide leg because it's almost seems like it's tapering, even if it's the same width.. there's just a whole lot more upper fabric + rise for me than I'm personally used to in a pant. Nice saturated teal just as in the ad, very drapey and linen-y to some extent (like linen washed a lot and blended with something else) There is no materials tag in the pant, only a brand tag with size & care tag on reverse of it. If you got a lot of hip, are preg or looking for a not-too restrictive short pant with a wide leg & high rise, give em a try! :)

Great color & comfort ~ Size small = fairly big + high rise Just got a compliment on these from someone in my house & she is glad to take these off my hands as they're pretty massive on me ~ Hip = 45" / Front rise 13.5" / Back Rise 18.5" I am 5'4 and pulling up to my belly button I still have 2' left hanging down in the crotch (and also must use the drawstring even to get them to stay as the elastic is also made for someone a bit larger). So it's not a fix to make them look tighter/more fitted just pulling them up unless you are taller with a wider hip. I range from xs to 4/6 all depends but these to me feel like a medium or like one of those very generous vanity sizing smalls. Also just realized the knees stretch so I get the kangaroo look like I'm bending my knees when I'm standing straight. The front panels of fabric down the legs are for some reason narrower than the back, so essentially you can see both inseams & outward seams from the front & not sure what that's doing to the silouette & wear. The wide hip takes away from the wide leg because it's almost seems like it's tapering, even if it's the same width.. there's just a whole lot more upper fabric + rise for me than I'm personally used to in a pant. Nice saturated teal just as in the ad, very drapey and linen-y to some extent (like linen washed a lot and blended with something else) There is no materials tag in the pant, only a brand tag with size & care tag on reverse of it. If you got a lot of hip, are preg or looking for a not-too restrictive short pant with a wide leg & high rise, give em a try! :)

Pretty nice linen wide leg pants! These lineny pants are made of a heavier material with a linen feel to it. They could possibly be hotter than I would like for summer but are not itchy, like most linen pants are for me. I’m 5’4”, so these are full length pants for me instead of capris, but I expected this. I would estimate the inseam length to be 28-29” in the XXL size. Their measurements of the length of the whole pant isn’t all that useful. They did fit perfectly in my normal size and are actually wide leg. These pants will be nice for spring, cool summer nights, and fall. The navy color is a bit darker than their picture and more of a truer navy color which I like better than the brighter blue in their pictures. They did arrive a bit wrinkled and folded into a small box for some odd reason. I’m hoping they will hang out, because this crinkled material won’t iron well. I can’t say yet about how they wash up but will update review if they shrink too much once it gets warm enough to wear them!

Ordered Up, but I Didn’t need to A bought a similar pair to these and they were a little snug, so I ordered up a size and they are definitely a little bit, so I would say order your usual size. These are a really pretty color and feel lovely and thin and soft. They washed and ironed up really well. They are long on me because I’m only 5ft! Definitely recommend!

Basic Linen Capri Pants These capri pants are of linen quality. I am 5'6, and they do land right where they should be in length. After washing and hanging dry these pants, I found they are a bit wrinkly but will work for casual wear. It has a basic elastic waistband with a drawstring and two side pockets. They fit as expected with very little give - no spandex in these.

Super comfy, casual pants for summer These wide leg pants are very comfortable, with an elastic waist and tie to keep them from being too loose. I am 5'8", so you can see the length in the photo. I have worn them a couple times and they are fairly wrinkle resistant. These are as comfy as sweatpants, but a lot better looking!

Super comfy, casual pants for summer These wide leg pants are very comfortable, with an elastic waist and tie to keep them from being too loose. I am 5'8", so you can see the length in the photo. I have worn them a couple times and they are fairly wrinkle resistant. These are as comfy as sweatpants, but a lot better looking!

Very nice product I bought these pants for a Caribbean vacation and they were really nice. Comfortable to wear and the tie waist helps to make sure it fits well. As you can tell, they are not cropped on me. I am 5'5 and they come right to the top of my feet, which was fine with me. I would definitely buy again. I may actually buy them in a different color.

Very stretchy material These are cute trouser style jeans, I love the front pockets, they are so stylish. The material is very stretchy so you might want to size down one. I'm 5'8" and currently around 158lb, I got the 31x31 and they are a little loose fitting, which is ok because the length is just long enough for me because these hang a little lower on my hips. I love that you can choose your length, I often have a problem with pants not being long enough. I just ordered the next size down and figured I'd make it a longer length (30x33) to see if I like the fit better. I wish they had this style in a darker denim and other colors, I would get more. They are very comfortable.

PERFECT! Finally...a pair of jeans that fit perfectly and feel amazing! The Silver Jeans Co. Women's Suki Mid Rise Curvy Fit Trouser Leg Jeans are a high-end high-quality pant. They are well made with "real" denim that is heavy weight and durable. The double button and zipper fly is attractive. The fit is amazing. I don't have many curves at 5'6" and 120 lbs. Most women's size pants fit terribly so I am doomed to wearing leggings 90% of the time or juniors clothing. These jeans are the answer to my pant needs! There is a very slight stretch to the denim...ever so slight...that is a really nice plus. I will likely buy an additional pair in another color. These are fabulous! Bravo!! I am so happy to have found a pair of jeans that fit great, look fantastic, are high quality, durable, and well made.

Cute shorts I'm 5'0" / 120lb and bought a small. They're slightly tight around the waist but still pretty comfortable and I think they look cute. They do wrinkle easily, but go well with a slightly cuter top. I like the length and cut, and they're pretty lightweight and nice for summer.

Nice dress shorts These dressy shorts fit nicely, hang well. For reference, I was 5'6", 39.5" hips, these are size M. I have a thing I do that shorts need to be "fingertip length" or longer, these are right there.

Cute, wide-leg, flattering waist, thicker material pants. True to size. My teenage daughter said this style of pants is stylish right now so I'm giving them a try. For reference, I am 4'10" tall and 123lbs and I ordered a size small, which fits pretty accurately (I'll need to hem them, but that's common for me). The style is 'barrel' so they are wide-leg and with the thicker material (almost denim?) they maintain the width. For a petite frame, it kinda makes me look shorter and that's why I can't decide if I like them? That said, the pants are very comfortable. I love the high-waist with button + zipper + cinch tie. The design and pattern is cute. My daughter said she likes them on me (and she's a critic), I haven't decided yet. If you are of average height and like a wide-leg jean, this could work great for you! True to size.

Wide Leg Pants with Pockets Very lightweight and see through when you hold it up to the light. Pockets! Elastic is generously sized. You can safely get your size. You could even size down comfortably. Definitely looks more skirt like than pants like which I was hoping for. I am 5 feet tall and if wear this high (above my belly button) the hem reaches my feet.

Pretty comfortable and lightweight but wrinkles easily. I wanted a pair of casual pants with pockets to hold my phone. The fit is good but an inch or so too long on me (5’ 4”). There is a drawstring to cinch the waist but I did not know there were drawstrings around the bottom of the pants too. If you cinch the legs, the loop of stretch cord is left hanging out and looks unsightly. My solution was to tuck the loop up under the pants leg and that kept it out of sight. There is one cargo pocket on the right leg and regular pockets at the top. There are no pockets on the back of the pants. I would recommend these pants if you are looking for lightweight, casual pants with storage.

They fit nicely! These are very cute pants and they fit very nicely. I love how they look on me and how comfortable they are! They are the perfect length for me. For reference, I'm 5'6 and 160 pounds, and I ordered a size medium.

They fit nicely! These are very cute pants and they fit very nicely. I love how they look on me and how comfortable they are! They are the perfect length for me. For reference, I'm 5'6 and 160 pounds, and I ordered a size medium.

Perfect for a petite curvy-ish girl with short legs! I'm a HUGE FAN! So, I'm 5'3", 135lbs, long waisted, short legs. I ordered the 30" inseam, size M. Comfortable versatile pants These pants are very comfortable. They have pockets In the front. The fabric is light weight and perfect for summer errands, yoga or casual wear events. I have a medium. Women’s 6/8 in clothes. I’m around 5’4. The length is fine for me but for someone shorter you it maybe a little long. It also washes very well and the color does not fade. I typically let the pants airdry.

Love the fit! These pants are SO comfy and perfect to lounge at home! I love the fit and color of them! I have them in the xs in the 30” inseam and they are a little long on me but nothing too crazy! I’m 5’1” for reference!

Runs big but so cute and comfy Authentic review incoming! I hope it's . My BFF, age 16, 1999: "What are you going to wear out tonight?" Me, age 16, 1999: "Well, black pants, obviously..." I wear leggings and joggers daily as an ultramarathoner and otherwise very lazy person, but haven't ventured back into flare/wide black pants since literally high school. I gambled on these and they're soooo comfy and cute. They're high-waisted, forgiving, with a wide waistband, nice deep pockets, and I love that they're not an aggressive flare; they're actually more like wide leg pants (or at least they are on me). One thing about the sizing: I'm 5'6" and 135-140 pounds but I'm built like SpongeBob SquarePants so I tend to size up to avoid things being tight in the waist. I can be anywhere from a small to a large depending on the brand. I went with a medium on this one and was hoping it wouldn't be too tight. Umm, quite the opposite. These are big on me, even in the waist (which is no small feat, pun intended), and even though I got the 30" at 5'6", they puddle on the floor a bit as shown in the picture. I don't mind because they are comfy AF and super cute. Except the time the hem got snagged in my rowing machine. Okay, so these will be more like around-the-house pants. Better yet, they'll be my pizza pants (shout-out to Joey Tribbiani). As if I haven't dated myself enough with this review, I can't help but think that these are giving JNCO (IYKYK). I hope I haven't scared off the trendy young'uns from buying these. I promise not to wear them out in public to embarrass you if you promise to stay off my lawn!

SO soft and comfortable! I am 5'9" and it is always a problem finding pants long enough for me. I LOVE that these come in a longer inseam! That is 5 stars alone. They are super soft and super comfortable. I wore them all day yesterday. They are great for lounging around the house or even to go out and run errands as they can pass as dress pants too. I did size up and am glad I did because of how the pockets are done on the pants. I am more of an apple shape so when pockets are made this way, they tend to pull out a bit because I do not have an hourglass shape. These pants would certainly look fantastic on an hourglass figure.

Size Up One Pretty thin. Moderately stretchy. A size too small. High rise. I am 5’3”, 27.5 “ waist, 37.5” hips, normally a size medium/8 bottom. The medium is too small. Very hard to zip up. I should have sized up to a large. Cute on though.

Sooooo comfy They are so comfy. A lot of stretch and I lol l like the cover my lower belly section giving me a nice flat lift. I’m 5’4 and they cone right to my lower ankle. The color is more black in person. Photo made it look light but it’s a solid black.

Cute and Very Comfortable I am 5'8" and about 130. I am usually a small, but I followed the size chart and ordered a medium in these. When I first put them on, they were perfect and snug, but like many denim items they loosened up and ended the day a little more relaxed looking. The photo is from the end of the day. The color is also a little bit pinker than they look here. I like the look as is, but these are so stretchy I think if I wanted a tighter fit I could have gone with an S.Here is the biggest complement I can give to an internet clothing order. I opened them last night and wanted to wear them immediately. I planned today's outfit around them. I went full hello barbie and paired them with a brighter pink tee. I felt great in them! You do have to watch the fly, as with many zippered high waisted options they need to have the zipper secured properly. And, I kept trying to put my hands into front pockets that aren't there. But I adore these. For this price? Forget about it. Order them.

Impressed with the fit and quality! These jeans are quality! I got these for my niece, so I actually got them in XS, so this isn’t my size, (they are too small for me, I am 5’8/ 132), but I really wanted to show the fit, because I really like the style of the jeans and it can give you an idea of which size to get. I think their sizing chart is correct, so definitely go by that. I find them very flattering (again, this isn’t my size so it does flatten my back side, but I can tell that if I had gotten my size, they would fit very nicely). I just really like the style, and it’s hard to find a pair of jeans that is as flattering as these.

Nice length crop, light summer weight denim These are a nice light pair of summer weight denim pants. I think they're meant to be crops, but I am 5'3"and these came down to my ankles (which I prefer anyway). The material is generally quite soft but some of the seams are very rough, especially around the waist area. It's like they chose to burn the thread ends rather than tie them off. I would make sure to always tuck in my shirt with these or wear substantial undergarments that come up to the waist (at least) to protect your skin. The thread roughness aside, I like the stretch of these pants. I wish they were a bit more structured in the midsection. Since they are so very high waisted, one would hope they would provide some tummy control, but these just move and stretch with you, which makes them incredibly comfortable. The green is very light and makes for easy pairing with many colors of tops and shoes. I normally wear an 8 or 29 and these pants in a large fit me well.

Def wouldn’t call these baggy, but they’re nice I’ve been wearing these sweats in my rotation at home since August, and they’re not my top pick, but they are pretty nice. To be fair, I like a sweatpant with a really loose fit, especially in the waist. I’m pretty average sized (5’4”, 150lbs, athletic, longer torso and shorter legs), and I would not call these baggy by any means. The waist is definitely tight enough to where you can feel that it’s there; these are more the type of sweatpants that I wouldn’t feel at all uncomfortable wearing out of the house. They’ve got a cinched ankle, a cute tie in the waistband, and good pockets. If you prefer some sweats that hug your bum and won’t slide down at all, or if you are a waif queen, these will definitely be a nice option for you! They are quite soft and comfy in terms of material. As a side note, I know the selling page says to hand wash, but I’ve been tossing these in the laundry with everything else and haven’t had a problem. Like with all my drawstring pants, I tie a little bunny knot with the strings so they don’t get lost in the waistband. The price is right, and for some, these might be the perfect sweaties!

High waisted bell bottoms Black extra small xs fits me 5'2" 104lbs currently, with 25in waist(for high rise pants only). Inseam is roughly 28.5in which is why I must wear 4 inch heels with these pants. I do have long torso and short legs though. They are definitely more bell bottoms than flare pants. I never had a pair of bell bottoms before so when I first tried these pants on I wasn't sold on not being able to see my feet at all. I had to Google and found out it's normal to not see much of your shoes if you're wearing bell bottoms or wide legged pants lol As I tried more tops with these pants, I started to like them. These are truly high-waisted and have a very flattering cut. The fabric is relatively thinner but drapes very well. I don't think it would wrinkle easily. Think spring and summer pants. I tried out different tops with the pants since I never had bell bottoms before, and I found that bodysuits and crop tops probably work the best.Note there are only three belt loops in total.I ended up liking these pants a lot. Sexy and slimming and gives the illusion of longer legs. 4 stars instead of 5 because left pant leg is about 1/5 inch (5mm) longer than right pant leg and ever so slightly bigger leg opening.

I ordered a few different pairs of pants on amazon and these were one of the less visually-offensive pockets I’ve gotten.

I have smaller thighs and often pants can be baggy in that area so I ordered the xs. (grey #1) BIG mistake. Way too small. These have stretch, but not that much. Size chart didn't help. Cute but Tricky! I was very skeptical and unsure about these pants but happy I purchased them. I’m 5’7 175lbs naturally curvy and athletic build. Different material than expected but comfortable and true to size. They're cute, not my favorite but they get the job done. I am 5'6" a little over 130lbs and my thighs and booty are larger than they look in this photo and these pants are a medium.

Disappointed - Not worth the price I'm 5'5", 140lbs, little thicker in the belly/hip area and these pants accentuated all the wrong parts!! I got a Medium. There is a little stretch in the waist which is nice, but the waist does run pretty tight compared to the legs. Not a 'skinny leg' fit like the picture. Plus, the material is pretty thin and cheap looking. I paid about $35 for these pants and I would expect this kind of quality from pants half the price or less. I don't return items from Amazon often but I will be returning these. For the price I paid, I could get similar pants with a much better quality and fit. If you're on the chubbier side and looking for high waisted jegging-style pants for comfort and lower belly concealment, these are NOT for you!!

Take me back to Y2K!! I purchased these to wear to a concert: Tori Kelly's "Purple Skies" tour. The album screamed Y2K vibes and... hello, PURPLE!! The fit: The pants fit a little bit snug around my waist. I ordered a Medium, as I'm typically a medium in leggings and sweatpants. Even though they fit snug, I don't know that I would have sized up because everywhere else, the fit was just right.. any baggier I wouldn't have loved the look as much. They come just above my bellybutton, so they'd look nice with a cropped top as well. I'm 5'7" and they hit right above my ankle; however, depending on the shoe you wear, it won't look like you're about to wade through a flash flood. You can also loosen or tighten the draw string at the ankles. The color: These pants are VERY purple. Less of a royal purple color, more like a deep lilac; which I quite like (see the second pic for the true color). So, keep that in mind :) The quality: Without a doubt, these are some pretty well made pants for an amazon find. It's always hit or miss for me when shopping for clothing.. sometimes things are so thin and very see through; but, the fabric used for these pants are on par with any decently priced cargo's from a traditional retailer. I've washed and dried them with no shrinkage or color bleeding and very minimal wrinkles. All of the pockets are functional!

Fit is great for TALL and feel very comfortable. These hug the hips and waist nicely. I ordered these dressy looking trousers for my granddaughter who I figured would appreciate a nice pair of trousers or pants designed for a taller frame such as she has. It looks like Tall MobPlace did a nice job with these. She notes "The polyester fabric should be durable and easy to care for, unfortunately, the white pants are rather see-through and you can very clearly see where the pockets were put in. The material looks classy and the fit is good. They are very comfortable for fit and easy to get on. They hug the waist and hips and they’re definitely very long and probably will be worn with heels - otherwise they will need to be hemmed up shorter." We considered that since she is an excellent seamstress and could quite easily do that, however the following day she was able to wear them with some 3" heels which she has. I hadn't anticipated that at 5'11" she would have those in her closet but I was obviously out of the loop. It looks like the 34" inseam will be about right with 3” heels. If we had ordered the 32” inseam they would be worn with short heels or flats to fit her correctly. As her grandfather I suggested: "I guess you can just wear the heels and look for tall guys that match up to your higher views and expectations in life."When ordering: Just realize that when they list as a 34" length, that is what they will be. The trousers in all other respects fit and feel real well.

Perfectly Baggy Yet Slimming These cargo jeans give major 90's vibes, and I love pairing them with cropped tops for a throwback look. Honestly, I was skeptical about ordering these since finding the right fit with cargo pants is such a challenge, but these are perfectly slimming yet baggy in all the right places. The high-waisted fit is perfect, and the straight leg that gets baggier towards the bottom is exactly what I look for in cargo pants. I ordered the Medium size with a 30" inseam, and they fit perfectly. For reference, I'm a size 8, 5'6", and I weigh 150 lbs, with a 29" waist and 39" hips. I found the fit to be snug through the waist and hips, which is my personal preference. However, if you prefer a more relaxed fit or find yourself between sizes, you might consider sizing up. I love all the details on these cargo jeans. The elastic waistband is super comfortable and the pockets lie flat and don't add any extra bulk. I chose the black color, and the dark wash is really cool looking. The heavy, thick denim material feels high-quality and durable, like they'll last me for years. I'm already planning on ordering more colors because finding jeans that fit this well can be such a challenge.

Found a new favorite Me: 5’6.5” (5’7” with shoes), 135 lbs, 36-30-37, still haven’t lost the last 15 lbs of baby weight and 10 of those are at my middle; used to be a 0-2 in pants. Comfort and stretch! These pants are extremely comfortable! The material is soft and flowy. It kind of reminds me of a thin & breathable spandex material but not thin enough that they are see through, they are definitely squat proof and not see through. The V shape on the belly area is flattering and the elastic around the waist keeps them up but isn’t so tight that it’s uncomfortable or restrictive. These do stretch as you wear them so if you’re in-between sizes, I’d suggest sizing down. I washed them on a normal cycle and tumble dried on low, they came out perfectly fine. These are so comfortable that I’ll likely be living in them! You can wear them to work out in or to dress casually. I wore them today with a tank top, cardigan and sandals for school pickup and actually received a few compliments. If you’re looking for stretch & comfort, these are everything!! I ordered a size large and for reference I’m 5’5 & 190 lbs.

Pretty Color; Not Amazing I had higher hopes for this one. I’m 192 pounds and 5’6. I got a size large. What I like about it: the color is beautiful. The fabric is almost silky and super soft. It has pockets. What I don’t love about it: the fit was a bit weird. Because of the waist band, I expected it to hold in your tummy a bit but it doesn’t. The pants are not see through but the fabric was thin enough that if I pulled up the waist band to be flattering on my waist, you could see my cellulite on my thighs. If you’re smaller and don’t have cellulite, these would probably work great for you! The problem is if I would have sized down one, the waist band probably would have been more flattering but then the pants would have been too tight for my liking on my thighs. All together: for me personally: they’re okay, but not super flattering. I probably won’t be wearing them out of the house, but they’re super comfortable for at home, they’re loose on the legs and it’s a silkyish stretchy material.

Good set for lounging at home I am an apple shaped, mid-sized curvy girl, 5'5" with waist 38, Hips 46, band 35, bust 42, weight about 195lbs, Bra size 38DDD. I am right on the edge between a size large and an XL, short torso, average inseam. The size chart doesn't have a hip measurement which is what I would have used to choose a size, so I went with the large. The fabric is pretty stretchy, so they fit fine but I probably would have preferred an XL to prevent pulling at the pockets. They are pretty long on me, so I think they could work even for some of the taller girls. Overall I think this is a good set for the price.

Sizing is way off! Size 2 fits like a 4+ at least! Unfortunately, I'm disappointed in the pants; the quality is very good, but sizing (for small people) is way off and far too big! They don't make these pants in a 0, but considering how big the size 2 was I wouldn't bother trying the 0. Sadly, sizing in the US is totally askew! Cute high waisted pinstripe pants These pants are so cute on. They have a highwaisted, wide legged design w/ a pleat above each leg. They have a button closure & half of the waistband is elastic to help w the perfect fit. I’m typically an 8/10 or medium in pants, but after checking out the measurements on the size chart I decided to order a size 6 & they fit perfectly. So with that being said, I think these run a little big, but as long as you choose your size based on the seize chart measurements you should be good. The length of these run long, but they’re perfect w heels which is how I prefer to wear them anyway. Not to mention, im on the short side at 5’2, so these are tall girl friendly. The material is on the thinner side,, so unfortunately these may not be the best to wear during the winter, but I plan on getting tons of use out of them before it gets too cold. The material also doesn’t have any stretch, but these are still comfortable due to the baggier fit. I love the classic pin stripe design, but the thin white stripes don’t stand out as much as some other pinstriped designs. Regardless they still have a great look & a white top will easily help to make the pinstripes more noticeable. These seem to be pretty well made too, as I have not come across any flaws. Overall these are great pants that are both cute & comfortable & I definitely recommend them.

Coooooozy mom sweatpants These Tronjori Womens High Waisted Pull on Baggy Fleece Sweatpants Fall Winter Athletic Jogger sweats are "mom jeans" 80's style fit in the waist for sure! I generally need a size short in jeans so too much length isn't a shocker to me. Definitely recommend.

Perfect Pant I have purchased many pants of this style but returned them all until I found these. The fit is so comfortable and flattering. The fabric is nice but note that there’s no lining so it’s thin and probably not great for winter weather. They fit true to size. I’m wearing a small with a 27” waist. They aren’t long, I’m 5’5” and they just touch the ground so I’ll be wearing them with flats. I first purchased in camel and loved them so got the fiery red color too.

Perfect for a stylish shortie! I’m curvy and 5’1”. There’s almost NEVER a pair of pants I can get off the hanger without having to alter them. These pants are THEM. No shortening needed, they were the perfect length. I got the pants for a Dapper Day look, so I was walking around Disneyland all day, but they were super light and comfortable, pretty resistant to wrinkles (and sweat!) through a whole day outside. They are a little wide for a shortie, but I loved them so much I bought the pink ones too!

LOVE These trousers are amazing. Bought in 4 colors. Goes with trainers or heels. Flatter anybody type. I’m 5’9” 145lbs. Size M. Material flows as you walk with the wide leg cut. Can make any outfit have extra style without breaking the bank. I styled the navy blue color, see picture, with jean jacket and trainers. Stretching in back waist gives extra comfort. Material is not thick. Wide leg cut gives classic style to wear any season. I wouldn’t pass these up.

Heavenly These were sent down from the heavens (my local Amazon warehouse) to me, and I couldn't be more happy. I ordered an XL according to the size chart and they fit plenty loose and comfortable on my hips and thighs while also being perfect at the waist! I'm 5'7, 200 pounds, and the length is perfect. Very accurate to the measurements, very comfortable, very flowy, good weight, love that they have pockets and belt loops and again, love the fit and that they are friendly to curvier bodies.
Cute pants! These are super cute, I’m 5’4 and about 155 lbs and these were a comfy fit. Great for a slightly more dressy business casual outfit!
5’1 size small 1 month PP in these and felt amazing. I have since bought 3 pairs!

Loved It’s so so wrinkly BUT I love them. Even after ironing them out they just got rewrinkled. They’re so cute though. I love them and so did everyone else. They hit perfectly at my waist. I’m 5’7 and roughly 148lbs.

OK for petite As an elder millennial my go to pant style has been slim or skinny, so I decided to try the current looser trouser pant trend and bought these in xs short in mocha. I'm glad I did! The quality is pretty good for price (got it on prime day) and the style is nice. My only concern, which is very specific to me - it's VERY hard to shop my size as I'm 4'9" and about 95 lbs. I'm extra petite. Rarely anything fits me perfect. The wide leg and pleats do kind of overwhelm me. But... I also feel I can pull it off as long as the rest of my outfit/top is fitted to counterbalance. Just take a look at the pics and you'll see yourself. I got the short length and while it should hit my ankle its full length on me and even when I'm wearing 2.5 inch heels (again, see pic). So, while my purchase isn't a miss, it's not perfect hit either but that's cause of my size. I'm still going to qear the pants tho! :)

Get SMALL if you're 5'7 and around 119 pounds I read through all the reviews and was stuck between ordering XS and SM. I went with XS bc I read a review that says there is a give bc of the elastic in the back and that was a mistake. It was too small. There is no give at all. The elastic is more for the aesthetic. Technically it could have worked bc it "looked fine" but it felt too tight for me and was squeezing my fat to spill over. If you enjoy that feeling of constriction, then XS can work for you. I ordered SM after and it fits perfectly. I wear them high waisted and it's still pretty long but was able to wear it with tory burch flip flops without it touching the floor. Quality is eh but it IS about $35 bucks for trouser style. Pretty heavy despite the thinness. You have to wear a thong or no show, bc your panty definitely shows through. And the clasp to close your pants is sown in an area where it pulls weird and is slightly unflattering. Only concern now is how they will look after they are washed and dried... Afraid it will be super wrinkly and hard to straighten out. But as for now, I think it's worth it for $35 bucks. Better than spending $250 for decent trousers that you can only dry clean. Hope my review helped!

I am around 5’4” and the length is perfect, no altering was required. There’s elastic in the waistband so the sizing is adjustable. I fluctuate between a 24 & 26 in high waist pants and the small is an ideal fit with a little room.

Long enough at 5’8” I ordered the beige (will need to wear nude undergarments as my black undies show through in the back) in a size L and they are surprisingly long enough for me at 5’8”! They touch the top of my feet so I’ll be wearing with flats or low heels. I’m 180lbs, 33” waist and usually a size 12. I debated between getting a L or XL and am fine sticking with the L. It has enough room without squeezing & giving a muffin top. Id prefer a button instead of just the clasp closure as it can make the waistband look a little pulled. Doesn’t bother me much though. I’m concerned the pants will be staticky and much too cold in the winter as the fabric is thin and cool. All in all, a good buy.

Nice work/dressy pants! I went by the measurements and the fit is great. Maybe just a titch long (I'm 5'6", waist 35, and got XL) but there is enough room in the rise that I can wear them high at my natural waist and they don't drag on the ground. They have belt loops, nice deep pockets, and a flat hook and zip closure on the fly. There's elastic on the back of the waistband. The material is thin suiting, kind of a drapy polyester twill, it has a flat, cool, smooth surface. It drapes great, doesn't seem to wrinkle, and since it is all-polyester, it does need a good spray of Static Guard to keep it from clinging. They flow when walking and are comfortable to wear, though the surface is kind of cool and slick. The pant legs are the same width all the way down, with two pleats in the front gathering the excess fullness to the waist. The back has no pleats and is thus less voluminous, so it shows the curve of the butt a bit more. I like the fullness of these and the material won't wrinkle easily, so they'd be nice for travel.

Great pants These pants are just great. The quality, material, and fit are all excellent. I bought two different pairs, the blue and the camel. They are a little too long, even though I bought the "S Short" size. For reference, I am 4'11" and weigh 114 lbs.

Super flattering but size down! I'm 5'10 usually a size 10/30 in pants, the large was pretty big on me. Medium long was perfect, I think I may get these tailored just a smidge so they aren't dragging on the ground. 10/10

Tronjori Palazzo Pants I really read through the reviews - especially the reviews on those who were 5'10 - 5'11". I'm 5'11" and 165 lbs. I usually buy large in pants to accommodate my hips. Some suggested ordering 1 and/or 2 sizes up. I ordered 2 sizes up (olive) and found the pants to be too large. I also ordered a black pair - one size up size and the fit is fine, but I think I could have ordered a large and would have been okay. However sometimes going with the larger size you may gain a tad more in length because the fabric drapes versus fitting too snugly. The hem of the pants come right at my ankle. I would have liked them a tad longer. I may let the hem out or embellish the hem some way. Nevertheless, the black pair looks great with a pair of strappy sandals with a heel and are comfortable and classy looking.(Addition) I did add some beautiful trim (that I purchased from JoAnn Fabrics) to the black pants to lengthen them. Although some are wearing palazzo pants at the ankle, I prefer to wear mine full length. I am totally pleased with the results.

Very flattering and super comfortable The trousers are lovely. I am 5’3” 118 lbs. I usually wear anywhere from a 2-6 depending. Wanted a dress up or dress down trouser and these definitely hit the mark. The best part is if you are on the height challenged side, most wide leg trousers make you look like you’re wearing your Grandpa’s pants , too wide and definitely too long. These are a perfect length! I ordered a sm/ short. Length is perfect with flats or heels. I am curious if the x/sm short would have fit a bit more tailored at the waste so perhaps the next ones I order I’ll try the smaller size. At any rate they are amazingly comfortable and the material is very nice .

Great fit, but VERY WIDE LEG They fit great. I am 190lbs and 5'5 if you are any shorter than me you may have to hem them up a little. But when they say wide leg the mean it. Loose in the thigh and I could fit 2 whole legs in the bottom of the pant leg. I would by them again in a heart beat though. Fit well in the waist (have a heavy tummy) don't see my pooch.

TALL GIRLS SIZE UP FOR LENGTH I was apprehensive ordering because I’m 5’9 and I always have trouble finding pants that fit in the waist and go down the full length of my legs. I’m a size 28Waist/36Length so I decided to play it safe and order the large and belt if the top was too big. The length stopped right at my ankles after I wore the pants as low-rise on my hips. They got a ton of compliments however and were very comfortable to wear. So if you want the pants to fit in the waist and reach the length of your legs these might not be the best choice for tall girls, but if you’re fine wearing them low waisted they work perfectly.

Are these pants giving welcome to Jurassic Park? Yes, but am I also falling in love with the vibe also yes. I am in between sizes so I when with the XXL short because I’m 5’5 and hate my pants to drag on the ground. I wish they were just 1/2 bigger and highwaisted. Apart from that I’m loving them.

5’3/ 155lbs/ Medium-Regular I originally purchased the medium-short but they were just a little too short so I returned. I’m 5’3, short legs and long torso. 155lbs. Medium regular is great. Maybe slightly long, but I like them that way. So if I wanted to wear heels I could. So comfortable. You can dress them up or wear them casually with sneakers. Not see through at all. I also purchased the camel color. Love them both!
Very pretty Very nice pants but a little too long for me, I’m 5’1” and can’t wear heels. They were about 2” too long I could have hemmed them but won’t wear them that frequently in the red color.

BEST PANTS EVER I have these in 3 colors and I almost never buy multiples of an item. This camel color is perfect. Exactly as pictured. Same with ivory and olive green. I am 5’6”, 170ish lbs, somewhere between an 8/10, and the Medium fits great in these. I have around 44” hips and 36” waist (at the end of a meal when I just measured lol). I hope that info is . I love the length because I have an injury which only permits me to wear flat shoes, so I love that I didn’t have to get these hemmed. Granted, I wear them directly below my ribs, like Fred Mertz. TLDR; buy the pants and go for your regular size.

Perf work pants I have been looking everywhere for pants like these… Saw these on TikTok and couldn’t wait to purchase and try them on. I went with the XL short. I am 4’10 174pounds. They pass the sitdown test. But I kind of want to try a size large because these are nice and flowy. The only thing that would make this look even better is style it up with a chain belt. It says in the description that this doesn’t have stretch, but I’m someone that needs stretch in all my pants and these feel nice and comfy to me. The short fit me perfect with or without heels. Buy the pants !!

Size comparison For anyone on the fence between 2 sizes, hopefully this comparison helps. I ordered a black S and mocha M short. For reference, I’m 5’4, 126ish lbs. The black S came to my belly button and was a little tight but they have elastic in the back so there is stretch. The bottom was just barely on the ground. The mocha was about an inch below my belly button and an inch above the ground. It’s really a preference but I felt like the mocha made me feel frumpy so I’ve decided to return them and stick with the S.

Great Anthropology Dupes I went to anthropology the other day and found almost the exact same pants as these, but they were $150. I was heartbroken but I immediately went on Amazon to see if I could find anything similar and I found these! They’re perfect! I just had a baby so I have that cute lil uterus postpartum bump that isn’t always flattering in pants, but these are so comfortable and they look good even with the bump! 10/10 recommend. Can dress it up with a nice blouse, or tank and a coat. And can also go causal with a graphic tee tucked in. I’m 5’7” and 170lbs and I got a Large and they fit perfectly! Nice elastic to give some extra room!

Great product for the price! Way better than expected. The fabric has a nice weight to it. It is not see through. The color is pretty accurate from the images. I’m 5’2” and I got the short length and it exposes a small portion of my ankles. Definitely not touching the floor which is what I wanted. It flows really nicely. It is high waisted so the zippers are long, but when you sit, it doesnt bulge that much. This sits where my muffin top is and it’s pretty snug but not tight that i cant eat so you may want to size up depending on your size. Pockets are deep enough to fit an iphone 12 pro without it peaking. There are no back pockets unfortunately. Would definitely buy again.

Get them!!! Get them. I wear these all the time with everything! From the office to off duty. They give you an instant fashion boost when you put them on. I also love that they are super comfy and they don’t wrinkle easily. Pockets are always a plus. As far as I know, they are not see-through. I usually check every time I wear something dark underneath. They are a lightweight fabric. There are some defects in the sewing, but they aren’t super noticeable. I had some overlapping stitching at the bottom of the pants near the seams but you wouldn’t notice normally. I do wish the pants fastener had a button in addition to the metal slider one. I’m 5’4” and I usually prefer to wear something with a slight heel with these or they may drag on the floor.

Flattering and comfortable!! Love these pants!! I am 5,5/160 lbs and I bought a large in the camel color. They are the perfect length to wear with flats! I am hesitant on drying them because I don’t want to lose the length. They are thin and very professional looking, I received compliments on them all day! A fashionable belt is the perfect accessory for this outfit.

Almost perfect ….. Pants fit well….I ordered them in 3 different colors. I’m 5’6” and weigh 114 lbs and I ordered a size small. I’m typically a sz 4 in pants and with chunky boots I will not have to hem them. I like the elastic back. Front closure is a little “fragile” so use care when opening and closing. My only complaint is that the pockets are lined with a white fabric - not the same fabric as the pants. When you put them on you can see a thin line of white “outlining “ the edge of the pocket. This is true for the 3 colors I ordered (mocha, brown sugar and olive). I can’t speak to the other colors but I would assume it is the same.

I LOVE THESE PANTS They’re lightweight, so perfect for modest outfits in hotter seasons! I love the triangle shaped folds, which hides my mom pooch. It’s also great for us tall girlies (I’m 5’9). For pants, I’m usually a size 10-12 and an XL fits great. The only downsides is that the pant legs are super wide and almost looks blocky. And that it SUPER high waisted so the crotch part hangs pretty low. But all and all, I’m keeping it! Love the look!

These pants are EVERYTHING. Great for petites! I can’t say enough good things about these pants. I am petite (barely 5’1” and 103 pounds) and bought the camel color in XS short. They are so classy and make me feel like Katharine Hepburn. The material is luxurious and they hang perfectly. I love them so much that I bought them in the “XS short” size in ivory and in “XS” regular length in yellow and army green. The XS short is the perfect length for flats and sandals and the regular length is great with heels. They are pretty wide, so if that’s not your thing, you may want to steer clear. Otherwise, say hello to the chicest pants in your closet.

Pear shapes rejoice! I agree with others to focus on your waist measurement - these are true wide leg pants. They're extremely flattering and comfy - my waist is 30" and my hips are 42" but I opted for the L because I typically don't trust anything that says I can wear M bottoms. They're a smidge loose at the waist but the elastic keeps them in place and I like the extra room. They're a little longer than I would have liked - they hit the floor for me and I'm 5'6". Also, the material is polyester and it does show - if they were a cotton blend it would be 5 stars from me.
Little long for “short” but nice! Nicely made. Fit in the waist. I haven’t washed them so I don’t know if they will hold up. They are xxl short. I’m 5’2” and they are longish for me.

Very Comfortable! This is my first time giving wide leg pants a try and I’m happy with my purchase. For reference, I’m 5’7, 200lbs with a long torso. I originally bought size XL and it fit everywhere except my waist. My torso is so long and the pants couldn’t sit high enough on my waist line like it’s intended to. The fabric kept bunching in the crotch area and looked really awkward. So I exchanged for one size larger and the extra room was perfect! I would recommend pairing these pants with a fitted top like a bodysuit for the best overall look if you’re a gal with curves.

Best Palazzo pants for petite girls I don't usually buy clothes from Amazon but oh boy was I surprised with these pants! I bought them in Beige and Camel, both in the length "short" and fell in love with them (I need every single color). Good quality, does not feel cheap, and isn't see through. What I loved most is that being 5'2, I could never wear Palazzo pants before as they would always be wayyyy too long. These ones are perfect and do not even touch the floor when I am barefoot. With a small heel, they are even better! The beige ones make you look so chic. Good for the office or for a chic summer look, I need to go buy more ASAP.

Decent Pants 5'6" Waist 31" Hips 39" I got a large. The pants are decent. I am not a huge fan of the material (it is light and breathable but I think it is the type to rub and fray against a purse or between your thighs or something like that, but only time will tell). The cut is fantastic though! The waist line itself has little to no stretch, but the wide leg allows for plenty of thigh room so you don't really need it. I got a large and sizing down or up would not fit for sure. My one complaint is that I feel like the seam on the backside is a tad asymmetrical, like I have to keep twisting the pants so it will be in the right place. They came pretty wrinkled I don't know how the material would take to a steaming yet. Overall, if you're looking for cheap pants that look alright I would recommend these.

I have no luck on Amazon with clothing; Most has a poor fit/cut or is too big for me. These were amazing! -fabric is similar to rayon - which I love for keeping pet hair off me. Both cool and warm - though a thin material, so not super warm. -seems well constructed. It’s very comfy and soft. -Waist has good stretch, but is limited - don’t expect it to go up more than 1 size. Cut hits well - no weird protruding pockets which I have issues with in similar pants. - I’m 5’3” and 115lbs. I was so happy the waist and length were perfect! - flattering though it’s flouncy in a way that makes your hips look wide - which works with the cut and gives you a cinched waist!

Absolutely Fantastic Pants Honestly, a 5/5 review for these pants. I bought them looking to supplement my flowy pants wardrobe :) I wasn't compensated in any way for this review. Lets start with how they feel: quality. How they look: professional, fantastic. Can be dressed up or down, a button up looks good tucked in, too. How they fit: exactly as ordered. I got the XS, my waist is 24", the band hugs perfectly. There's a clear button and a clasp on the inside to make sure the pants stay on. Length: adequate. I'm 5'7", and I often wear these pants with a pair of booties to get that shorter look that's "in" now. Just one thing - the rule of everything flowy - to make them look more professional, iron them, the 5 minutes doing so is absolutely worth it. I wore these to several events and many of my lady friends asked where I got them, they ordered some and were happy as well :) 5 stars. Stop shopping around, buy these. Totally worth it.

Perfect wide leg pants I'm 5' 2" 116 lbs, and I wasn't sure that this pants will fit me by length. I ordered small petite. I was really surprised when I tried them on! They fit perfectly! Material is silky and light. I hope they will be fine for spring and summer.

Breezy and professional I’m super happy with this purchase. The pants fit amazingly. Only thing was is that the hem is kind of long, but that wasn’t a concern to me. I’m 5’2 and this seems to be the case with most of the pants I purchase online. Also I figured these trousers are meant for heels and I wore flats with them to work 😅 overall I got my normal size and they fit my waist, hips, and thighs nicely. I got tons of compliments! Try out these pants, you won’t regret it.

SO much better than expected! these pants are SO flattering, they were what i was looking for and more!! i was looking for this specific style & i couldnt find any on other reputable websites. the pictures on amazon looked cool but i figured that because it was amazon, it would probably be poor quality.
