The good, the bad and the rest Probably closer to a 4.5 really. I went with the Fit Type: 28" Inseam, Size: X-Large in Color: Khaki. For reference I'm over 50 years old. Height 5'6", weight 170lbs measurements Bust: 43 Waist: 34 Hips: 43. The fabric on these pants reminds me of the polyester pants my mother would wear to work in. It's rather a bit of a heavier weight with no stretch other than the back elastic band. The feel is smooth and it's so loose that it doesn't really come in contact with the skin much at all. When it does, like when sitting, it hasn't aggravated my sensitive skin. It has been through the washer and dryer, than sat over night in the dryer before I folded them up and stored in a basket for a few days before I got to this review. Yes, they are a bit wrinkled but not as badly as I would have expected given the way I treated them. They do iron out well. Otherwise, there was no harm in the cleaning cycle, didn't bleed, shrink or twist, pill or pick up excess lint. Really what drew me to these pants was that they were different. Yes, they are a bit large on me, but I like to order a size larger so I can alter if needed. Only minor alterations will be needed to make these a really nice set of slacks for me. That's the best compliment, in my opinion, if it's worth messing with to make them exactly what you want and these feel like they are. Overall, I'm happy with them but can't see wearing them in warmer weather. Peace Peace

Material Content Not As Advertised This is listed as 80% linen, 20% cotton, but the pants that I received were 40% linen, 60% cotton (assuming by "lines" they meant linen). The only reason I bought these pants was because of the high linen content. This explains why some reviewers think they feel more like cotton pants - because they are 60% cotton! They are also very small. Short and much tighter than shown in the photo. I am 5'7" with 41" hips - I ordered the size M, as per their size chart, a M fits up to 42.91" hips. They are very snug around the hips - if I was any bigger, I would be afraid to sit down for fear that they would tear. They are snug enough that the waitband pulls down in the back, and the pockets don't lay completely flat, because the fabric is pulling across my butt. The length reaches to about 1" above my ankle bone if I wear these at my natural waist. I'm afraid to wash them, because if they shrink, I won't be able to wear them. Probably will be returned, even though I like the color, and they seem to be made well.

MY FAVORITE PANTS I LOVE THESE! I'm 5'2" so buying pants online is always a little bit of a chance because usually they're too long but these are perfect! I got them in September 2023 (6 months ago) and they are still in perfect condition, I've worn them ice skating, sledding in the snow and they're still in perfect condition. They're not very thick and feel thin so I was worried they'd rip when I totally failed at ice skating but they didn't. Because they're thin they don't get too hot or feel too thick, and I wore leggings under them in the snow and they worked great and I didn't get cold. They also have a lining on the inside so it feels like I'm wearing swishy pants. I wear them at work a lot with a blouse so they work for a business setting, and they're my go to coffee shop pants so they can be dressed down and up. I'm literally going to wear these until they die then buy another pair.

Feels luxurious compared to other loungewear I’ve tried! I am at a point in my life where I want comfort and luxury or at least look and feel more luxurious! I have had 4 abdominal surgeries/Csections (5 kids) and just had a hysterectomy. I have been looking for clothes that make me feel good and look better than typical sweat outfits, etc. with a flattering effect or piece. I am pleasantly surprised by this outfit. It is a little see through for the white one, but I seriously would be surprised if it wasn’t see through (duh, it’s white) . Maybe it’s the white color that makes it look a little fancier? Not sure. But I do like it! The texture is soft (I’m a seam and texture type). I just tried it on and I’ll be sure to wear it for a day indoors! I’m a klutz in white! I’m 158lbs, 5’4” and one week post surgery (filled with air) - I think it’s fits nice now and I only expect it to fit even nicer once the bloat goes down.

They aren't jean material like I thought they would be, they are stretchy yoga pant material. With that being said there are no pockets or belt loops. Let me start with telling y'all I'm 5ft 10inches and 142 pounds and ended up with the medium. If you are tall and thin like me you may run into some issues with sizing. The leg length to waist ratio wasn't too great. If I got the correct size in the waist (small) I needed 2 sizes up for length. But the size that fit my length (large) are huge in the waist. So I went in the middle and bought a medium. They are a little shorter than I would like and a little baggy in the waist. Since there is no belt loops there is not much I can do with that. So for us tall ladies there is definitely an issue in sizing.

I take comfort in having even a little (even though I have a flat stomach) but if you have a belly or are just self conscious, I'd be a little weary. Just got reference: I'm 5'10, 42 inch hips, 32 inch waist. American Eagle pant size 12 Long.

Strange, long length Overall, I would say that these pants are average, which is why I am giving them an average 3-star rating. The main reason I'm not a huge fan of them is because they are a weird length. In the photos they look like a capri length, but they're much longer than that. I'm tall (5'11"), and they are as long as pants on me. So if you're taller they might be cropped/capri length, but if you're shorter they would probably be too long even for pants. The material is quite thin and see through. It doesn't have much stretch to it at all as it's almost like a chiffon type material, so I would definitely size up if you want them to fit flowy and loose as pictured. I don't mind the color though.

I took pictures right after taking them out so they appear to be quite wrinkled in them. However, after a wash, they're much better. I didn't iron them and I don't have many wrinkles at all. I'm 5'9" and about 151 pounds and got a size large. I think that was the right call for a person of my size. I wish I had more of a defined waist, as I think these would look cuter. Overall, I'm happy with these lightweight, ankle length pants that add a fun flair to my wardrobe!

Waistband is small! These pants are super adorable, and very nice quality. I absolutely LOVE the pink color. However, here are the issues I have with these pants: 1. The waistband is soooo tight. I stretched them out on the back of a chair for two days hoping it would make the elastic give a little. I even steamed it while stretched but neither gave me much, if any relief. 2. I am tall, 5'9" and pants are always short on me. I can make these long enough by pulling them down from my waist (which then makes the elastic even tighter when lowered to my hip) but I have a lot of sagging in the crotch. I will still wear these but will have to wear them with a long silk shirt that will cover up the crotch area. 3. Last things to know are that you should NOT wear any lotion of any kind when wearing these pants. I mistakenly had some on when I wanted to wear these and this fabric really shows moisture so I couldn't wear them and had to send them to the wash. Which, MAKE SURE YOU WASH THEM SEPARATELY. The pink dye in these pants bled onto other items in my wash. Nothing was important so I didn't care but everyone should know and take heed of this warning. Anyway, super cute pants. I may just take the elastic out completely (if that is possible) and make it a drawstring waist because I DO want to wear these.

Perfect Pants for the Holidays These tartan plaid pants are really cute! The perfect pant for Christmas celebrations. The red color is vibrant, and the pattern is really beautiful. The big bow that ties in the front adds festive style! I found them to be plenty long enough for my long legs (I normally wear 32 inch inseam jeans). They did run a touch snug though in the waist, so if you are between sizes in that area you may want to size up. There was a lot of extra room in the hip area though. I like the cut with the gathered waistline and loose, wide leg. Very on trend currently. The fabric was different than I expected. It is heavier and a bit more stiff than other dress pants in my closet. It feels like a quality fabric that should last. I do wish that there were pockets. For that missing element, I took off one star. All told, I am quite pleased with these fun red plaid pants. Looking forward to wearing them at the family holiday celebration!

Excellent pants with some caveats I'm 5'8", about 120 - 125 lbs, but have a really short torso and the inseam of someone closer to 5'10". I first ordered these pants in size medium in white (pictured) and I loved them so much that I also ordered them in green. For reference, I'm wearing the pants pulled up to my natural waist and wearing 3" heels. I thought the medium fit great and looked ok on my butt, until I tried on the size small in green which looked way better on my butt than the medium, and better all-around. The problem with the green is that they are about 1.5" shorter than the white, despite the product page saying the inseam on small and medium are the same. The picture speaks for itself - clearly they are not the same length. Someone else made this same comment with a photo about the white vs. blue, only she ordered the exact same size and the white were a lot longer. I did not find them to be see-through at all as others have mentioned, but perhaps that's because I didn't buy a skin tight fit. I received many compliments on the white pair and will keep them, but unfortunately any other color seems to be too short, so the green pair is going back. Update: I ordered the blue in medium and they are as long as the white medium so it seems as if the color doesn’t matter, but the small is a lot shorter than the medium even though the product page says the inseam is the same.

Run a bit big but look fine with maternity shirt over the baggy space Currently 21 weeks pregnant, 5' 9", 145#. this is my second baby so my belly is a lot bigger this time around and I needed maternity scrubs. I ordered these in medium (my pre-pregnancy Wonderwink size) and the Cherokee maternity scrubs in a small. I found that the Cherokee maternity scrubs were very well fitted except for the fact that the belly band was way too tight and by the time I was nine months they would be impossible to wear. I have the same problem with these scrubs as other reviewers that the space in between the tummy and your legs is very big, however, once I put them on with a maternity shirt you can't tell the difference anyway. The good thing about them being a little big is I definitely have room to grow and they are super comfortable. Far more comfortable than the Cherokee were. I was going to try them in a small but I ultimately decided that the medium would fit me the best all the way through my pregnancy.

Thank you reviews that recommended sizing down First off, I LOVE these jeans! Secondly, the reviews I read on here were a huge help in sizing. It’s weird because this are definitely Women’s jeans, but the sizing is in Juniors. For reference I am 6’0, 170-175 lbs, and a solid Women’s size 10/30, which would translate to a Juniors size 11. Per the suggestion of previous reviews, I reluctantly sized down to a 9. The last thing I wanted was for the jeans to fit for an hour, only to stretch beyond repair. I ordered a size 9 with 34” inseam. I am SO happy I did! When I pulled it out the bag I thought to myself, there’s NO WAY this is gonna work. But I squeezed myself into the initially doubtful fit. Wisely I decided to wear them around the house for a couple hours, w/o removing tags (just in case.. again, because of reviews). And to my pleasant surprise this wonderful pair of jeans decided to form to my figure in the best ways possible. So if you’re in doubt and you got some junk in the trunk, size down and trust the process. Because I could totally see if I had bought these in an 11, I wouldn’t like them now. Also the 34” inseam does almost hit the floor for me, but the next pair I order will be a 36”, just so I have some extra length.

A Review from a girl who Actually Collects These Styles of Pants Hi! So okay, I have had a few pairs of these “soft, boho pattern flare pants” throughout my young life. I remember when they peaked back in early 2010s! So, if you see my photos, I already owned a few pairs in Red/Pink and Black/White. These pants are my favorite. They are so soft, such good quality. They feel like cotton, they’re pretty thick (and don’t show panty lines!), and they’re the perfect length on my 5’7 self, they are truly the best. I really wanted some in Blue, but I couldn’t find any in my area. So I decided to give Amazon a shot. I’m a bit disappointed. You may not be able to tell from my photos, but these pants are… different. The pattern is clearly laser printed. It doesn’t look as ‘real’ as the red and black ones. Also, the quality is much worse. It doesn’t feel like soft cotton, it feels more like polyester. The pattern does look good, but it’s just not the same. The fit is fine, but it’s more lightweight than my genuine boho pants. It’s also more see through!?! That’s not supposed to happen, IMO. Overall, they’re thinner, not as soft, and really cheaply made. But still, If you’re just going for a boho look, then go for it. I’d say, if you can, try to find the real deal boho flares in discount department stores.

Not the Palazzo Pants I was expecting Pictured: Front and back of the pants before and after wash. To me, the term "Palazzo pants" evokes a longer, more flowy silhouette. The length feels more akin to long gauchos or very baggy capris. The inseam on these pants are 24" so bear that in mind when you purchase. They must have used models that are maybe 5' 5" or 5' 6" for the hem to almost hit the ground. Or maybe the photos are edited. I got these pants thinking they'd at least hit my ankles or skim the top of my feet. No such luck. The cotton fabric feels like light denim or twill. It's kinda stiff, so the pantlegs retain their voluminous shape. The contrasting white stitching on the seams give these pants a very casual look. I'm not 100% sold on the wide white string tie. I may remove it altogether since the waistband is elasticized. Be sure to air dry as these pants are cotton and will shrink up even higher!

100% thick, quality cotton, wide leg pants Surprisingly, length wise, they fit me super well. I am 5‘4“, 110 lbs. I ordered the size XS. I don’t have to flip the waist over, as i normally do with pants. I can wear them as sewn and they graze the top of my foot. The waist is a little too snug to be comfortable though. That’s sad because in all other ways these pants are perfect. I wore them for several hours and was uncomfortable. The legs are wide. The ecru, bone color I chose looks so smart with a sweater. I wish they fit me better because they are cute. . The cotton isn’t blended, it’s actually 100% cotton, which I prefer. It’s thick enough to feel almost like twill, but soft and it drapes well. It’s a great weight for pants. Based on my experience, I would order up a size.

Comfy lightweight casual I live in a neutral wardrobe and I like these comfy courduroy pants. They're lightweight so they're a nice transitional piece between seasons. Elastic waistband and big pockets - it does seem to run pretty roomy. For reference, I'm 5'8" and 155lbs and ordered a medium. If I had a lot in my pockets these pants would be ridin' super low. :D

Odd fit The pants look great when I wear them with a long top, as in the product listing. But the fit under the top is odd and somewhat uncomfortable, mostly in the way the fabric bags out and folds down at the crotch. If I try to pull them up higher to prevent this, they come way above my waist. The pockets are smaller than I usually find in pants of this style. I can slide in my phone (I have a small one), but the top sticks out, making me worry that it might fall out. Also, as noted in the listing, these pants are 100% polyester. I hoped they might turn out to have at least a small amount of cotton, but they are in fact entirely synthetic fiber. The one thing I really like is the brown color, which is a kind of a rich copper shade. For reference, I'm 5'4" and 155 lbs, and I ordered size Medium. I was able to machine wash these in cool water and dry them on low heat without having them change their size or shape.

Funky and fun trousers! These trousers are funky and fun! Definitely not as bold and funky as the listing photos show, which I’m thankful for! Whenever listings say lantern sleeves or pant legs they never are as pronounced as the photos. Trousers run true to size, I ordered a size small and they fit great! For reference I’m 5’7 approximately 135lbs, thick hips and thighs. Very casual and comfortable! I love that corduroy is coming back! Highly recommend!

GET THESE PANTS NOW I was apprehensive about getting these pants because I thought they might not be flattering on me, but YEOOOOO I wear these boys like TWICE A WEEK IM IN LOVE. They're cute asf with a crop top or oversized sweater tucked in!!! And theyre so COMFORTABLE. The waist is completely elastic like pajama pants, but still has a button and zipper to look like jeans. I usually wear them with a belt to sinch in my waist a little more, but they're cute without one too! The pockets are DEEP too I can actually fit my phone in em its wild. I get compliments literally every single time I wear them! The only thing I could say is if you're on the taller side like me (5'10/11) the leg holes kind of ride up if you sit with your legs crossed or bend over. Just wear long socks or tights underneath if its cold, otherwise it really doesnt even matter. I think I got a 3xl just because I was scared they were Asian sizes and I wear a size 14/16 jeans so u know how it is lol. They fit GREAT but I could have comfortably gone down to a 2x or maybe even 1x. They really really feel like you're just wearing pajamas I love it

Weird fit Even when I saw that these Cropped Corduroy Pants were 100% polyester I was not dissuaded from ordering them. This is because they are from Yesno, a brand that I own pieces from and wear often. If I didn't know that these were polyester pants, I wouldn't know. The fabric feels nice and soft, if pressed I would have guessed that they are a cotton blend. As is usual with this brand, these pants are very well made. They have roomy slash pockets in front and an interesting dropped yolk detail at the back waistband. The belt loops are nice and large so that a wide belt can be worn with them. The waist is mostly elastic and very stretchy, and the zipper and buttons at the front are nice quality. I'm very happy with the way these pants are constructed, the feel of the fabric and the rustic brown color. Sadly, for all I love about these pants they do not fit properly. They fit me, just not properly. There is a dropped crotch which was not mentioned in the listing. So if I have the pants fastened at my belly button or just above, the crotch is hanging two or three inches below that. This makes the rear end look weird because it puckers horizontally by the back seam. They do look better if I pull them up, but then the waist is right under my bra line. Even though a dropped crotch is not my preference, I would still wear them except the fit is not comfortable either. The back seam just rests against my hiney and somehow feels tight... Which is very strange since I'm pretty flat back there. So, even though the fit is not working for me these pants will probably work for others and I think are not a bad deal at $34.99. If you have a longer torso and more booty, these might be your pants! For reference, I am 5'8" 190 lb, and ordered the XL.

Beyond comfortable These baggy corduroy pants are seriously so comfortable they feel like you are wearing pajamas! The fabric is soft yet pretty good weight. The waist is partial elastic, so they fit well without any gap. I am short (5'3") so they fit in length like a regular pair of pants for me and not cropped but I feel like it still works. I typically wear an 8-10 in women's pants and ordered these in a size M.

Please read this review. THE GOOD: —Nicely packaged. —Hose are sheer —Husband said they looked good —Elastic top —Reinforced toe —Top will stretch to 9” (with effort) —They were appropriate length (to my knee) —Colors were as expected Sounds good so far!! But… THE BAD: —Elastic top is REALLY snug —Uncomfortable after 20 minutes wear —After 35 minutes, I had to take them off because of the pain around the top. Felt like a rubber band around my leg. I’m 5’6” and weigh 194. I actually thought these knee highs would be too large, if anything. Nope! They are definitely not plus size. Remember, the gals in the advertising look like they’re happy as can be. I’ll bet in reality, they couldn’t wait to get done with the shoot so they could get circulation back in their lower legs. Bahaha!! Unfortunately I cannot in good conscience recommend these knee highs to plus size gals. They may be great, however for smaller ladies.
Not bad I am 5”1 so it’s a little long for me but I like it anyway. The fabric is thin

Comfortable but some issues. I am 5'9", 165lb. Ordered size large. These pants are comfortable and fit pretty well. They aren't as thin or as stiff as linen, but still breathe. I also appreciate the deep pockets. However, there are some things I don't like about them. My major complaint is the light-colored material is see-through. I hate that I will have to consider what type of undies or shirt I wear. Also, the front is somewhat baggy while the back seems to cling and emphasizes my crack when bending forward. The material also clings to show the front pockets underneath. Finally, the back pockets are stitched more to the side than the back. What is frustrating about this is that if they were stitched to the back, they could have helped with the transparency issue! I will keep them for comfort, but not order more.

These cotton work pants are a nice weight cotton fabric, and very comfortable. They do require a little ironing after washing and drying because they do wrinkle some. Being 97% cotton, the pants did shrink when washed on cool and dried on light. Inside seams are straight going down pantleg. Two front and two back pockets hold my cell phone without falling out. The drawstring is a nice addition if you need to tighten the pants. The elastic around the waist doesn't bunch or irritate my skin. The length is perfect for a 5' 2 height.

Size chart is body measurements not garment measurements These khaki pants are made of nice quality, thin cotton fabric, with front and rear pockets large enough to hold a cell phone (yay!). The size chart is true to body (not garment) measurements, so these size large pants are too big to fit my daughter's 41" hips. The pants are very comfortable, but they aren't really suitable for yoga. These are casual, elastic-waist pants that would be great for someone who has trouble with zippers, since they are easy to put on and take off.

5’5” 165lbs apple shaped, ordered a XL They fit perfectly but look like work pants/ scrubs. I’ll still wear them because they fit nicely and it’s rare to find high waisted pants that are flattering on apple shaped bodies so a win nonetheless!

These actually surprised me, in a good way! I ordered these to have some pants I could wear out for strictly for comfortability because I gained 40 pounds in just 3 months after I turned 43. (No more high metabolism for me lol). But when I tried them on, they fit SO GOOD. I thought they’d look like something you’d wear to an office job, but no, they look so much better than expected. I thought they’d be a little short in length, but they come down low, just as I like & are high waisted elastic. I’m so glad I ordered these, might get another pair in a different color. True to size as well. I’m 5’3” & I weigh 160 lbs. & I ordered an XL

Perfect Pants Lightweight and breathable. These pants held their shape through two wears. I’m 5’ 4” and 135 lbs. with a wider bottom and thighs and slim waistline. These pants were super flattering as they sat slightly high waisted and flowed nicely. The pattern was vibrant and I received lots of compliments.

These are so comfy! I am 5ft 9in & approx 165-170lbs. Bought an XXL. I normally wear a size 10 pant. I dont normally get that size, Im usually an L. Got these extra big, hoping they would be flowy and I could wear with heels. I love the pockets. Material is soft and hangs well. Good quality.

Best yoga/ work pants! I just opened the package and decided to give these yoga pants a try I did my Beachbody On Demand workout and the pants were very comfortable easy to do my work out. I am 51 and 100 pounds I purchased the small petite 29 inch inseam and they are perfect. You can also tell by the photos that they would work with work shoes as well and they look great the pants do not look like yoga pants when you put heels on. I would highly recommend them and I’m looking forward to getting them and other colors. UPDATE 3-8-2022 -these are my go to pants for work now. I have attended to major fundraisers in the past 2 weeks and wore these pants. They hug your butt which is great, especially as you get older and have less of a butt and more of a waist. They make me feel great when I wear them. I have also worked out in then, washed them a few times so far and they look and feel like new. They did not shrink or fade. I’m purchasing another pair!!

Best Work Pants EVER!!! I don't normally leave reviews, but I absolutely had to for these pants. I am a server at a very busy restaurant, in Florida where it gets hot as heck. I was out of work for 2 months thanks to covid shutting down all our restaurants, and well I gained some weight while sitting at home doing nothing 🤣🤣🤣. I was called to return to work on Memorial Day weekend and of course, my black work pants would not fit over my newly found, voluptuous body. I spent 2 days running from store to store, fitting rooms were closed and I was getting desperate. I bought and returned no less than 10 pairs to various stores. I found 2 pairs that fit decently, but I absolutely hated because they didn't fit right. I have a small waist, big hips, big thighs, etc., so I need more of a stretchy material. I gave in and hit Amazon figuring if I had to buy and return multiple pairs, I could at least do it in the comfort of my own home. Well, this was the 1st pair i ordered. I can absolutely say, i LOVE LOVE these! They fit perfect, stretch in all the right places, they are light enough so they aren't hot, so soft, unlike starchy, rough dress pants. If you have a job where you are required to wear black dress pants, I highly recommend these. For reference, sizing is spot on. I am 5'7", 165 lbs and typically wear a size 11 juniors/8 or 10 womens. I bought the Medium 31" inseam and they fit perfectly! I ordered 2 more pairs right away!

Best TEACHER pants ever! I’m 5’7” 180 lbs and am very pleased with the Larges. Also, these are long enough to look sexy in a pair of heels but not too long to drown my flat sneakers.

They FIT!! I’ve seen these adorable pants everywhere on Amazon but never in my size. I’m usually a 12/14 or XL. I also prefer all cotton/natural fibers; it’s very hot & humid here in Fl. I decided to take a chance on these in size Large and happy to , not only do they fit, but they fit well! I carry excess weight in my tummy. My waist is approx 36/37” and my hips are 43.” I prefer my clothes to fit oversized and loose. Think Free People, which these btw, are pretty good dupes. The length is spot on for me at 5’5” and 165lbs. I’m wearing 2.5” platforms on my images. The only reason I took one star off is for the fiber content. I was surprised when I saw the care tag read 100% cotton. They def feel like they contain some type of synthetic material mixed in with cotton. They did soften up after washing on low with fabric softener. I also dried on low and there was zero shrinkage or fading of colors. Despite my mixed feeling about the material, these are super lightweight and comfortable. The elasticized waistline is stitched nicely in place so no worries about it twisting or turning. The drawstring is also functional, not just decorative and the front (and back) pockets are a nice touch. I received these through Amazon Vine, but I’d pay full for these. However, I probably wouldn’t wear these in the heat of summer. These will be great to throw on with a cotton tee or tank and a cardigan as it gets cooler. My only other issue is what color to get next - love the great variety of brilliant shades from which to choose. The brand name, Ziesexy, makes me chuckle, but I do recommend them. I’ve purchased some really cute cotton tops from them with which I’m super happy. Definitely recommend these pants and the brand which has a store here on Amazon.

Decent tracksuit: but has some flaws This 2 piece tracksuit is decent for the price. It runs very true to size - I ordered XL: I'm 5'10" and around 150lbs. It's actually a little bigger than I prefer: but I'd rather order a size up than have it be too small. The fabric has a lot of stretch to it and can accommodate varying bust/hip sizes. It does has some flaws though, which may be a deal breaker for some people. The sleeves are too short - and the pants are a little too long + slightly baggy around the ankles. Also, the collar circumference of the top is way too big around my neck: even when it's fully zipped. To camouflage this, I plan on wearing it with the "lapels" turned down. The fabric is also a little bit scratchy feeling and not very soft. The white zippers are pure snow white color, while the white stripes of the fabric are an off-white color. You can tell if you really look in person but it's barely noticeable. Overall, this isn't too bad for the value if you can overlook some potential flaws. I give this 3 out of 5 stars

Run small, nice thicker fabric and decent pockets. I wasn't sure what to expect from these but I have been looking for a nice midweight wide leg pant for work and thought I would give these a try.I really like the fabric, it is a good midweight that I can wear in the winter without freezing to death. The fabric has a sleek feel to it and the texture is somewhere between a jean and a knit pant, it's a very unique fabric. The fabric moves well and feels great on, it also doesn't seem to attract pet hair which I love since I have cats and always struggle with keeping black pants pet hair free.Unfortunately, these do seem to run a bit small. I am 5'7" and 155lbs with a 31" waist. I normally wear between a size M and L. I got these in a size L which says it has a 32-33" waist because I really don't like my waistbands tight. As you can see this waistband was pretty tight, not uncomfortably so, but I definitely have a bit of belly bulge going on here. The waistband has enough stretch that when I sit down it's still comfortable but not the most flattering.There are functioning pockets on the front and back. They are slightly smaller than normal pants pockets but still work to fit my wallet or phone (I have a Z4 fold).These pants are comfy and look cute if I have a shirt on that doesn't show the waistband. I really do like them but wish the waistband was an inch wider. I also think they are a bit pricey for what you are getting. However, overall I would recommend...just size up and don't go by their size chart.

FANTASTIC!! My husband has a medical condition that makes him feel very cold all the time. It’s hard to find reasonably-priced lined pants, so when I came across these on sale $10 off, I decided to try them. He loves them! They are thick and warm! Great quality. The first thing he did when he put them on was to put his (very large) cell phone in one pocket and his hard-cased reading glasses in the other pocket — both fit very nicely without making the pants too tight. He is 5’9” and weighs about 160 pounds. I got him size large—the ones with the cuff around the ankles because I didn’t want to hem them. They fit perfectly and do not have a lot of excess length of material gathered up around the ankles.He says the only thing that would have made them better is if they had back pockets.I took several photos so that you can see how they fit and how much of the tip of his cell phone shows at the top of his pocket. These are great pants and I highly recommend them.I write honest reviews to help others make informed purchases because I read reviews myself and appreciate how they can be. I hope that my review helped you!

To all my fellow plus-sized ladies, just throw your old tactical pants in the trash and get these! Seriously. I am a size 16-18; I ordered both the size 18L and the 20R (a size up as other reviewers suggested), and I am so glad I did. The 18L were a little tight and the length was a little long (I am 5'9"-- but they were still wearable with my work boots)... and by tight, I mean I had to lay down on the bed and grab the channel locks. The 20R are prefect. They are super freaking comfortable and unbelievably flattering. I have a mommy pouch, and all other tactical pants made me super self concious about it; in fact, I think they accentuated it. I don't know what it is about the front panel of the pant, but it completely flattens your tummy. I also have a healthy sized rear end and thighs. They hug and flatter every curve, instead of giving you a saggy diaper looking butt or baggy pant legs like others. My hubby actually checked me out in work my work pants 😉 Spend the money on these; you won't regret it. These are super comfy, stretchy pants that look amazing and give me a great range of motion at work. With that said, they would be great for police, EMTs, and etc.

Best tactical pants for women in today’s market! I’ve been looking for tactical pants for a long time. I’ve tried every single brand and style until I found the 5.11 Stryker Tactical Pants. Good enough! ATTRACTS A TON OF LINT! I just need these for EMT class so I will hopefully find a better alternative after class. Tried Under Armour's cute tactical pant too but not a chance. These do the trick if you're curvy, just don't expect perfection. The waist gaps in the back (without a shirt you would see a good 2" of crack haha) and I am only 5'4.5" and it shows some ankle when I sit. Great quality! but still not quite right for Women's Bodies I had a pair of these for years and loved them! They are very durable and breathable for hot days. This fabric is not loud (swishy) and is perfect for the office and the field. However, these are still not quite designed for Women's Bodies. I have a 27" waist and nearly 40" hips, and these are tight around the hips. I'll try ordering a size up, but I have a feeling I will need to tack a larger pair at the waist. The "Apex" 5.11s fit women's proportions better, but these pants are made of a better fabric.

Comfortable and actually fit my lady body! I love that these pants, even though I’m built like a bean pole, are comfortable and fit me well. I am 5’7” with a 26” waist, so I got a size 2 long. They are perfect! They aren’t skin tight, perfect amount of room for me to tuck my shirt in and possibly wear thermals under in the winter months. These are the only pant’s I could find that don’t make my butt look weird either. LOVE

Finally I look like a girl in my uniform!! I am used to wearing unattractive men's prison pants as the pants for my Ranger uniform. I hate them. They are unflattering, but free. I've finally decided to get my own and these are so flattering! I have a butt! I look like a woman! So exciting! The fit.... I am 5'5'' and 155+lbs with a little extra weight in the middle. I got the 8 Long. The 8's fit nicely. Not tight to my body, but not loose. They were too long, which was expected. They did not have regular. I hemmed them myself no problem though. I recently lost some weight and am down to 147 lbs (and going!) and am wishing I got a size 6. They might fit a bit better. The style and function... They are higher waisted, hitting a couple inches below my belly button. The pockets are super useful- the thigh pockets have slots inside so you can organize what you put there. There is a smaller pocket in the front that I keep my chapstick in. I attach my radio to the main pockets on the front/sidd. The belt loops are thick and secure. I attached quite a few pictures: some showing the non-hemmed version, a couple hemmed.

Always my go to. If you’re thinking about buying these... just DO IT! These are the most comfortable tac pants I’ve owned. They fit like your favorite pair of jeans, have durable belt loops, and great pockets. They also have just the right amount of stretch so no guest appearances ;) the blue matches my uniform perfectly. They do fade slightly, BUT it’s after at least 50 washes, and fade about as quickly as the rest of my uniform. I’m 5’8” and Wore a 14 long when I was 185-200lbs, and have been wearing a 12 long from 165(current) to 185, although I may opt for the 10 long next time I buy. I’d say go one size up from the size of jeans you wear. If you’re over 5’8” I definitely recommend getting a long inseam because the regular inseam hits right above the ankle bone.

Flattering, comfy, on trend wide leg striped pants! I am always on the lookout for a cute pair of pants that are comfy, yet stylish. I wear sweatpants often, and it is nice to have a more stylish alternative that is also just as comfortable to wear. These pants check all the boxes! They have an elastic waist with a fabric tie, a flowy wide leg cut, and a cute striped pattern. These pants are perfect to wear on casual days out shopping. I also love pants like this when I’m traveling or catching a flight. Another option I’m thinking of is wearing these pants as a bathing suit coverup in the summer with a fitted tank top or tee. The options are endless with these pants. I have worn similar pairs of pants with a fitted top and a denim jacket for a put-together look. I’m only 5’4 so the pants were a bit long on me and I wore them with platform sandals for the photo. But luckily- I actually like my pants to fit a little bit longer and drag the floor so I don’t mind! I would like to order these pants in several other colors because I can see myself living in them. 5 stars!

10 stars for these pants! These pants blew me away. I was not expecting them to be this great based on the pics the seller has provided. But they are! I’m so glad I took a risk on these because now I want them in all the colors!!!!!! Ok, where do I start. They are sooooo soft like nothing I’ve ever felt before, and yet when you look at the fabric up close, it’s sort of ribbed, which gives the pants their perfect stretch. They are slightly longer than I’d like, but with these kitten heeled boots on, they look just right. They are very comfortable and have pockets, so cmon, that’s a win in itself for women’s clothing. I especially like that they’re not the usual white/beige color. The white is actually more of a cream and the beige is a greenish tan color. Very nice. I plan to wear the hell outta these pants in the winter and summer. Can be styled in so many ways bringing great value to my closet with a really affordable pair of pants.

I had initially got this for beach use, but they're so comfortable that I want to use them as lounge wear too. I think the price is fair, and the quality is great. They fit great! I'm 5'6" and 128 lbs; they fit my waist and leg length very nicely. I'll likely get these in other colors.

Best affordable compression for curvy figure If you have the problem of leggings constantly falling off of your waist, these might be your lifesaver. These offer a lot of compression and can stretch high up on your waist so nothing jiggles. They are very smooth and silky like a second skin. They cinch in more at the waist and then fan out at the hips to flatter a curvier body but also stay put for all of the miles. They are squat proof and hold up well over many washes and wears (mine have gone over 1,000 miles and still seem like they have a bunch of life left in them). But they are also cool enough to wear in hot climates for more compression or sun protection. I have run several marathons and ultras in Texas and Florida in them. I also love that they can stretch over my ankles and offer more solid compression when I need it while traveling. This is a rare find as I’m nearly 6 ft tall. and mostly leg. I am not sure how this will affect more petite ladies, though, so something to keep in mind. My only complaint is that the shape/seams in the rear could allow more room and be more flattering for larger booties. But for the price and functionality, these are real winners.

Don't Hesitate - They're Worth It I'm a grad student with kids, so my budget is limited but I lead a very active lifestyle. I have a few pairs of random $5 - $7 cheap running leggings from Amazon, collected over the years. Some are better than others, but in general, they fall down or annoy me in one way or another. Had my first Tough Mudder obstacle course race coming up and wanted truly reliable running pants. These pants win on every level. They're the right height, never slip. Held up to a couple months of cross training, indoors as well as outdoors, and through the entire Mudder with all of its various obstacles. I didn't think about them once during the race, and to me, that is how you know you are wearing some good pants. Highly recommend. I got them on a lightning deal for $16, but would pay more. They're worth it. For sizing reference, I am 6'0" and 145 pounds and ordered a size small.

My Go To Scrubs Height: 5’2” Weight: 155 lbs Chest: 34DDD I personally love Adar brand scrubs because of the cool touch material. I have psoriasis and am sensitive to heat and heavier/scratchy fabrics. The cool touch material feels silky and smooth on my skin. It doesn’t retain heat and washes/dries well. My only wish was that the jogger style scrub pants and heathered colors Pro styles came in Petite. These are Medium size joggers and they’re just a little too long on me at 5’2”. They start out ok but stretch and get looser on a 16 hour shift. I feel like if they were made in Petite, they would fit like a glove. The waist band is very stretchy and has a lot of give. Easy to pull on and off when I’m rushing to get ready for work. These scrubs were fresh out of the package, fold marks and all. The only thing with the purple and wine colors are that they bleed a bit. So, it’ll either rub off on your skin or on your clothes if you wear a sweatshirt or jacket over your tops. You can usually tell by looking at the plastic poly bag, if there is color on the bag, then that same color will transfer to your skin and should be washed separate on its first wash. I carry most of my weight in my chest and my belly. I do not have big hips or a big booty. My curves are more so in my chest. The scrub top is just a smidgen tight on my midsection. I am a 34DDD and got a size Large top. It’s nothing that would deter me from wearing the top. If anything, just more incentive to take the stairs at work and hopefully drop a couple more lbs. Overall, happy customer 8/10

I have psoriasis and am sensitive to heat and heavier/scratchy fabrics. The cool touch material feels silky and smooth on my skin. It doesn’t retain heat and washes/dries well. My only wish was that the jogger style scrub pants and heathered colors Pro styles came in Petite. These are Medium size joggers and they’re just a little too long on me at 5’2”. They start out ok but stretch and get looser on a 16 hour shift. I feel like if they were made in Petite, they would fit like a glove. The waist band is very stretchy and has a lot of give. Easy to pull on and off when I’m rushing to get ready for work. These scrubs were fresh out of the package, fold marks and all. The only thing with the purple and wine colors are that they bleed a bit. So, it’ll either rub off on your skin or on your clothes if you wear a sweatshirt or jacket over your tops. You can usually tell by looking at the plastic poly bag, if there is color on the bag, then that same color will transfer to your skin and should be washed separate on its first wash. I carry most of my weight in my chest and my belly. I do not have big hips or a big booty. My curves are more so in my chest. The scrub top is just a smidgen tight on my midsection. I am a 34DDD and got a size Large top. It’s nothing that would deter me from wearing the top. If anything, just more incentive to take the stairs at work and hopefully drop a couple more lbs. Overall, happy customer 8/10

The butt was snug fitting which is weird cause I usually have no butt. Some stretch left in the butt though. Like no problem bending down. The waist band was a little bit snug but perfect for how I wanted it to fit. I usually wear a. XXS/ petite in FIG pants and have to tighten the waist. So it will be nice not to have to do that with these. The length was perfect too. Have not worn to work or washed so I can’t say how they will hold up. Overall I will buy more in the future. I might size up next time just because if we order tacos for lunch that waist band won’t have a lot of stretch left. Also you know how the dryer shrinks your clothing too Haha.

Toss the Figs, get Adar!!! I adore Adar! They’re reasonably priced and flattering to a curvier figure. I’m 5’6 & 183 pounds. I’m busty (38G) and I have wide hips (childbirth x 4). XL fits like a dream, doesn’t make me look frumpy, and lets me move freely enough to do yoga. (Yes, literally, pictures attached.) FINALLY, there’s a brand for me! Thank you, Adar! Cherokee is hit or miss with sizing that seems inherently catered more towards males. Figs only look good if the person wearing them is built like a stick figure. Carhartt has disappointed more than it’s ever delivered. But Adar is just stellar! This is my fourth pair, and I’m definitely gonna get them in every color. (Cough, cough…can a fan get a loyal customer discount?) Like I said, XL is perfect for me. I get the pants in XL Tall even though I’m only 5’6, just because I like to wear them higher (the waist band sits at my navel) and I like them to still cover my ankle/come down to the top of my shoes. Just a personal preference. I have these in black (3 sets) and teal (1 set). I’ve washed the black ones a gazillion times and they’ve never faded. I make my scrubs work as hard as I do, so I can say confidently that the seams and the material hold up to nearly-abusive wear. I fully anticipate being able to say the same about the teal set. Absolutely love these! Comfy, cute, and affordable. It’s a win! Seriously, give this company a try…they won’t let you down!

You can wear then out to a bar/club, out to dinner, to the gym (although they may make you too hot), lounging around the house (did I mention yet how comfy they are?) and also as snow pants. Love the lightweight material and fit except the pockets I purchased these because I recently gained weight and needed something to cover all this extra fluff. These joggers were in line with my measurements and the material I wanted. We moved to Mexico last year, so I needed something lightweight and breathable. Since these are rayon spandex, I knew they would be perfect for the hot weather here. They fit as expected based on the size chart. I got the large. I'm 5'7", and in the photos I'm 175 lbs, 44 bust, 37 waist, 42.5 hips. My only complaint is the pockets. I know everyone wants pockets these days, but since rayon spandex is so lightweight, the tiny pockets in these joggers aren't deep enough to lay flat against the body. When I wear these, the pockets always end up puckering out in the front. If they were deeper and laid flat against the body, I wouldn't mind them so much.

I wish they offered the petite length in more colors I really like these pants. They’re comfortable, the fabric is flattering and doesn’t show off my cellulite and how out of shape I am. It’s thick and definitely not sheer. It’s stretchy, but hard to describe. I don’t think I have anything else like what these pants are made out of. I like that I can wear them with sneakers and a tee shirt, or put on a nice top to be a little more dressed up. I guess on me they kinda look a bit more like frumpy old lady pants, but honestly I don’t care. I’m 43 and I guess I am kinda frumpy, lol. The length on me is obviously not like it is in the listing photos. I’m 5’ 1” and have a longer torso and shorter legs, so everything is long on me. They’re still just barely cropped pants, and I think they look cute with my clogs. I’m not sure how much I’ll wear them in the winter, but we’ll see. I really love that they’ve got deep pockets. Like, I can fit my entire iPhone XS Max in there with room to spare. If only all women’s pants had pockets like these. I liked these so much I went ahead and ordered a different style of Ajisai joggers that aren’t offered in this shorter length. I’m hoping they’ll work out, but I suppose I can always have them tailored if they’re too long, but I really love them otherwise. I’d absolutely buy these in more colors if they offered them in the petite length. I’m just not interested in navy, although I may buy a second pair in black when they’re restocked. Finding cute pants when you’re short is so difficult. Especially when you don’t like to actually go out shopping and try things on. I’ve worn them several times now, and I’ve washed them and put them in the dryer on low, and they look perfect. They don’t collect lint and they don’t wrinkle easily. I’d definitely recommend them. Just be aware that if you’re short and not as skinny as you could be, (like me, I’m a size 10, creeping towards an 8) then you too are likely to look a bit frumpy in them rather than looking cool and statuesque like the model wearing them in the photos for the listing.

Else, I ordered according to the size chart and found it to be accurate. I am very happy with the black pants, though, and would consider getting another pair but stick to the dark colors to avoid the pocket seams showing.

Quality material...fits long. These are wonderfully comfortable pants. The material is similar to my adidas brand track pants that I love, however the waist is smaller and the length is longer (by about 3 inches). I am 5'4", average weight, even if I were to have gone down a size they would still have been too long.

Great Joggers! For some reason I thought these were going to be cotton, like sweatpant material, but they’re 90% polyester and 10% elastane and I love the feel. Very cooling and seem well made. I ordered the grey and Iike the color. I’m 5’9” and about 150 lbs and got a medium. The waist is slightly tight but it’s supposed to be with the elastic and there’s definitely give. What I’m really impressed with is the length, and as they are super long on me. Anyone who is shorter take note that these are long.

Kinda Shiny but Decent I am always on the lookout for comfortable pants that feel like wearing pajamas but look cute enough to wear as casual pants. These would have fit the bill except that they are weirdly shiny and are much too long for my liking. I noticed they looked a little shiny in the photos but I was hoping they were a more tech material like actual workout pants, especially since they're being called hiking pants, but it's like a weird shiny polyester that I personally just did not find attractive. Suffice to say, I will not be keeping these.Personal tastes aside, the pants themselves seem decently made and they do fit true to size except for the length which was too long on me (I'm 5'6" for reference). The waist is a true high waist as well with elastic and a drawstring. Honestly, if you're okay with shiny cargo pants, they're not bad, just not for me.

Chic pants & great quality I'm 5'8" 160lb and purchased a medium. Length is perfect. Paired these with boots with a heel and they still looked great. Quality of the fabric is incredible. It's heavy, thick, and buttery soft. I didn't get a chance to iron before putting on so theres a few wrinkles. They look fantastic with this turtle neck I also got on Amazon. Already have another pair coming in a different color. These can be casual but also dressed up with a nice sweater to give a chic but uber comfy look. Absolutely love these pants and will be a staple this fall.

Love these pants! I wore them in Toronto. Super warm and stylish. I wore them with a sneaker. I could’ve gone down a size ( I ordered a L) however I’m tall ( 5’8 174lbs) so I didn’t want them to be short . I have added like 4 other colors to my cart I’m going to try at least one in a medium and go from there. These are totally elevated casual! I’ll be promoting these for awhile

Comfortable, well fitting casual pants I am 5'4" and 135lbs, I ordered the medium in navy blue The fit: the waist fits nice and snug without any gapping, they are very roomy in the hips and thighs without looking too big, the length is long on my height so they should be good for taller women as well The fabric: this spandex is a little thicker than average which should make them warmer this winter, the color is exactly what I expected and they hang nicely. I think they will be great for working out, hanging out at home or even out and about running errands! I believe they are valued correctly at $28.

Perfect cozy yet put together pants! These pants pleasantly surprised me! They’re not the typical thick sweat pant material but they look really nice and put together while still being comfortable. They are perfect for when I work wedding set ups and want to look slightly professional while still being able to be physically active. I wish they were slightly longer, but as a 5’7” girlie that is hard to come by. I’m able to wear them a little lower on my waist than preferred to get my desired length. Another thing to note is that they are a cat hair magnet. But other than that they are perfect and I’ll definitely be purchasing in other colors.

Slightly Weird Fit PROS: - soft and thick material - thick waistband CONS: - a little short for me (I'm 6ft, and these reached ~1in above the ground) - the cinch feature isnt functional - theres a weird amount of fabric at the top of the pants, right under the waistband (see picture) - small and tight waistband for an XL. I'm usually an 18 and these were tight enough that I'd have muffin top when sitting

Sequin joggers Bought these pants for a Christmas party and they were awesome! Very comfy. Inner thigh Sequins do get caught on each other a bit if your thighs rub together and mine do. But it wasn’t a huge problem. Bought a Large; for reference I weight 167lbs and I’m 5’7”.

Exactly what ordered? They are definitely exactly what I ordered and maybe it’s on me for not looking at the fabric or maybe I thought the fabric was different than what stated, but they’re very soft cloth not as sturdy as I would like since I will be using them for work are definitely more lounge around kind of joggers I am 4’11 and about 150 pounds to 160 pounds I ordered a medium

Love these joggers!! Love the buttery soft material of these joggers. I've purchased quite a few variations over the last couple of years. The full length cargo jogger in neon pink lived up to my expectations! It fits the same as all the other pairs I own, and the color is just as expected. The other pairs I have held up nicely in the wash - for the longest time I didn't dry them, but lately I've been lazy & threw them in the dryer. No shrinking, fading or pilling! Current stats: 5'7", 161 lbs, 42 bust, 34 waist, 41 hips, and I ordered the XL so they're not tight on my 24" thighs. Even when I got down to 138 lbs, 38 bust, 30 waist, 39 hips, 22 thigh they were still flattering, just a bit baggier.
